items.q: How many levels of the religion are there?
items.a: The levels of the religion are three. They are: Islām, Īmān (Faith), Iḥsān (Excellence in worship).

items.q: What are the four issues that are obligatory upon us to learn?
items.a: The first is: knowledge; the second: acting according to it; The third: advocating it, and the fourth: being patient upon any hardship endured upon its path.

items.q: What are the three principles that are obligatory upon every Muslim to know?
items.a: Knowing Allāh, His Messenger, and the religion of Islām. These three principles are what a person will be asked about in his grave.

items.q: Who is your Lord?
items.a: My Lord is Allāh.

items.q: Who is your Prophet?
items.a: My Prophet is Muḥammad, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.

items.q: What is your religion?
items.a: My religion is Islām.

items.q: Who created you?
items.a: Allāh, Exalted is He, created me and created all of creation.

items.q: Why did Allāh, Exalted is He, create us?
items.a: He created us to worship Him.

items.q: What is "worship"?
items.a: It is an all comprehensive word that includes everything that Allāh loves and is pleased with from statements and actions that are apparent and hidden.

items.q: What is Islām?
items.a: It is to submit to Allāh through affirming Tawḥīd and carry out His obedience and to free oneself from Shirk and the people of Shirk.

items.q: How many are their pillars of Islām?
items.a: Five, and they are: * To bear witness that there is no god except Allāh and that Muḥammad is the Messenger of Allāh. * Establishing the Prayer. * Paying the obligatory charity (Zakah). * Fasting in the month of Ramaḍān. * Making the pilgrimage to the House of Allāh for those able to do so.

items.q: What is the religion that Allāh will accept none but it?
items.a: The religion of Islām.

items.q: What religion is every child born upon?
items.a: Every child is born upon the religion of Islām.

items.q: Mention four different religions of disbelief.
items.a: Judaism – Christianity - As-Ṣābiʿah (Sabians) - Majoos (Fire worshipers, or Zoroastrianism)

items.q: Are the Jews and the Christians pleased with the Muslims?
items.a: No, they are not pleased with the Muslims. Allāh, Exalted is He, said: “Never will the Jews nor the Christians be pleased with you till you follow their religion. Say: Verily, the Guidance of Allāh that is the true Guidance” [Al-Baqarah: 120].

items.q: Who are the most evil of creation and who are the best of creation?
items.a: The most evil of creation are the disbelievers and the best of creation are the believers.

items.q: Do the disbelievers live a good life?
items.a: No.

items.q: Who are the People of the Book?
items.a: The Jews and the Christians.

items.q: Is the Religion of Islām complete or does it need completion?
items.a: The Religion of Islām is complete.

items.q: From where do Muslims take their religion?
items.a: From the Book of Allāh and the Sunnah of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.

items.q: What is your aqeedah (creed)?
items.a: I am Sunni Salafi.

items.q: What is the definition of Jāhiliyyah?
items.a: Everything that opposes the Book and Sunnah.

items.q: What is the first handhold of Islām to be nullified and what is the last?
items.a: The first is ruling by Allāh's Legislation, and the last of them will be abandoning prayer.

items.q: What is the definition of al-Iḥsān?
items.a: To worship Allāh as if you see Him, and if you don't see Him, know that He sees you.

items.q: In what sūrah is the statement of the Exalted: “Verily, Allāh enjoins justice and worshiping none but Allāh Alone and al-Iḥsān, and giving relatives, and forbids al-Fāḥisha, and Al-Munkar, and Al-Baghy, He admonishes you, that you may take heed”?
items.a: In Sūrat an-Naḥl.

items.q: What's the definition of Īmān (faith)?
items.a: Īmān is the statement of the tongue, belief in the heart and action of the limbs. It increases with obedience and decreases with disobedience.

items.q: How many are the pillars of Īmān?
items.a: Six: Belief in Allāh, His angels, His Books, His Messengers, the Last Day, and the decree, the good of it and the bad.

items.q: Does Īmān increase and decrease?
items.a: Yes, it increases with obedience and decreases with sins.

items.q: How many branches of Īmān are there?
items.a: There are 60 or 70 branches of Īmān.

items.q: What is the highest branch of Īmān?
items.a: The highest level of Īmān is the statement: "Lā illāha illAllāh (there is no god but Allāh)".

items.q: What is the humblest branch of Īmān?
items.a: The humblest level of Īmān is to remove harm from the road.

items.q: Mention some of the branches of Īmān?
items.a: Believing in Allāh, His angels, His Books, His Messengers, the Last Day, believing in the decree (the good and bad of it), performing the prayer, paying the Zakāh, fasting the month of Ramaḍān, performing Hajj, being just, being dutiful to the parents, keeping family ties, being truthful, patience, upholding the trusts, seeking knowledge, eating ḥalāl food, having shyness and removing harm from the road.

items.q: What is the strongest handhold of Īmān?
items.a: Loving and hating for the sake of Allāh, alliance for the sake of Allāh, and enmity for the sake of Allāh.

items.q: Who is more beloved to Allāh, the weak believer or the strong believer?
items.a: The strong believer is more beloved to Allāh.

items.q: What is the first obligation upon the slaves?
items.a: Tawḥīd of Allāh, Exalted is He.

items.q: What is the thing all the Prophets called to?
items.a: To worship Allāh alone and to avoid Ṭāghūt.

items.q: Who are awliyāʾ (allies) of Allāh?
items.a: They area: “Those who believed and feared Allāh” [Yūnus: 63].

items.q: What is the right of Allāh upon His slaves?
items.a: To worship Him alone and not to associate any partners with Him.

items.q: What is the right of the slave upon Allāh?
items.a: That He does not punish those who do not associate any partners with Him.

items.q: How many categories of the Tawḥīd of Allāh are there?
items.a: Three: Tawḥīd al-Ulūhiyyah (Divinity). Tawḥīd ar-Rubūbiyyah (Lorship). Tawḥīd al-Asmāʾ (Names) wa aṣ-Ṣifāt (Attributes).

items.q: What is Tawḥīd ar-Rubūbiyyah?
items.a: To single out Allāh in His actions which are: creating, dominion, providing, and disposition.

items.q: What is Tawḥīd al-Ulūhiyyah?
items.a: It is to single out Allāh in worship.

items.q: What is Tawḥīd al-Asmāʾ wa aṣ-Ṣifāt?
items.a: To affirm for Allāh what He has affirmed for Himself or what His Prophet has affirmed for Him, from Names and Attributes without asking how, comparing Him, or negating anything.

items.q: What is the statement of at-Tawḥīd?
items.a: Lā ilāha illa-Allāh (There is no god but Allāh).

items.q: What is the meaning of Lā ilāha illa-Allāh?
items.a: Nothing is worthy of worship except Allāh.

items.q: In what sūrah is the Statement of Allāh: “See you not how Allāh sets forth a parable? - A goodly word as a goodly tree, whose root is firmly fixed, and its branches (reach) to the sky. Giving its fruit at all times, by the Leave of its Lord and Allāh sets forth parables for mankind in order that they may remember”?
items.a: In Sūrat Ibrāheem.

items.q: What are the conditions of bearing witness that there is no god but Allāh and that Muḥammad is His Messenger?
items.a: The conditions of bearing witness that there is no god but Allāh and that Muḥammad is His Messenger are: Knowledge. Certainty. Following. Accepting. Sincerity. Truthfulness. Love. Disbelieving all that is worshiped besides Allāh.

items.q: What are the pillars of Lā ilāha illa-Allāh?
items.a: They are two pillars: Negation—There is no god (Lā ilāha). Confirmation—except Allāh (illa-Allāh).

items.q: Where is Allāh, and what is the proof?
items.a: Above the heavens, and the proof is the Statement of Allāh, Exalted is He: “The All-Merciful; rose over the Throne” [ṬāHā: 5].

items.q: Is Allāh with us?
items.a: He is with us by His Knowledge, His Encompassing, His Hearing, and His Sight.

items.q: Does anyone other than Allāh know the unwitnessed?
items.a: No one knows the unwitnessed except Allāh.

items.q: What is the most truthful statement that a poet ever made?
items.a: It is the statement of Labeed: "Verily, everything but Allāh is false".

items.q: What is the greatest good deed?
items.a: Tawḥīd of Allāh.

items.q: What is the greatest sin?
items.a: Shirk (associating partners with Allāh).

items.q: What is Shirk?
items.a: To worship anything other than Allāh.

items.q: What is the gravest of major sins?
items.a: Shirk, undutifullness to one's parents, and bearing false witness.

items.q: What are the seven destructive sins?
items.a: They are: Shirk. Taking the life of that which Allāh has prohibited except in truth. Eating the wealth of the orphans. Sorcery. Eating interest (Ribā). Fleeing from the battlefield. Slandering the chaste believing women who would never think of anything touching their chastity.

items.q: Is there any benefit in an action when Shirk is present?
items.a: No, the action does not benefit because shirk nullifies the action.

items.q: Is it permissible to slaughter for other than Allāh?
items.a: No, it is not permissible because of the ḥadīth that states: “Allāh has cursed the one who slaughters for other than Allāh”.

items.q: What's the importance of Duʿāʾ?
items.a: Duʿāʾ is worship itself.

items.q: What is the ruling on calling upon the dead and the one who is absent?
items.a: It is major Shirk.

items.q: Is it permissible for us to call upon the Prophet when making Duʿāʾ, saying: "O Messenger of Allāh..."?
items.a: No, it is not permissible to supplicate to other than Allāh.

items.q: Is it permissible to name a child, Slave of the Kaʿba (ʿAbdul Kaʿbah), Slave of the Prophet (ʿAbdul Nabī), Slave of the Messenger (ʿAbdu ar-Rasūl), and Slave of Ḥusayn (ʿAbdul Ḥusain)?
items.a: No, it is not permissible because it is from Shirk.

items.q: What is the punishment for the one who visits the soothsayer or fortuneteller?
items.a: His prayer is not accepted for forty nights, and if he believes what he is told, then he has disbelieved in what has been revealed to Muḥammad, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.

items.q: What is the ruling on sorcery?
items.a: Sorcery is Kufr.

items.q: What is An-Nushrah?
items.a: Undoing sorcery for the afflicted.

items.q: What's the ruling of An-Nushrah?
items.a: If it were by the means of sorcery then this is not permissible. If it is by way of legitimate ruqyah then this is permissible.

items.q: Mention some authentic supplications used as Ruqyah.
items.a: “Athibi l-baʾsa, rabba n-nās, wa-shfi anta sh-shāfī, lā shifāʾa illā shifāʾuka, shifāʾan lā yughādiru saqaman” (O' Allāh! the Lord of mankind! Remove this disease and heal [him or her]! You are the Healer. There is no healing except Your healing, a complete healing which leaves behind no disease). “Bismi llāhi arqīka, min kulli shayʾin yuʾthīka, min sharri kulli nafsin aw ʿaynin, aw ḥāsid; Allāhu yashfīka, bismi llāhi arqīka” (In the Name of Allāh, I recite over you (ruqyah) to cleanse you from all that troubles you, from the evil of every soul or from the evil of the eyes of an envier. May Allāh heal you, in the Name of Allāh, I recite over you).

items.q: Is it permissible to hang amulets?
items.a: No, it is not permissible because this is from Shirk.

items.q: What is the ruling on the slaughter of the disbeliever that is not from the people of the Book?
items.a: It is not permissible.

items.q: What is at-Tawakkul (reliance upon Allāh)?
items.a: It is the true reliance of the heart upon Allāh in acquiring that which is beneficial and the removal of that which is harmful, while taking the necessary legislative means.

items.q: Is it permissible to say, 'I rely upon Allāh then upon you'?
items.a: No, it is not permissible.

items.q: By what do we swear?
items.a: We swear by a Name from the Names of Allāh or an Attribute from His Attributes.

items.q: Mention some forms of oath which are impermissible.
items.a: Swearing by forefathers, swearing by honor, swearing by trust, and swearing by every vow that is by other than Allāh.

items.q: Does Allāh swear by whatever He wills?
items.a: Yes, because He is the Creator of everything.

items.q: What's the ruling on insulting Allāh, His Messenger, and His religion?
items.a: Major Kufr.

items.q: By what do the Muslims rule?
items.a: They rule by the Book and Sunnah.

items.q: What is aṭ-Ṭāghūt?
items.a: It is when a slave [of Allāh] transgresses the bounds in that which is followed, worshiped, or obeyed.

items.q: What are the major Ṭāghūt?
items.a: They are five: Iblees (may the curse of Allāh be upon him). Whoever is worshiped other than Allāh and is pleased with it. Whoever calls the people to worshiping himself. The one who claims the knowledge of the unwitnessed. The one who rules by other than what Allāh has revealed.

items.q: What are al-Lāt, ʿUzza, and Manāt?
items.a: They were idols worshipped in al-Jāhiliyyah other than Allāh.

items.q: What is the ruling on denying the jinn?
items.a: Denying the jinn is Kufr.

items.q: What is the ruling on abandoning prayer?
items.a: Kufr.

items.q: What's the ruling on insulting or cursing the Companions?
items.a: Kufr.

items.q: In what sūrah is the Statement of Allāh, Exalted is He: “And the first to embrace Islām of the Muhajirun and the Ansār and also those who followed them exactly. Allāh is well-pleased with them as they are well-pleased with Him. He has prepared for them heavens under which rivers flow, to dwell therein forever. That is the supreme success”?
items.a: Sūrat At-Tawbah.

items.q: Who are those who insult the Companions?
items.a: They are the Rāfiḍah.

items.q: Which sects are the most astray from amongst the sects that attribute themselves to Islām?
items.a: 1. Al-Bāṭiniyyah. 2. Ar-Rāfiḍah. 3. Al-Jahmiyyah. 4. Extreme Ṣūfiyyah.

items.q: What is democracy?
items.a: The ruling of people themselves by themselves.

items.q: What is the ruling on democracy?
items.a: Major Shirk.

items.q: What is the ruling of partisanship?
items.a: Ḥarām.

items.q: Does Allāh have Names and Attributes?
items.a: Yes, He has Names and Attributes that are befitting for His Majesty.

items.q: Write Surah al-Ikhlās.
items.a: Bismillāhi ar-Raḥmāni ar-Raḥīm: “Say He is Allāh, One. Allāhu aṣ-Ṣamad. He begets not, nor was He begotten; and there is none co-equal or comparable unto Him” [Al-Ikhlās: 1-4].

items.q: How many are the Names of Allāh?
items.a: They are plenty, and no one knows the exact number except Him. From these Names are 99 Names that whoever preserves them enters al-Jannah.

items.q: Mention ten Names of Allāh.
items.a: Ar-Raḥmān (The All-Merciful). Ar-Raḥīm (The Especially Merciful). Al-Malik (The King). Al-Quddūs (The Most-Holy). As-Salām. Al-Muʿmin. Al-Muhaymin. Al-ʿAzeez (The Mighty). Al-Jabbār. Al-Mutakabbir.

items.q: What are the Names of Allāh called?
items.a: They are called Al-Asmāʾ Al-Ḥusnā (The Most Excellent Names), as Allāh, Exalted is He said: “And to Allāh belong the Most Excellent Names, so call on Him by them, and leave the company of those who belie or deny His Names. They will be requited for what they used to do” [Al-Aʿraf: 180].

items.q: Which are more in number: The Names of Allāh or His Attributes?
items.a: Allāh's Attributes are more in number.

items.q: Mention ten of Allāh's Attributes.
items.a: Mercy. Knowledge. Ability. Might. Sublimeness. Dominance. Creation. Hearing. Sight. Will.

items.q: What are the Attributes of Allāh called?
items.a: They are called the Highest Attributes. Allāh, Exalted is He, said: “and He has the Highest Attributes” [Ar-Rūm: 27]. “and for Allāh are the Highest Attributes” [An-Naḥl: 60).

items.q: Who are those that said Allāh's Hand is shackled and what did Allāh refute them with?
items.a: They are the Jews and Allāh refuted them with His statement: “The Jews say: ‘Allāh's Hand is shackled’. Be their hands shackled and be they accursed for what they uttered. Nay, both His Hands are outstretched. He spends as He wills” [Al-Maʿidah: 64].

items.q: How many is the mercy Allāh created?
items.a: A hundred mercies.

items.q: How many of it did He place on earth?
items.a: One mercy.

items.q: Which of the two precedes the other: the Mercy of Allāh or His Wrath?
items.a: Allāh's Mercy precedes His Wrath.

items.q: By what means do we entreat Allāh?
items.a: By His Names and His Attributes, righteous actions, and the supplication of a righteous man.

items.q: What is the meaning of: Muḥammad is the Messenger of Allāh?
items.a: It means: He is the Messenger of Allāh to all people, both jinn and mankind.

items.q: Whom must we love more than all of people?
items.a: Muḥammad, the Messenger of Allāh, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.

items.q: What are the conditions for the acceptance of righteous deeds?
items.a: 1. Sincerity to Allāh, Exalted is He. 2. Following the Messenger of Allāh, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.

items.q: What do we call an action that is not upon the guidance of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: It is called bidʿah (innovation).

items.q: In which sūrah is this Statement of Allāh, Exalted is He: “Or have they partners with Allāh, who have legislated for them from religion what Allāh has not allowed”?
items.a: Sūrat ash-Shūra.

items.q: Are bidaʿ evil and heedlessness?
items.a: Yes.

items.q: Are bidaʿ accepted or rejected?
items.a: They are rejected and Allāh does not accept them.

items.q: Is there such thing as a good bidʾa in the religion?
items.a: There is no good bidʾa in this religion, rather all bidaʿ are heedlessness.

items.q: In which sūrah is this Statement of Allāh, Exalted is He: “Indeed in the Messenger of Allāh you have a good example to follow for those who hope for Allāh and the Last Day and remember Allāh much”?
items.a: Sūrat Al-Aḥzab.

items.q: What is the belief in the angels, the Jannah, the Fire, the Basin, the Path, and in all of the things that we don't see with our eyes called?
items.a: It is called the belief in the unwitnessed.

items.q: What are the angels created from?
items.a: From light.

items.q: Who is the best of the angels?
items.a: Jibreel, ʿalayhi as-sallām.

items.q: Who is the Holy Spirit?
items.a: Jibreel, ʿalayhi as-sallām.

items.q: Who is the one who split open the Prophet's chest and washed it with zamzam water?
items.a: Jibreel, ʿalayhi as-sallām.

items.q: Who is the Trustworthy Spirit?
items.a: Jibreel, ʿalayhi as-sallām.

items.q: Who is the one who descended with revelation from Allāh, Exalted is He, to the Prophets?
items.a: Jibreel, ʿalayhi as-sallām.

items.q: Do the angels have wings?
items.a: Yes.

items.q: How many wings does Jibreel, ʿalayhi as-sallām, have?
items.a: He has 600 wings.

items.q: How many times did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, see Jibreel in his natural form?
items.a: Twice.

items.q: To whom do the angels lower their wings?
items.a: Knowledge seekers.

items.q: Who is the angel responsible for rain and plants?
items.a: Mikāʿeel, ʿalayhi as-sallām.

items.q: Who is the angel responsible for blowing the trumpet?
items.a: Isrāfeel, ʿalayhi as-sallām.

items.q: Who is 'the Keeper of the Fire'?
items.a: Mālik, ʿalayhi as-sallām.

items.q: Who are the two angels responsible for the questioning in the grave?
items.a: Munkar and Nakeer, peace be upon them both.

items.q: How many carriers of the Throne will there be on the Day of Judgment?
items.a: Eight: [4] Bearers of the Throne [5] Keepers of Paradise [6] Keepers of Hell [7] Angels of the Grave

items.q: Mention some actions of the angels.
items.a: 1- Angels who attend the sittings of thkir (remembrance of Allāh). 2- Deeds recorders. 3- Those that preserve the bodies. 4- The carriers of the Throne. 5- The Keeper of al-Jannah. 6- The Keeper of the Hellfire. 7- The angels of the grave.

items.q: Name the angels of torment mentioned in sūrat Al-ʿAlaq.
items.a: Az-Zabāniyah.

items.q: Who is the angel responsible for taking souls?
items.a: The Angel of Death.

items.q: Has it been confirmed that the name of the Angel of Death is ʿAzrāʿeel?
items.a: No, it has not been confirmed.

items.q: Do the angels eat and drink?
items.a: No. Allāh, Exalted is He, said: “And when he saw their hands reach not for the meal he felt mistrust of them” [Hood: 70].

items.q: What is the name of the house in the seventh heaven and how many angels enter it every day?
items.a: Al-Bayt Al-Maʾmoor (The Frequented House), and 70,000 angels enter it everyday and, never return.

items.q: Is it found amongst the angels those who disobey Allāh?
items.a: No. Allāh, Exalted is He, said: “And they do not disobey Allāh what he has commanded them and they do as they have been commanded” [At-Taḥreem: 6].

items.q: Where do the angels record the deeds of the children of Ādam?
items.a: In the Books of Deeds.

items.q: Are the numbers of the angels plenty?
items.a: Yes, and no one knows their numbers except Allāh; “And none knows the Lord's Army except Him” [Al-Muddath-thir: 31]. Also in a ḥadīth it is mentioned that: "there is not a space in the heavens the width of four fingers except that there is an angel bowing or prostrating".

items.q: Do the angels have evil within?
items.a: No.

items.q: Which of the two were created first, jinn or man?
items.a: Jinn were created first.

items.q: Do we see the jinn and the Shayāṭeen in their natural form?
items.a: No.

items.q: Do we fear the jinn and the devils?
items.a: No, rather we fear Allāh alone.

items.q: Are all the jinn disbelievers?
items.a: No, from them are believers and from them are disbelievers.

items.q: What is the final destination of the believing and the disbelieving jinn?
items.a: The believing jinn enter al-Jannah and the disbeliever from amongst them enters Fire.

items.q: What animals could see the jinn?
items.a: Donkeys and Dogs.

items.q: Do the jinn eat and drink?
items.a: Yes.

items.q: Where do the Jinn live?
items.a: They live in the dilapidated ruins, deserts, impure places (i.e. bathrooms), latrines, garbage areas, grave yards and the market place.

items.q: What does the Shayṭān say to his followers if a man enters his home and says 'Bismillāh' and when he eats says 'Bismillāh'?
items.a: He tells them, “You have no resting place nor any dinner”.

items.q: Do the jinn have offspring?
items.a: Yes.

items.q: Do the Jinn die?
items.a: Yes.

items.q: Do devils reside in human homes, and how could they be expelled?
items.a: Yes, and they are expelled by saying Bismillāh, with the remembrance of Allāh, and the recitation of the Qurʾān, particularly sūrat Al-Baqarah.

items.q: What is it that the devils flee from and can't bear its sound?
items.a: The athān

items.q: When are the rebellious devils chained?
items.a: In Ramaḍān.

items.q: Does Shayṭān have horns?
items.a: Yes, and the sun rises and sets between the two horns of the Shayṭān.

items.q: What is the place of Shayṭān in the children of Ādam?
items.a: He flows through their veins.

items.q: Who are those that the Shayṭān has no power over?
items.a: They are the sincere slaves of Allāh.

items.q: Do humans have a Shayṭān who is inseparable from each of them?
items.a: Yes, and he is al-qareen (companion).

items.q: Who is the one whose qareen accepted Islām?
items.a: The Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.

items.q: Do the jinn change form?
items.a: Yes, they change to many different forms: humans and animals, specifically black animals.

items.q: Does Shayṭān have the capability to take the form of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: No.

items.q: Are the jinn responsible for their actions?
items.a: Yes.

items.q: Do the Jinn have from amongst them messengers?
items.a: No, rather they have warners.

items.q: Is the Shayṭān our enemy?
items.a: Yes.

items.q: What is the biggest objective that the Shayṭān strives to accomplish?
items.a: His objective is for man to be thrown into the Fire.

items.q: Mention some methods that the Shayṭān uses to accomplish this objective.
items.a: Causing man to fall into shirk and kufr. Causing man to sin. Causing him to fall into bidʿa. Preventing man from obedience. Planting enmity and hatred between the slaves. Evil whispering in order to ruin obedience. Partaking with them in their meals and homes if they don't say Bismillāh (In the Name of Allāh). Using harmful animals the likes of rats to burn down the homes of humans. Causing humans to stray and stumble at the time death. The Shayṭān possessing the bodies of humans.

items.q: Mention some measures that can be taken as a means of protection from Shayṭān.
items.a: Seeking refuge with Allāh. Sincerity to Allāh. Holding fast to the Book and Sunnah, and not innovating in the Religion of Allāh. Seeking refuge in Allāh when entering the bathroom, when angry, during sexual intercourse, when hearing the braying of donkeys, and when reciting Qurʾān. Seeking refuge for the children and family members. Increasing in the remembrance of Allāh. Holding fast to the general population of the Muslims (al-Jamāʿah). Repenting and seeking forgiveness.

items.q: What of the Qurʾān is best for seeking refuge?
items.a: "Qul aʿoothu bi Rabbi al-Falaq” (Say I seek refuge in The Lord of the Day break), and, "Qul aʿoothu bi Rabbi an-Nās” (Say I seek refuge in Allāh the Lord of Mankind).

items.q: Do the jinn know the unwitnessed?
items.a: No.

items.q: Where does Shayṭān place his throne?
items.a: Above the water.

items.q: Mention four of the revealed Books.
items.a: Al-Qurʾān, at-Tawrāh (Torah), al-Injeel (Gospel), az-Zaboor (Psalms).
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Books

items.q: Mention upon which Prophet each book was revealed.
items.a: Al-Qurʾān was revealed to Muḥammad; at-Tawrāh was revealed to Moosā; al-Injeel was reveal to ʿĪsā; az-Zaboor was revealed to Dāwood, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhim wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Books

items.q: Which of the revealed books Allāh wrote with His Hand?
items.a: At-Tawrāh.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Books

items.q: Is it permissible for us to read at-Tawrāh and al-Injeel?
items.a: No, it is not permissible because it has been falsified and it has been abrogated by Al-Qurʾān.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Books

items.q: What is the best of these Books?
items.a: Al-Qurʾān.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Books

items.q: What do we believe concerning the Qurʾān?
items.a: It is the Speech of Allāh revealed and it is not created.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Books

items.q: How many divisions are the signs of Allāh divided into?
items.a: The signs of Allāh are of two kinds: creation signs, and legislative signs.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Books

items.q: Mention some of the legislative signs of Allāh.
items.a: The āyāt of the Qurʾān that we read.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Books

items.q: Mention some of the creation signs of Allāh:
items.a: The sun, the moon, the stars, the mountains, the trees, and the animals.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Books

items.q: In which sūrah is the Statement of Allāh, Exalted is He: “Do they not look at the camels, how they are created? And the sky how it is raised? And at the mountains how they are rooted and fixed firm? And at the earth how it is spread out”?
items.a: Sūrat al-Ghāshiyah, (88:17-20).
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Books

items.q: Is the Qurʾān in Arabic or non-Arabic?
items.a: The Qurʾān is in Arabic.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Books

items.q: Who is the father of mankind?
items.a: Ādam, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: In which sūrah is the Statement of Allāh, Exalted is He: “And (remember) when your Lord brought forth from the Children of Ādam, from their loins, their seed (or from Ādam's loin his offspring) and made them testify as to themselves (saying) "Am I not your Lord?" They said: "Yes! We testify," lest you should say on the Day of Resurrection: "Verily, we have been unaware of this"?
items.a: Sūrat Al-Aʿraf.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: From what was Ādam created?
items.a: From clay.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: From what was Ḥawwāʾ created?
items.a: From the rib of Ādam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What is the sin Ādam and Ḥawwāʾ commited and disobeyed their Lord with?
items.a: They ate from the tree which they were prohibited from by Allāh, Exalted is He.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What did Ādam, ʿalayhi as-sallām, and Ḥawwāʾ do after their sin?
items.a: They repented to Allāh and Allāh accepted their repentance.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Why did Allāh become angry with Iblees, curse him, and remove him from al-Jannah?
items.a: Because he disobeyed Allāh and refused to prostrate to Ādam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who is the first of the prophets?
items.a: Ādam, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who is the last of the prophets?
items.a: Muḥammad, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What is the meaning of the seal of the Prophets?
items.a: The last of them.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who are the Messengers that are referred to as, “The Possessors of Determination”?
items.a: Nūḥ, Ibrāheem, Moosā, ʿĪsā, and Muḥammad, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhim wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who is the best of the Messengers?
items.a: Muḥammad, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Were the Prophets shepherds?
items.a: Yes, and the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, tended sheep for a small fee for the people of Makkah.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Are the Prophets inherited from?
items.a: No, whatever they left is charity.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Do the Prophets sleep?
items.a: Their eyes sleep but not their hearts.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Does the earth consume the bodies of the Prophets?
items.a: No.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Are there Prophets from amongst women?
items.a: No, Prophethood is specific for men.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Did Allāh mention to us the stories of all the Messengers?
items.a: No.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who are the Prophets who have Arabic names?
items.a: Hūd, Sāliḥ, Shuʿayb, and Muḥammad, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhim wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who is it that Allāh commanded the angels to prostrate to?
items.a: Ādam, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Did Iblees prostrate to Ādam, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam ?
items.a: No, he did not prostrate, rather he was arrogant and was from amongst the disbelievers.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Is Iblees from the angels or from the jinn?
items.a: He is from the jinn.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who is the first Messenger sent to the people of the earth?
items.a: Nūḥ, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: How long did Nūḥ remain amongst his people?
items.a: A thousand years except fifty.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: How did Allāh save Nūḥ and his followers?
items.a: By the ark.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: By what did Allāh destroy the people of Nūḥ?
items.a: By the flood.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Mention a prophet whose son was a disbeliever.
items.a: Nūḥ, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Mention a prophet whose father was a disbeliever.
items.a: Ibrāheem, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Mention two prophets whose wives were disbelievers.
items.a: Nūḥ and Lūṭ, peace and blessings be upon them both.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who is the Father of the Prophets?
items.a: Ibrāheem, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who is the first of the creation on the Day of Judgment to be clothed?
items.a: Ibrāheem, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who was the first to host a guest?
items.a: Ibrāheem, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who said: “My Lord bestow religious knowledge upon me and join me with the righteous and grant me a honorable mention in later generations” [Ash-Shuʿarāʿ: 83-84]?
items.a: Ibrāheem, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: When was Ibrāheem, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, circumcised?
items.a: When he was 80 years old.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who is the mother of Ismāʿeel, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: Hājar, ʿalayhā as-sallām.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What spring was made to gush forth for the mother of Ismāʿeel?
items.a: Zamzam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What happened when Ibrāheem was thrown into the fire?
items.a: It became cold and peaceful for him.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Which of Ibrāheem's sons did Allāh command to be slaughtered?
items.a: Ismāʿeel, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: With what did Allāh ransom Ismāʿeel?
items.a: He ransomed him with a great sacrifice: a ram.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who was the father of Ibrāheem?
items.a: Āzar, and he is from the people of the Fire.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who debated with Ibrāheem about his Lord?
items.a: It was an-Numrood.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who are al-Muʾtafikah or al-Muʾtafikāt (Over thrown cities)?
items.a: They are the people of Lūṭ.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Was Ibrāheem a Jew or a Christian?
items.a: No, rather a Ḥanīf Muslim. He was neither a Jew nor a Christian. There was no Judaism or Christianity except after him.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who raised the foundation of al-Bayt al-Ḥarām?
items.a: Ibrāheem and his son Ismāʿeel, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhimā wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: In which sūrah is the Statement of Allāh, Exlated is He: “Verily, the first House (of worship) appointed for mankind was that at Bakkah (Makkah), full of blessing, and a guidance for Al-ʿAlamīn (the mankind and jinns) }?
items.a: Sūrat ʾImrān.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Which city did Ibrāheem supplicate for?
items.a: Makkah.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Which place did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, supplicate for as Ibrāheem supplicated for Makkah?
items.a: Al-Madeenah.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What is the punishment for the one who intends harm for the people of Madeenah?
items.a: Allāh will cause him to vanish as salt vanishes in water.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What are the virtues for dying in Madeenah?
items.a: Whoever dies in Madeenah will gain the intercession of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who is Isrāʾeel?
items.a: Yaʿqoob, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who is the greatest Prophet from amongst the people of Isrāʾeel?
items.a: Moosā, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Which prophet did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, see praying standing in his grave?
items.a: Moosā, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: To whom did Allāh send Moosā, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: Firʿawn and his people.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What did Allāh command Moosā’s mother?
items.a: He commanded her to nurse her son Moosā and if she feared for him, to place him in a basket and cast him in the river.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who did Allāh speak to?
items.a: Moosā, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What are the nine signs that Allāh gave Moosā?
items.a: 1- The staff. 2-  Drought. 3-  Locust. 4-  Lice. 5-  Frogs. 6-  Blood. 7-  The flood. 8-  The hand. 9-  The sea.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who is the one that said, “I am your Lord the Most High”, and how did Allāh destroy him?
items.a: Firʿawn (may the curse of Allāh be upon him), and Allāh destroyed him by drowning.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who was the Minister of Firʿawn?
items.a: Hāmān (may the curse of Allāh be upon him).
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who advised his people to follow Moosā and in which sūrah was his story mentioned?
items.a: A believer from the people of Firʿawn, and his story was mentioned in sūrat Ghāfir.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What is the name of the wife of Firʿawn?
items.a: Āsiyah bint Muzāḥim, and she is from the people of al-Jannah.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who is the man from among the people of Moosā who transgressed and was proud of his money, whereby Allāh caused him to sink into the ground?
items.a: Qāroon, and he is from the people of the Fire.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: How did Allāh destroy Qāroon?
items.a: By sinking him into the ground.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who did Moosā go to seek knowledge from?
items.a: The Prophet of Allāh: Khadhir, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What is the name of the youth who was Moosā's companion and later became a prophet?
items.a: Yooshaʿ bnu Nūn.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Was the sun ever stopped for anyone?
items.a: Yes, for the Messenger of Allāh Yooshaʿ bnu Nūn, ʿalayhi as-sallām.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who was the Messenger sent to the people of Thamood?
items.a: Sāliḥ, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What was the sign that Allāh sent to the people of Thamood?
items.a: A female camel.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: By what did Allāh destroy the people of Thamood?
items.a: By the Shout.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Which Messenger did Allāh send to the people of ʿĀd?
items.a: Hūd, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Where did the people of Hūd, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, reside?
items.a: In the curved sand hills of Yemen.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: How did Allāh destroy the people of ʿĀd?
items.a: With strong cold winds.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Which Prophet did Allāh allow to utilize the Jinn, mankind, birds and the wind?
items.a: Sulaymān, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Which Prophet spoke to the hoopoe bird and heard the speech of an ant?
items.a: Sulaymān, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What did Sulaymān, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, write in his letter to the Queen of Yemen?
items.a: He wrote: "In the Name of the All-Merciful, the Merciful, be you not be exalted against me but come to me as Muslims".
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Which prophet did not eat except from the fruits of his labor?
items.a: Dāwood, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Which prophet killed Goliath (Jāloot) the disbeliever?
items.a: Dāwood, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Which prophet was tested with illness and was patient and thankful?
items.a: The prophet of Allāh Ayyoob, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Which prophet was a carpenter?
items.a: Zakariyyā, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Which prophet did Allāh command to hold fast to the Book with strength?
items.a: Yahyā bnu Zakariyyā, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who is the companion Thū an-Nūn (the Companion of the Whale)?
items.a: The Prophet of Allāh Yoonus, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What is the meaning of 'An-Noon'?
items.a: It is a whale.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Why was the Prophet of Allāh Yoonus called 'the Companion of the Whale'?
items.a: Because the whale swallowed him when he was thrown into the ocean.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What supplication did Yunus, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, make while inside the whale?
items.a: “There's no god but You. Holy You are; verily I was from the wrong-doers” [Al-Anbiyāʾ: 87].
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Which plant did Allāh cause to grow upon him?
items.a: The pumpkin.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who are referred to as Aṣ'hābul Aykah (The Dwellers in the Wood)?
items.a: The people of Shuʿayb, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Which prophet was sent to the people of Madyan?
items.a: Shuʿayb, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who is the honored, son of the honored, son of the honored, son of the honored?
items.a: Yūsuf bnu Yaʿqoob bnu Ishāq bnu Ibrāheem, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhim wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: To which of the prophets did Allāh gave half of beauty?
items.a: Yūsuf, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Which one is more beautiful: Ādam or Yūsuf, peace and blessings be upon them both?
items.a: Ādam, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, because the people of Jannah will resemble him.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Mention three Prophets whom Allāh gave prophet-hood at a young age.
items.a: Yūsuf, ʿĪsā, and Yahyā, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhim wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: How long did the people of the cave remain asleep?
items.a: Three hundred and nine years.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who is it that the Shayṭān did not touch during childbirth?
items.a: ʿĪsā and his mother Maryam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Which prophet did Allāh raise up to Him and is still alive?
items.a: ʿĪsā, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What do the Christians believe concerning ʿĪsā, the son of Maryam, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: Some of them say that he is Allāh, some say he is the son of Allāh, and some of them say he is one of three.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What is the correct creed concerning ʿĪsā the son of Maryam?
items.a: He is the slave of Allāh, His Messenger and His word which He bestowed upon Maryam, and a spirit created by Him.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who are al-Ḥawāriyyoon?
items.a: They are the companions of ʿĪsā, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What is the time span between ʿĪsā and Muḥammad, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhimā wa-sallam?
items.a: Six hundred years.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What sign was ʿĪsā, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, given?
items.a: He used to heal the blind and the leper and bring the dead to life by Allāh's permission.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Name the only female mentioned in the Qurʾān?
items.a: Maryam, ʿalayhā as-sallām.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What are the three dwelling places?
items.a: 1- Dār ad-Dunyā (Life of this world). 2- Dār al-Barzakh (Life of the grave). 3- Dar al-Ākhirah (Life of the Hereafter).
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Mention three names for the grave.
items.a: Al-qabr (grave), al-bayt (house), al-Jadath (resurrection site).
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What is the first abode to the Hereafter?
items.a: The grave.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Is there bliss and torment in the grave?
items.a: Yes, there is bliss and torment in the grave for those who deserve it.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: In which sūrah is the Statement of Allāh: “The Fire, they are exposed to it, morning and afternoon. And on the Day when the Hour will be established (it will be said to the angels): ‘Cause Firʿaun’s (Pharaoh) people to enter the severest torment!’"?
items.a: Sūrat Ghāfir.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What things follow the deceased to the grave and what remains?
items.a: His family, money, and deeds follow him. His family and money return, and his deeds remain.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Mention three things that follow the deceased after his death.
items.a: Beneficial knowledge, a continuous charity, a righteous son who supplicates for them.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What are the questions people get asked in their graves?
items.a: Who is your Lord? What is your Religion? Who is your Messenger?
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What is the response of the Muslim?
items.a: My Lord is Allāh. My religion is Islām. My messenger is Muḥammad, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What is the response of the disbeliever or the hypocrite?
items.a: Uh....uh...I don't know.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Mention some the signs of the Hour.
items.a: 1- Knowledge will be taken away. 2- Increase of ignorance. 3- Increase of fornication. 4- Drinking of alcohol. 5- Decrease of men in number and increase of females in number. 6- Increase in killing. 7- Increase of trials and tribulations. 8- An increase of sudden death. 9- Discredit of the trustworthy. 10- The ignorant will speak in the general affairs of the people. 11- Beautification of the masjids.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What are the ten major signs of the Hour?
items.a: 1- The Dajjāl. 2- The descending of ʿĪsā bnu Maryam. 3- The appearance of Yaʾjooj and Maʾjooj. 4-  The Smoke. 5- The Beast. 6- The rising of the sun from the west. 7-  The sinking of the earth in the east. 8-  The sinking of earth in the west. -9. The sinking of the earth in the Arabian Peninsula. 10- Fire from the depths of Aden and people will be herded to the gathering place.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Mention some of the names of the Day of Judgment:
items.a: 1- Al-Hāqqah (The Inevitable). 2- Al-Qāriʿah (The Striking Hour). 3- Al-Ghāshiyah (The Overwhelming). 4- As-Sākhah (The Deafening Noise). 5- At-Tāmatu l-Kubrā (The Greatest Catastrophe). 6- Al-Wāqi'ah (The Event).
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: On which day will the Hour be established?
items.a: On a Friday.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: After the appearance of which sign will the door of repentance be closed?
items.a: The rising of the sun from the west.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Where does the sun set?
items.a: The sun goes and prostates under the Throne of Allāh and seeks permission to return to where it rose from.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What happens to the sun and the moon on the Day of Resurrection?
items.a: They will be rolled up into a ball and thrown into the Fire.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What is the greatest trial that will occur before the Day of Resurrection?
items.a: The trial of the Dajjāl.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What is written between the eyes of the Dajjāl?
items.a: كافر (Disbeliever—Kāfir).
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What are the cities that the Dajjāl cannot enter?
items.a: Makkah and al-Madeenah.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What is the speed of the Dajjāl?
items.a: The like of abundant rain blown by heavy wind.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who is the one that will kill the Dajjal?
items.a: ʿĪsā the son of Maryam, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Are Ya'jooj and Ma'jooj from the offspring of Ādam?
items.a: Yes.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who is the one who built the barrier upon Yaʾjooj and Maʾjooj?
items.a: It is Thul Qarnayn.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Are Ya'jooj and Ma'jooj plenty in number?
items.a: Yes, they will fill the earth.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: How will they be destroyed?
items.a: Allāh will send upon them a particular worm called Naghath (Myiasis) on their necks which will cause them to die.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Which is the land of the Gathering?
items.a: The land of ash-Shām.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: When ʿĪsā descends at the end of time, will he judge by the Legislation of Muḥammad or by the Bible?
items.a: By the Legislation of Muḥammad, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Is Iblees still alive, or has he died?
items.a: He will remain alive until Allāh permits his demise.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What is the trumpet?
items.a: It's a horn that is blown.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: How many times will the trumpet be blown?
items.a: Twice. The first blowing will cause fear and death and the second will be the resurrection.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What is the ruling on one who denies the resurrection?
items.a: He is a disbeliever.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Upon whom will the earth split open first?
items.a: The Prophet Muḥammad, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: How will people be gathered on the Day of Resurrection?
items.a: Barefoot, naked and ghurlan.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What is the meaning of ghurlan?
items.a: Uncircumcised.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: How will the Prophet recognize his ummah at the place of gathering?
items.a: They will come to him with radiant faces, hands and feet, from the traces of wuḍooʾ.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: From what will the people be formed again after they have become dust?
items.a: From the tail bone.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: How long will the Day of Resurrection last?
items.a: It will be 50,000 years.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: How close will the sun come to people on the Day of Resurrection?
items.a: It will approach until it is a mile away from people.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What will the disbeliever wish for on the Day of Resurrection?
items.a: That they were dust.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What is the first thing which will bear witness against people on the Day of Resurrection?
items.a: The palm and the thigh.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What is the Major Intercession?
items.a: It is the intercession for all the people at the gathering at the beginning of the reckoning.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who will carry out the Major Intercession?
items.a: The Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who is more deserving of the intercession of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: That who says Lā ilāhā illā Allāh (there is no god but Allāh) sincerely from their heart.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: By what will the believer take his book on the Day of Resurrection?
items.a: By his right hand.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: By what will the disbeliever take his Book on the Day of Resurrection?
items.a: By his left hand, behind his back.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: In which sūrah is the Statement of Allāh, Exalted is He:  “How will it be when We bring forth each nation a witness and We bring you as a witness against these people”?
items.a: Sūrat an-Nisāʾ, āyah 41.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Mention some of the descriptions of the Hawḍ of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.a: Its length is a month, and so is its width. Its water is whiter than milk and sweeter than honey, its drinking vessels are as numerous as the starts in the sky, and whoever drinks from it will never be thirsty again.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: By what will the deeds be weighed on the Day of Resurrection?
items.a: By the Scale.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What is the Path (as-Ṣirāt)?
items.a: It is a bridge laid over Jahannam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: How will the speed of people be while they cross the Path?
items.a: It will be according to their deeds. Some will cross as fast as the blinking of an eye, the speed of lightning, like the wind, fast as a bird, as a racing horse and rides, and others will crawl.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Where will the believers stand after crossing the Path?
items.a: They will stand at a bridge between al-Jannah and the Fire before entering al-Jannah, so that everyone gets their due from one another.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: In which sūrah is the Statement of Allāh, Exalted is He: “There is none of you except that he will pass over it. This is, with your Lord, a decree that must be fulfilled”?
items.a: Sūrat Maryam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What is the reward for the believer and what is the compensation for the disbeliever on the Day of Resurrection?
items.a: The reward for the believer is al-Jannah and the compensation for the disbeliever is the Fire.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Do al-Jannah and Hellfire exist now?
items.a: Yes.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Where is al-Jannah and the Fire?
items.a: Al-Jannah is in the highest of highs in the sky, and the Fire is in the lowest of lows in the lowest earth.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: How many doors does al-Jannah and Hellfire have?
items.a: The doors of al-Jannah are eight and the doors of the Fire are seven.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Where do the rivers of al-Jannah gush forth from?
items.a: From al-Firdaws.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Mention some of the rivers of al-Jannah that are also from the rivers of this world.
items.a: Sayḥān, Jayḥān, the Nile, and Eupharates.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What are the types of rivers found in al-Jannah?
items.a: Rivers from pure water, rivers from pure honey, rivers of wine, and rivers of milk.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What is al-Kawthar?
items.a: It is a river in al-Jannah for the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Where does al-Kawthar flow?
items.a: Into the Ḥawḍ of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Will there be death in al-Jannah?
items.a: No, there is no death in al-Jannah.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who are the majority of the people of al-Jannah?
items.a: The weak and the poor.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who are the majority of the people of the Fire?
items.a: Women.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who are the fuel of the Fire?
items.a: People and stones.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Will disbelievers who used to do good deeds enter al-Jannah?
items.a: No; for disbelievers do not enter al-Jannah under any circumstances.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Will the people of tawḥīd (those who single out Allāh in worship) remain in the Fire forever?
items.a: No.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Do the Jews believe in the Fire?
items.a: They say they will enter for a number of days and then the Muslims will take their places.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Which of the nations will be the first to enter al-Jannah?
items.a: The ummah of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who will request the door of al-Jannah to open and be the first to enter it?
items.a: The Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Mention some of the names of al-Jannah:
items.a: Dār As-Salām (Home of Peace), Al-Ḥusnā (Goodness), Dār Al-Muqāmah (The Abode of Settlement), Dār Al-Qarār (The Abode of Stability).
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What is the highest rank of al-Jannah?
items.a: Al-Firdaws, and its roof is the Throne of ar-Raḥmān (the All-Merciful).
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What is the highest position in al-Firdaws?
items.a: Al-Waseelah, and it is for our Prophet Muḥammad only, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Will there be grudge and envy in al-Jannah?
items.a: No, there will be neither grudge nor envy in al-Jannah.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: In how many rows will the people of al-Jannah be?
items.a: A hundred and twenty rows.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: How many of those rows will be for this ummah?
items.a: eighty rows.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: By how many years will the poor precede the wealthy in entering Jannah?
items.a: By 500 years (half a day).
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: How long is a day for Allāh?
items.a: A thousand years compared to one day of ours.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What is the tree in al-Jannah in whose shade a rider may travel for one hundred years without reaching its end?
items.a: The tree called Ṭoobā.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What will be the form of the people of al-Jannah?
items.a: They will be in the likeness of their father Ādam, 60 arm spans in height.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What will be the first meal of the people of al-Jannah?
items.a: The appendix of the whale's liver.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What is the difference between as-Sundus and al-Istabraq?
items.a: As-Sundus is fine (thin) silk brocade, and al-Istabraq is thick silk brocade.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: How many will enter al-Jannah without reckoning or torment?
items.a: Seventy thousand, and with every thousand, another seventy thousand, and in another narration: with every one, seventy thousand.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What are their descriptions?
items.a: 1- They are those who do not request ruqyah. 2- They do not brand themselves for medicinal purposes. 3- They do not implement omens and superstitions. 4- They rely on Allāh.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who is the companion to whom the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, said: "You are one of them"?
items.a: ʿUkāshah bnu Miḥṣan, may Allāh be pleased with him.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Will the believers see their Lord on the Day of Resurrection?
items.a: Yes, in the ʿAraṣāt (Standing Grounds) on the Day of Resurrection, and in al-Jannah.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: In which sūrah is the Statement of Allāh, Exalted is He: “Some faces that Day will be shining and radiant * Looking at their Lord”?
items.a: Sūrat al-Qiyāmah.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What's referred to as "even more" in the āyah: “For those who have done good is the best reward and even more”?
items.a: Looking at the Glorious Face of Allāh.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who are those who will be in the lowest depth of the Fire?
items.a: The hypocrites.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: How many reins will be attached to Jahannam?
items.a: Seventy thousand reins, each rein will be pulled by seventy thousand angels.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What is the tree that grows in the middle of the Fire, from which the people of the Fire will eat?
items.a: The tree of az-Zaqqoom.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Is there cold in the Fire?
items.a: Yes, as extreme cold of this life is from the excessive bitter cold of the Jahannam, and the excessive heat of this world is from the blazing heat of Jahannam.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Mention some of the names of Jahannam.
items.a: Al-Ḥuṭamah (The Crusher), Laẓā (The Blaze), Saqar (The Scorching Fire), al-Hāwiyah (The Pit), as-Saʿīr (The Inferno).
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Will al-Jannah and the Fire come to an end and cease to exist?
items.a: No, rather both of their dwellers will reside therein forever.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Will al-Jannah and the Fire become completely filled?
items.a: Yes.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: How large are the skin and molar tooth of a disbeliever in the Fire?
items.a: The breadth of his skin will span a three-day journey, and his molar tooth will be as large as Mount Uḥud.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: Who are the people of al-Aʿrāf?
items.a: They are people whose good and bad deeds will be equal.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What will be their final destination?
items.a: They will be stopped between al-Jannah and the Fire, and then their final destination will be al-Jannah.
items.section: Chapter: The Belief in the Messengers

items.q: What was the first thing that the pen wrote after Allāh, Exalted is He, created it?
items.a: It wrote everything which will exist until the Day of Resurrection.
items.section: Chapter: Faith in al-Qadar (The Devine Decree)

items.q: Where did Allāh, Exalted is He, write the creation's decree?
items.a: In al-Lawḥ al-Maḥfūẓ (the Preserved Tablet).
items.section: Chapter: Faith in al-Qadar (The Devine Decree)

items.q: Where is al-Lawḥ al-Maḥfūẓ?
items.a: It is with Allāh, Exalted is He, above the Throne.
items.section: Chapter: Faith in al-Qadar (The Devine Decree)

items.q: How many levels of al-Qadar (The Devine Decree) are there?
items.a: Four: 1- Knowledge. 2- Writing. 3- Will. 4- Creating.
items.section: Chapter: Faith in al-Qadar (The Devine Decree)

items.q: Do the afflictions occur with the Permission of Allāh?
items.a: Yes.
items.section: Chapter: Faith in al-Qadar (The Devine Decree)

items.q: What does one say when afflicted with an affliction?
items.a: He says: “Innā lillāh wa innā ilayhi rājiʿūn, Allāhumma ʾjurnī fī muṣībatī, wa akhlif lī khayran minhā” (Verily, to Allāh we belong and to Him we will return. O' Allāh reward me in my affliction and replace it for me with that which is better).
items.section: Chapter: Faith in al-Qadar (The Devine Decree)

items.q: Is there good and evil in al-Qadar?
items.a: Yes.
items.section: Chapter: Faith in al-Qadar (The Devine Decree)

items.q: Allāh, Exalted is He, said: “and all things We have recorded with numbers (as a record) in a Clear Book”. What is the 'Clear Book'?
items.a: It is al-Lawḥ al-Maḥfūẓ.
items.section: Chapter: Faith in al-Qadar (The Devine Decree)

items.q: What are the keys of the unwitnessed which none knows except Allāh?
items.a: It is mentioned in the Statement of Allāh, Exalted is He: “Verily Allāh, with Him is the knowledge of the Hour, He sends down the rain, and knows that which is in the wombs. No person knows what he will earn tomorrow, and no person knows in what land he will die. Verily, Allāh is All-Knower, All-Aware” [Luqman: 34].
items.section: Chapter: Faith in al-Qadar (The Devine Decree)

items.q: Mention an āyah which proves the vastness of Allāh's Knowledge.
items.a: He, Exalted is He, said: “Those who disbelieve say: The Hour will not come to us. Say: Yes, by my Lord, the All-Knower of the Unseen, it will come to you; not even the weight of an atom or less than that or greater escapes His Knowledge in the heavens or in the earth but it is in a Clear Book” [Sabaʾ: 3].
items.section: Chapter: Faith in al-Qadar (The Devine Decree)

items.q: Did Allāh know who are the people of al-Jannah and the people of the Fire before He created them?
items.a: Yes.
items.section: Chapter: Faith in al-Qadar (The Devine Decree)

items.q: How many types of decreeing (taqdīr) are there?
items.a: The Eternal Decree: before the creation of the heavens and the earth by fifty thousand years. The General Decree: after the creation of the heavens and the earth, when the Covenant was taken. The Lifespan Decree: at the formation of the drop in the womb. The Annual Decree: on the Night of al-Qadr. The Daily Decree.
items.section: Chapter: Faith in al-Qadar (The Devine Decree)

items.q: What are the four words which the angel writes for the fetus in its mother’s womb?
items.a: Their provision, lifespan, deeds, and whether it will be wretched or blessed.
items.section: Chapter: Faith in al-Qadar (The Devine Decree)

items.q: When death comes, can it be delayed or hatened?
items.a: No.
items.section: Chapter: Faith in al-Qadar (The Devine Decree)

items.q: When was the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, born?
items.a: The year of the Elephant.

items.q: Where was the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, born?
items.a: In Makkah.

items.q: Why was it called the Year of the Elephant?
items.a: Because Abraha the Abyssinian tried to destroy the Kaʿbah with an army and an elephant during that year.

items.q: Was he able to destroy the Kaʿbah?
items.a: No, rather Allāh destroyed him with birds that pelted them with stones of fire.

items.q: Who is the father of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: He is ʿAbdullāh bnu ʿAbdi l-Muṭṭalib bnu Hāshim.

items.q: Who is the grandfather of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: ʿAbdu l-Muṭṭalib bnu Hāshim bnu ʿAbdi Manāf.

items.q: From which tribe was the Messenger of Allāh, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: From Quraysh.

items.q: Who was his mother?
items.a: Āminah bintu Wahb.

items.q: Did Allāh permit him to visit his mother’s grave?
items.a: Yes, but he was not permitted to supplicate for her because she died upon shirk.

items.q: Mention some of the wet nurses of the Messenger, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.a: Ḥaleemah As-Saʿdiyyah and Thuwaybah the freed slavewoman of Abī Lahab.

items.q: Who financially took charge of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: His grandfather ʿAbdul Muṭṭalib, thereafter his uncle Abū Ṭālib.

items.q: What is the name of Abī Ṭālib?
items.a: ʿAbdu Manāf bnu ʿAbdul Muṭṭalib

items.q: Mention the wives of the Messenger, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.a: Khadeejah bintu Khuwaylid, Sawdah bintu Zamʿah, Maymoonah bintu l-Ḥārith, Āʾisha bintu AbĪ Bakr as-Sideeq, Juwayriyah bintu l-Ḥārith, Zaynab bintu Jaḥsh, Zaynab bintu Khuzaymah, Ramlah bintu AbĪ Sufyān, Safiyyah bint Ḥuyayy, Ḥafsah bintu ʿUmar bnu al-Khattāb, Hind bintu AbĪ Umayya, may Allāh be pleased with them all.

items.q: Who was his first wife?
items.a: Khadeejah bintu Khuwaylid, may Allāh be pleased with her.

items.q: What did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, give her glad tidings of?
items.a: He gave her glad tidings that she would have a house in al-Jannah made of pearls, in which there is no noise nor toil.

items.q: Two wives of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, died in his lifetime. Who are they?
items.a: Khadeejah bintu Khuwaylid and Zaynab bintu Khuzaymah, may Allāh be pleased with them both.

items.q: Three of the Mothers of the Believers migrated to Abyssinia. Who are they?
items.a: Umm Salamah, Sawdah, and Umm Ḥabeebah, may Allāh be pleased with them all.

items.q: What did the disbelievers of Quraysh used to call the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, before prophet-hood?
items.a: Aṣ-Ṣādiq al-Ameen (The Truthful and the Trustworthy).

items.q: Who believed in the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, and said to him: "Never did anyone come with the likes of what you came with and wasn't made an enemy"?
items.a: Waraqah bnu Nawfal, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: What is the kunya (teknonym) of the Messenger, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: Abū l-Qāsim, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.

items.q: Mention seven names of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.a: Muḥammad, Aḥmed, al-Muqaffā (The Followed), al-Hāshir (The gatherer), al-ʿĀqib (The successor), Nabiyyu ar-Raḥmah (The Prophet of Mercy), and Nabiyyu at-Tawbah (The Messenger of Repentance).

items.q: What was engraved in the ring of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: The ring was made from silver and on it was engraved: "محمد رسول الله" (Muḥammad the Messenger of Allāh), each word had its own line.

items.q: What was the character of the Messenger of Allāh, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: His character was the Qurʾān.

items.q: Who is the best of people in character?
items.a: The Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.

items.q: Was the face of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, like a sword?
items.a: No, rather it was like the moon.

items.q: Mention some of those who resembled the Messenger of Allāh, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.a: Al-Ḥasan bnu ʿAli, Jaʿfar bnu Abī Ṭālib, Abū Sufyān bnu l-Ḥārith, Qutham bnu l-ʿAbbas bnu ʿAbdu l-Muṭṭalib, and Fāṭimah the daughter of the Messenger, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, may Allāh be pleased with them all.

items.q: Mention the children of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.a: Ibrāheem, Fāṭimah, Umm Kulthoom, Ruqayyah, Zaynab, al-Qāsim, and ʿAbdullāh.

items.q: Which of the wives of the Messenger of Allāh, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, bore his children?
items.a: All of them were from Khadeejah, except Ibrāheem who was from Māriyah al-Qibṭiyyah, his concubine.

items.q: Did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, have full brothers?
items.a: No, he had no full brothers.

items.q: Which uncle of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, was an enemy to him?
items.a: Abū Lahab.

items.q: What is Abū Lahab's name?
items.a: ʿAbdul ʿUzzā bnu ʿAbdu l-Muṭṭalib.

items.q: What is the name of the sūrah revealed specifically about him?
items.a: Sūrat al-Masad (Tabbat).

items.q: How did the Qurʾān describe Umm Jameel, the wife of Abī Lahab?
items.a: The carrier of wood.

items.q: Where did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, used to worship in the beginning of his affair?
items.a: In the cave called Ḥirāʾ.

items.q: When did revelation descend upon him?
items.a: When he reached the age of fourty.

items.q: What accusations did the mushrikeen direct to the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: They said he was a liar, a mage, crazy, and a poet.

items.q: What is the greatest miracle given to our Prophet Muḥammad, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam ?
items.a: The Qurʾān.

items.q: Mention six miracles of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.a: Splitting of the moon, weeping of the tree trunk, water springing forth from between his fingers, tasbeeḥ of food, the foreleg speaking, speaking to animals.

items.q: From where did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, migrate, and to where?
items.a: He migrated from Makkah to Madeenah.

items.q: How many days did ʿAlī bnu Abī Ṭālib remain in Makkah after the migration of the Messenger, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: 3 days.

items.q: How many times did the companions migrate?
items.a: Three times. The first and second migrations were to Abyssinia, and the third was from Makkah to Madeenah.

items.q: Who is the king of Abyssinia who gave shelter to the companions and defended them?
items.a: An-Najāshī.

items.q: What did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, do when an-Najāshī died?
items.a: He announced his death and prayed the Janāzah prayer for him as an absentee.

items.q: How long did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, remain in Makkah after his prophet-hood?
items.a: 13 years.

items.q: In whom's house did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, hide in Makkah?
items.a: Al-Arqam bnu Abī al-Arqam.

items.q: Who were the first to accept Islām alongside the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: From men: Abū Bakr aṣ-Ṣiddīq; from the children: ʿAlī bnu Abī Ṭālib; from women: Khadeejah bintu Khuwaylid, may Allāh be pleased with them all.

items.q: To where was the Prophet's miraculous night journey?
items.a: To Bayt al-Maqdis.

items.q: Where was he ascended to?
items.a: To the heavens.

items.q: On what riding animal was the Prophet's miraculous night journey?
items.a: Al-Burāq.

items.q: Who accompanied the Messenger of Allāh, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, during al-Hijrah?
items.a: Abū Bakr, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who is Thāt an-Niṭāqayn (She of the Two Waistbands)?
items.a: Asmāʾ bintu Abī Bakr, may Allāh be pleased with her.

items.q: Why was she called Thāt an-Niṭāqayn?
items.a: Because she cut a piece of her waistband and tied the opening of the bag that contained the food for the Messenger of Allāh, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, and his companion Abū Bakr during al-Hijrah.

items.q: Who followed the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, whilst he migrated to Madeenah and the foot of his horse sunk into the ground?
items.a: Surāqah bnu Mālik.

items.q: What is the name of the cave in which the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, and his companion hid during their migration?
items.a: The cave called Thawr.

items.q: What was the name of the female camel which the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, rode during his Hijrah?
items.a: Al-Qaṣwāʾ.

items.q: Who was in the service of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, and Abū Bakr during migration?
items.a: ʿĀmir bnu Fuhayrah.

items.q: Who was the first person to see the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, when he arrived in Madeenah?
items.a: A Jewish man.

items.q: Which masājids were founded upon taqwā (fear of Allāh) from the first day?
items.a: Masjid Qubāʾ, and also the masjid of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.

items.q: To which masjid did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, visit every Saturday whether riding or walking?
items.a: Masjid Qubāʾ.

items.q: What's the origin of the two tribes: al-Aws and al-Khazraj?
items.a: They are Qaḥṭāniyyoon (Qaḥṭānis) from al-Azd from Yemen.

items.q: What are the names of the three Jewish tribes found in Madeenah?
items.a: Banū Quraythah, Banū an-Naḍeer, and Banū Qaynuqāʾ.

items.q: Who is the one that placed magic upon the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: Labeed bnu al-Aʿsam, the Jew.

items.q: What is the definition of a companion?
items.a: He is the one who met the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, while believing in him and died upon that, even if a period of apostasy occurred, according to the correct view.

items.q: Was any of the companions unreliable or dishonest?
items.a: No, all of the companions were upright and trustworthy.

items.q: Who is the best of the companions?
items.a: Abū Bakr, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who are al-Khulafāʾ ar-Rāshidoon (The Rightly-Guided Caliphs)?
items.a: Abū Bakr aṣ-Ṣiddīq, ʿUmar bnu l-Khaṭṭāb, ʿUthmān bnu ʿAffān, and ʿAlī bnu Abī Ṭālib.

items.q: Who are those the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, said about: "No one loves them except a believer, and no one hates them except a hypocrite"?
items.a: The Anṣār.

items.q: What are the best homes of the Anṣār?
items.a: Banū an-Najjār, Banū ʿAbdul Ash-hal, Banū l-Hārith, and Banū Sāʿidah. And there is good in every home of al-Anṣār.

items.q: Who are the ten who were given the glad-tidings of al-Jannah?
items.a: 1) Abū Bakr al-Ṣiddīq. 2) ʿUmar bnu al-Khaṭṭāb. 3) ʿUthmān bnu ʿAffān. 4) ʿAlī bnu Abī Ṭālib. 5) Abū ʿUbayda bnu al-Jarrāḥ. 6) az-Zubayr bnu al-ʿAwwām. 7) Saʿīd bnu Zayd. 8) ʿAbdur-Raḥmān bnu ʿAwf. 9) Saʿd bnu Abī Waqqāṣ. 10) Ṭalḥa bnu ʿUbaydillāh, may Allāh be pleased with them.

items.q: Who is the most beloved man to the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: Abū Bakr aṣ-Ṣiddīq, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: What is the name of Abū Bakr aṣ-Ṣiddīq, may Allāh be pleased with him?
items.a: ʿAbdullāh bnu ʿUthmān.

items.q: Who is al-Fārooq?
items.a: ʿUmar bnu al-Khaṭṭāb, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: What does Shayṭān do if he meets ʿUmar, may Allāh be pleased with him?
items.a: If ʿUmar takes a path, Shayṭān takes another.

items.q: Who is the one the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, said concerning him: "Verily Allāh placed the truth upon his tongue and heart"?
items.a: ʿUmar bnu l-Khaṭṭāb, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who is Thū an-Noorayn (The Owner of the Two Lights)?
