items.a: ʿUthmān bnu ʿAffān, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Why was he called Thū an-Noorayn?
items.a: It is because he married two of the Prophet's daughters: Ruqayyah, and when she died, he married Umm Kulthoom.

items.q: From whom did the angels shy away?
items.a: ʿUthmān bnu ʿAffān, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who dug the Well of Roomah?
items.a: ʿUthmān bnu ʿAffān, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who gathered the Qurʾān?
items.a: Abū Bakr gathered the Qurʾān out of fear that it will be lost, and ʿUthmān gathered it out of fear of differing.

items.q: What is the writing system of the Qurʾān known as?
items.a: The ʿUthmānic script (ar-rasm al-ʿUthmānī).

items.q: Who prepared the army of al-ʿUsrah?
items.a: ʿUthmān bnu ʿAffān, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who is the most modest of this ummah?
items.a: ʿUthmān bnu ʿAffān, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: What is the expedition of al-ʿUsrah?
items.a: It is the expedition of Tabook.

items.q: Who are the three who didn't join the expedition of Tabook, then Allāh accepted their repentance?
items.a: Kʿab bnu Mālik, Murārah bnu ar-Rabeeʿ, and Hilāl bnu Umayyah.

items.q: Who was the husband of the Prophet's daughter Fāṭimah, may Allāh be pleased with her?
items.a: ʿAlī bnu Abī Ṭālib, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: What did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, say concerning ʿAli in the expedition of Khaybar?
items.a: He said: “Tomorrow I will give this banner to a man by whose hands Allāh will grant victory. He loves Allāh and His Messenger, and Allāh and His Messenger love him”.

items.q: About whom did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, say: "You are from me and I am from you"?
items.a: ʿAlī bnu Abī Ṭālib, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who was the ḥawārī  (disciple) of the Messenger of Allāh, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: Az-Zubayr bnu al-ʿAwwām, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who was the husband of Asmāʾ, the daughter of Abī Bakr, may Allāh be pleased with her?
items.a: Az-Zubayr bnu al-ʿAwwām, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who was the first infant born in al-Madeenah from amongst the Muslims?
items.a: ʿAbdullāh bnu az-Zubayr, may Allāh be pleased with them both.

items.q: Who is the Trustworthy of this ummah?
items.a: Abū ʿUbaydah bnu al-Jarrāḥ, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, send to Bahrain in order to collect jizyah?
items.a: Abū ʿUbaydah bnu al-Jarrāḥ, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who is the first one to shoot an arrow for the sake of Allāh?
items.a: Saʿd bnu Abī Waqqāṣ, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: To whom did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, said: "Shoot, may my father and mother be sacrificed for you"?
items.a: Saʿd bnu Abī Waqqāṣ, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who was the servant of the Messenger, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: Anas bnu Mālik, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who was the one in charge of the sandals, the pillow, and the water-container?
items.a: ʿAbdullāh bnu Masʿood, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who was the caretaker of the Prophet's mule?
items.a: ʿUqbah bnu ʿĀmir, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: What was the name of the Prophet's donkey?
items.a: ʿUfayr.

items.q: Who is the female whom the Prophet took as captive in the expedition of Khaybar then freed her and married her?
items.a: Safiyyah bintu Ḥuyayy, may Allāh be pleased with her.

items.q: Who was the companion who died a shaheed after partaking a meal (poisoned lamb) with the Messenger, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, which was poisoned by the Jewish woman Zaynab bintu l-Hārith?
items.a: Bishr bnu l-Barā'.

items.q: Who are the two chiefs of the youth of the people of al-Jannah?
items.a: Al-Ḥasan and al-Ḥusayn.

items.q: Who were the two rayḥānas (beloved delights) of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, in this world?
items.a: Al-Ḥasan and al-Ḥusayn.

items.q: Who is the Lion of Allāh and the Lion of His Messenger?
items.a: Ḥamzah bnu ʿAbdi al-Muṭṭalib, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who was the interpreter of the Qurʾān and the scholar of this ummah?
items.a: ʿAbdullāh bnu ʿAbbās, may Allāh be pleased with them both.

items.q: Who was the one whom ʿUmar used to admit along with the elders of Badr?
items.a: ʿAbdullāh bnu ʿAbbās, may Allāh be pleased with them both.

items.q: Who is the companion about whom the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, said: "A sword from the swords of Allāh"?
items.a: Khālid bnu al-Waleed, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who was the companion who was given a beautiful voice like the beautiful voices of the family of Dāwood?
items.a: Abū Moosā al-Ashʿarī, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Which companion narrated the most ḥadīths?
items.a: Abū Hurayrah, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who are the prolific narrators of ḥadīth, those who narrated more than one thousand ḥadīths?
items.a: Abū Hurayrah, Ibn ʿUmar, Anas bnu Mālik, Abū Saʿīd al-Khudrī, Ibn ʿAbbās, Jābir bnu ʿAbdillāh, and ʿĀʾishah, may Allāh be pleased with them.

items.q: Who are the four companions with the name of ʿAbdullāh?
items.a: ʿAbdullāh bnu ʿAbbās, ʿAbdullāh bnu ʿAmr bnu al-ʿĀṣ, ʿAbdullāh bnu ʿUmar, ʿAbdullāh bnu az-Zubayr, may Allāh be pleased with them all.

items.q: Which companion did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, supplicate for to increase his wealth and his children?
items.a: Anas bnu Mālik, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Which companion did Allāh speak to directly after he was killed as a shaheed for the sake of Allāh?
items.a: ʿAbdullāh bnu ʿAmr bnu Ḥarām, the father of Jābir, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Which companion did Allāh command the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, to recite to him the statement: “Those who disbelieve from the People of the Book were never” [Al-Bayyinah: 1]?
items.a: Ubayy bnu Kaʿb, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who is the one the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, advised to speak the truth even if it was bitter?
items.a: Abū Tharr al-Ghifārī, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who is the companion the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, said referring to him: "There is no one on earth or under the sky who speaks more truthfully than Abū Tharr".
items.a: Abū Tharr al-Ghifārī may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Due to whose death did the Throne of Allāh shake?
items.a: Saʿd bnu Muʿāth, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Whose funeral did the angels carry?
items.a: Saʿd bnu Muʿāth, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Whose testimony is equivalent to two witnesses?
items.a: Khuzaymah bnu Thābit, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Whose body was washed by the angels?
items.a: Ḥanthalah bnu Abī ʿĀmir al-Anṣāry, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who was protected by a swarm of wasps from the mushrikeen?
items.a: ʿĀṣim bnu Thābit, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Which companion did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, command to learn the Jewish language?
items.a: Zayd bnu Thābit, may Allāh be pleased with him, who learnt it in 17 days.

items.q: Who are the four which the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, commanded that the Qurʾān be learnt from them?
items.a: Bnu Masʿood, Ubayy bnu Kaʿb, Muʿāth bnu Jabal, Sālim Mawlā Abī Ḥuthayfah (The freed slave of Abī Ḥuthayfah), may Allāh be pleased with them all.

items.q: Who was The keeper of the Messenger's secret?
items.a: Ḥuthayfah bnu al-Yamān, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who dreamt about the athān?
items.a: ʿAbdullāh bnu Zayd bnu ʿAbdi Rabbih, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who were the leaders in the expedition of Muʾtah?
items.a: Zayd bnu Ḥārithah, Jaʿfar bnu Abī Ṭālib, ʿAbdullāh bnu Rawāḥah.

items.q: Who is the companion who will have two wings to fly with in al-Jannah?
items.a: Jaʿfar bnu Abī Ṭālib, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who is the companion whose name is mentioned in the Qurʾān?
items.a: Zayd bnu Ḥārithah, may Allāh be pleased with him, in sūrat al-Aḥzāb: “So when Zayd had accomplished his desire from her” [Al-Aḥzāb: 38].

items.q: Who did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, tell: "You are our brother and our freed slave"?
items.a: Zayd bnu Ḥārithah, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who is the beloved of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, and the son of his beloved?
items.a: Usāmah bnu Zayd, may Allāh be pleased with them both.

items.q: Who said: "I used to ask the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, concerning evil matters out of fear of them afflicting me"?
items.a: Ḥuthayfah bnu al-Yamān, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Mention some of the companions who became blind.
items.a: Bnu ʿAbbās, bnu ʿUmar, Kaʿb bnu Mālik, Ḥasān bnu Thābit, Jābir bnu ʿAbdillāh, bnu Ummi Maktoom, ʿItbān bnu Mālik, may Allāh be pleased with them all.

items.q: Who are the two companions the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, sent to Yemen?
items.a: Abū Moosā al-Ashʿary and Muʿāth bnu Jabal, may Allāh be pleased with them both.

items.q: Who was the blind person who the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, did not permit to pray in his home?
items.a: Bnu Ummi Maktoom, as he was strong and able to go to the masjid.

items.q: Who was the blind person the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, permitted to pray in his house?
items.a: ʿItban bnu Mālik, as he was weak and not able to go to the masjid.

items.q: Who was the blind man about whom Allāh, Exalted is He, revealed: “He frowned and turned away * because there came to him a blind man” [ʿAbasa: 1-2]?
items.a: Bnu Ummi Maktoom, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who was the leader of the hypocrites?
items.a: ʿAbdullāh bnu Ubayy bnu Salool.

items.q: Who is his father, and who is his mother?
items.a: His father is Ubayy, and his mother is Salool.

items.q: Who is the husband of the Prophet's eldest daughter, Zaynab?
items.a: Abū al-ʿAas bnu ar-Rabeeʿ, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, carry while praying?
items.a: Umāmah bint Abī al-ʿAas bnu ar-Rabeeʿ, may Allāh be pleased with her.

items.q: Who said at the time of his death: "I could not endure gazing at him for long out of reverence", referring to the Messenger of Allāh, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: ʿAmr bnu al-ʿĀṣ, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who is the companion about whom the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, said: "One of the best of those from Yemen. His face shows an angelic beauty"?
items.a: Jareer bnu ʿAbdillāh al-Bajalī, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who is the companion about whom the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, said: "What a fierce warrior he is! If only he had someone to support him"?
items.a: Abū Baṣeer (ʿUtbah bnu Aseed), may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Which companion said: "I have ten children and I have never kissed any of them"?
items.a: Al-Aqraʿ bnu Ḥābis, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who was captured, and tied in the masjid, then accepted Islām thereafter?
items.a: Thumāmah bnu Athāl, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who deceived the mushrikeen in the expedition of al-Aḥzāb?
items.a: Nuʿaym bnu Masʿood, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Which companion did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, hear his recitation in al-Jannah, and who was also dutiful to his mother?
items.a: Ḥārithah bnu an-Nuʿmān, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who is the companion about whom the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, said: "His voice in the army is better than a thousand men"?
items.a: Abū Talḥah, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who was buried with the father of Jābir in one grave and had a limp?
items.a: ʿAmr bnu al-Jamooḥ, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Which companion informed the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, that he had a lapse during prayer?
items.a: Thul Yadayn (He of the Two Hands): Al-Khirbāq bnu ʿAmr, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who is the companion the angels descended to listen to his recitation?
items.a: Usayd bnu Ḥuḍayr, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: About which companion did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, say: "He is filled with Īmān to his very marrow"?
items.a: ʿAmmār bnu Yāsir, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who killed Sumayyah, the mother of ʿAmmār, may Allāh be pleased with her?
items.a: Abū Jahl, may the curse of Allāh be upon him.

items.q: Who is the one for whom the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, performed the absentee [funeral] prayer?
items.a: An-Najāshī, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who was the first Jew to accept Islām?
items.a: ʿAbdullāh bnu Salām, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Whose shin is heavier on the scale than Mount Uḥud?
items.a: ʿAbdullāh bnu Masʿood, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who was the noble companion Jibreel took his form?
items.a: Diḥyah al-Kalbī, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who is the companion who took the Prophet's sword with full responsibility, and used it to crack open the skulls of the mushrikeen?
items.a: Abū Dujānah: Simāk bnu Kharashah, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who asked the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, for his companionship in al-Jannah, and what did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, reply?
items.a: He is Rabeeʿah bnu Kaʿb al-Aslamī, Abū Firās, may Allāh be pleased with him. The Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, replied to him: "Help me regarding yourself by increasing in prostration".

items.q: Who is the first of the Muslim leaders to ride the sea of Rome?
items.a: Muʿāwiyah bnu Abī Sufyān, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Mention three of the athān callers during the time of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.a: 1- Bilāl bnu Rabāḥ. 2- Abū Maḥthoorah. 3- Bnu Ummi Maktoom, may Allāh be pleased with them all.

items.q: Mention three poets of the Messenger, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.a: 1- Ḥassān bnu Thābit. 2- ʿAbdullāh bnu Rawāḥah. 3- Kʿab bnu Mālik, may Allāh be pleased with them all.

items.q: Who is the poet about whom the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, said: "O' Allāh aid him with the Holy Spirit"?
items.a: Ḥassān bnu Thābit, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Mention five writers of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.
items.a: 1- ʿAmr bnu al-ʿĀṣ. 2- Zayd bnu Thābit. 3- ʿAbdullāh bnu Rawāḥah. 4- Muʿāwiyah bnu Abī Sufyān. 5- Khālid bnu al-Waleed, may Allāh be pleased with them all.

items.q: Mention the messengers the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, sent to kings.
items.a: 1- To Qaysar, he sent Diḥyah al-Kalbī. 2- To an-Najāshī, he sent ʿAmr bnu Umayyah. 3- To Kisrā, he sent ʿAbdullāh bnu Huthāfah. 4- To al-Muqawqis, he sent Ḥātib bnu Abī Baltaʿah, may Allāh be pleased with them all.

items.q: Which king tore the book of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, whereby Allāh tore his kingdom?
items.a: Kisrā.

items.q: Which companion used to work in the cloth market and gifted the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, a black decorated tunic?
items.a: Abū Jahm, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Why did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, return the black decorated tunic?
items.a: Because of its decoration lines which distracted him when he prayed.

items.q: How did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, make Abū Jahm feel good after returning his gift?
items.a: He said: "Bring me a simple wool tunic from Abū Jahm".

items.q: Which companion hosted the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, in his home when he arrived in Madeenah?
items.a: Abū Ayyoob Al-Anṣāry, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Which companion did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, give a key to the Kaʿbah at the conquest of Makkah?
items.a: ʿUthmān bnu Talḥah, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Mention the name of a companion from the jinn.
items.a: Zawbaʿah, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who is the bedouin that urinated in the masjid?
items.a: Thu l-Khuwayṣirah al-Yamānī, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who said: "Be just O' Muḥammad"?
items.a: Thul Khuwayṣirah at-Tamīmī al-Khārijī.

items.q: Mention the names of each kunyah: Abū Qatādah, Abū Hurayrah, Abū Jandal, Abū Tharr, Abū Ayyūb, Abū Moosā al-Ashʿarī, Abū Bakrah, Abū Saʿeed al-Khudri.
items.a: Abū Qatādah: Al-Hārith bnu Ribʿiyy. Abū Hurayrah: ʿAbdu ar-Raḥmān bnu Ṣakhr. Abū Jandal: Al-ʿĀṣ bnu Suhayl bnu ʿAmr. Abū Tharr: Jundub bnu Junādah. Abū Ayyūb: Khālid bnu Zayd. Abū Moosā al-Ashʿarī: ʿAbdullāh bnu Qays. Abū Bakrah: Nufayʿ bnu al-Ḥārith. Abū Saʿeed al-Khudrī: Saʿd bnu Mālik, may Allāh be pleased with them all.

items.q: Who are the best of women of all creation?
items.a: Fāṭimah bintu Muḥammad, Khadeejah bintu Khuwaylid, Āsiyah bintu Muzāḥim (the wife of Firʿawn), and Maryam binti ʿImrān, may Allāh be pleased with them all.

items.q: Who is the chief of women of al-Jannah?
items.a: Fāṭimah the daughter of the Messenger of Allāh, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.

items.q: Who is the most knowledgeable woman?
items.a: ʿĀʾishah, may Allāh be pleased with her.

items.q: Who is the first woman to accept Islām?
items.a: Khadeejah bintu Khuwaylid, may Allāh be pleased with her.

items.q: Who is the companion who used to have seizures and she is from the people of Jannah?
items.a: Ummu Zufar, may Allāh be pleased with her.

items.q: What is the name of the female slave who ʿĀʾishah, may Allāh be pleased with her, freed?
items.a: Bareerah, may Allāh be pleased with her.

items.q: What is the last statement the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, said before his death?
items.a: "O' Allāh, admit us to the highest companionship".

items.q: Who are the highest of companionship?
items.a: They are the Prophets, the Ṣiddīqūn, the shuhadāʾ, and the righteous.

items.q: How old was the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, when he died?
items.a: Sixty three.

items.q: In which wife's home did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, die?
items.a: In the home of ʿĀʾishah, may Allāh be pleased with her.

items.q: Was the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, stripped of his clothes when he was washed after his death?
items.a: No. He was washed while his clothes were on him.

items.q: Where was the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, buried?
items.a: He was buried in the room of ʿĀʾishah. The Prophets are buried in the place where they die.

items.q: How old was Abū Bakr, may Allāh be pleased with him, when he died?
items.a: Sixty three.

items.q: How old was ʿUmar bnu al-Khaṭṭāb, may Allāh be pleased with him, when he was killed?
items.a: Sixty three.

items.q: How old was ʿUthmān, may Allāh be pleased with him, when he was killed?
items.a: He was over eighty years old.

items.q: How old was ʿAlī bnu Abī Ṭālib, may Allāh be pleased with him, when he was killed?
items.a: Sixty three.

items.q: Who killed ʿUmar bnu Al-Khaṭṭāb, may Allāh be pleased with him?
items.a: Abū Luʾluʾa al-Majoosī, may the curse of Allāh be upon him.

items.q: Who killed ʿUthmān, may Allāh be pleased with him?
items.a: Al-Khāwārij.

items.q: Who killed Alī, may Allāh be pleased with him?
items.a: ʿAbdu ar-Raḥmān bnu Muljim, may the curse of Allāh be upon him.

items.q: Who was the last of the companions to die?
items.a: Abū aṭ-Ṭufayl ʿĀmir bnu Wāthilah, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: What are the best centuries?
items.a: The companions, then the tābiʿeen (the followers), and then the tābiʿī at-Tābiʿeen (the followers of the followers).

items.q: Who is the chief of the tābiʿeen?
items.a: The leader of the tābiʿeen in knowledge is: Saʿeed bnu al-Musayyib, and in worship and abstinence: Uways al-Qaranī.

items.q: What was the title of ʿAli bnu Al-Ḥusayn bnu ʿAlī bnu Abī Ṭālib?
items.a: Zaynu al-ʿĀbideen (Ornament of the worshipers).

items.q: What is the greatest battle of the Muslims?
items.a: The Great Battle of Badr.

items.q: What was the number of Muslims in the battle of Badr?
items.a: There were approximately 310 men.

items.q: What was the number of the mushrikeen in the battle of Badr?
items.a: Their number was 1000.

items.q: How many were killed and held captive from amongst Quraysh on the day of Badr?
items.a: 70 were killed and 70 were held captive.

items.q: Who are the ones about whom the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, said: "Allāh has looked upon them and said: 'Do as you wish, for I have forgiven you'"?
items.a: The people of Badr.

items.q: Which sūrah explains the events of the Battle of Badr in detail?
items.a: Sūrat al-Anfāl.

items.q: When did the battle of Uḥud take place?
items.a: In the 3rd year of Hijrah.

items.q: In which battle was the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, wounded, suffered a head injury, and had his upper front teeth broken?
items.a: The battle of Uḥud.

items.q: What was the number of Muslims in the battle of Uḥud?
items.a: There were 1000 fighters.

items.q: What was the number of the mushrikeen in the battle of Uhud?
items.a: There were 3000 fighters.

items.q: Who was the leader of the archers whom the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, appointed to hold post on the mountain of the archers on they day of Uḥud?
items.a: The leader was ʿAbdullāh bnu Jubayr, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: What did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, command those archers?
items.a: He commanded them to remain at their posts no matter what happens.

items.q: What did the archers do after that?
items.a: As soon as the archers saw the defeat of the mushrikeen, they left their posts and cried out "The spoils! the spoils!", except for ʿAbdullāh bnu Jubayr and nine men with him.

items.q: What did Khālid bnu al-Waleed do when the archers left their posts?
items.a: He encircled the Muslims from behind and killed ʿAbdullāh bnu Jubayr and those with him, then continued an attack upon the Muslims from behind.

items.q: How many Muslims died as shuhadāʾ in the battle of Uḥud?
items.a: There were 70 shuhadāʾ.

items.q: Who is the chief of the shuhadāʾ?
items.a: Ḥamzah bnu ʿAbdu al-Muṭṭalib.

items.q: Who killed Ḥamzah?
items.a: Waḥshī bnu Ḥarb, may Allāh be pleased with him, before he accepted Islām.

items.q: Who is the companion that fought defending the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, until his hand became paralyzed?
items.a: Talḥa bnu ʿUbaydillāh, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: What is the name of the mountain that the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, said about: "It loves us and we love it"?
items.a: The mountain of Uḥud.

items.q: Who is the companion who died as a shaheed on the day of Uḥud and none recognized him except his sister by his fingertips?
items.a: Anas bnu an-Naḍr, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: What did the Muslims do when they knew the confederates were embarking upon them to wage war?
items.a: They dug the trench.

items.q: How many Muslims were in the Battle of the Confederates?
items.a: Three thousand.

items.q: How many mushrikeen were in the Battle of the Confederates?
items.a: Ten thousand.

items.q: What was the slogan of the Muslims on the Day of the Trench?
items.a: "Ḥā meem, they will not be victorious".

items.q: Who did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, send in order to view the state of the mushrikeen on the Day of the Trench?
items.a: Ḥuthayfah bnu al-Yamān, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: What is the name of the Battle in which the mushrikeen placed Madeenah under siege?
items.a: Al-Ahzāb (the Confederates).

items.q: How long did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, hold the people of Qurayẓah under siege?
items.a: 25 nights.

items.q: Who passed judgment concerning Banū Qurayẓah, and what was his ruling?
items.a: Saʿd bnu Muʿāth. He ruled that their men be killed, their women and children be held as captives, and their wealth divided.

items.q: What is the name of the pledge the companions gave the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, under the Tree?
items.a: The Pledge of Riḍwān (pleasure / contentement / approval / acceptance).

items.q: In which sūrah is the Statement of Allāh, Exalted is He: “Indeed, Allāh was pleased with the believers when they gave their Bayʿah (pledge) to you (O' Muḥammad ) under the tree, He knew what was in their hearts, and He sent down as-sakeenah (calmness and tranquility) upon them, and He rewarded them with a near victory”?
items.a: Sūrat al-Fatḥ.

items.q: Which companion wrote the Peace Treaty of Ḥudaybiyyah?
items.a: ʿAlī bnu Abī Ṭālib, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: When was the Battle of Khaybar?
items.a: In the 7th year of Hijrah.

items.q: When was the battle of Muʾtah?
items.a: In the 8th year of Hijrah, in the month of The First Jumādā.

items.q: Which battle was it that some of the Muslims were impressed by their numbers, then the land felt too narrow even when it is so vast?
items.a: Battle of Ḥunayn.

items.q: When was the battle of Ḥunayn?
items.a: In the 8th year of Hijrah in the Month of Shawwāl.

items.q: When was the Battle of Tabook and against whom was it fought?
items.a: It was in the 9th year of Hijrah, and it was between the Muslims and the Romans.

items.q: During which expedition did Allāh send forth a whale for the Muslims, from which they ate for about a month?
items.a: Sayf al-Baḥr (The Sword of the Sea).

items.q: What was the first battle that occurred between the Muslims and the Romans after the death of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: It was the battle of Ajnādeen, and the leader was ʿAmr bnu al-ʿĀṣ.

items.q: What is the name of the conclusive battle between the Muslims and the Persians?
items.a: Al-Qādisiyyah.

items.q: In the reign of which caliph did the battle Thāt aṣ-Ṣawārī take place?
items.a: ʿUthmān bnu ʿAffān, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who was the first Muslim governor of (Andalus) Spain?
items.a: Moosā bnu Nuṣayr.

items.q: Which Abbāsī caliph’s reign was a time of goodness and jihād for the sake of Allāh?
items.a: Hāroon ar-Rashīd.

items.q: Which Umawī caliph was fond of architecture?
items.a: Al-Waleed bnu ʿAbdu al-Malik.

items.q: Who was the last Abbāsī caliph?
items.a: Al-Mustaʿṣim bi-Allāh.

items.q: Is the Qurʾān created?
items.a: No, rather it is the Speech of Allāh, descended and not created.

items.q: In which chapter of the Qurʾān does Allāh, Exalted is He, say: “And if anyone of the Mushrikīn seeks your protection then grant him protection, so that he may hear the Word of Allāh (the Qurʾān), and then escort him to where he can be secure, that is because they are men who know not”?
items.a: Sūrat at-Tawbah.

items.q: Which sect stirred up the trial over the claim that the Qurʾān was created?
items.a: The Muʿtazilah.

items.q: Which scholar refused to say that the Qurʾān was created and was harmed due to this?
items.a: Al-imām Aḥmed bnu Ḥanbal, may Allāh have mercy on him.

items.q: In which sūrah does Allāh, Exalted is He, say: “So, when the Qurʾān is recited, listen to it, and pay attention, so that you may have mercy upon you”?
items.a: In sūrat al-Aʿrāf.

items.q: Who are the people of Allāh and His chosen ones?
items.a: They are the people of the Qurʾān, those who act according to it in worldly life.

items.q: How was the Qurʾān revealed?
items.a: It was revealed all at once to Bayt al-ʿIzzah (the House of Honor), in the sky of this world. Then, according to various circumstances and events, it was revealed in stages over a period of twenty-three years.

items.q: On which night was the Qurʾān revealed?
items.a: It was revealed on the Night of al-Qadr in the month of Ramaḍān.

items.q: What was the first to be revealed from the Qurʾān?
items.a: The first 5 āyāt of sūrat al-ʿAlaq.

items.q: Is the Qurʾān in Arabic or non-Arabic?
items.a: The Qurʾān is Arabic.

items.q: How many suwar are there in the Qurʾān?
items.a: There are 114 suwar in the Qurʾān.

items.q: What is the definition of Makkiyy suwar and Madani suwar?
items.a: The Makkiyy suwar are those revealed before the migration, and the Madani suwar are those revealed after migration.

items.q: How many Makkiyy suwar are there?
How many Madani suwar are there?
Mention the different divisions of suwar?
items.a: The Seven long ones. The Hundreds. Al-Mathāny. Al-Mufaṣṣal.

items.q: What are the Seven Long ones?
items.a: Al-Baqarah. Āl ʿImrān. An-Nisāʾ. Al-Māʾidah. Al-Anʿām. Al-Aʿrāf. At-Tawbah.

items.q: Which suwar are referred to as the Hundreds?
items.a: They are the suwar whose āyāt number about one hundred, slightly less or slightly more.

items.q: What suwar are referred to as al-Mathāny?
items.a: These suwar begin after the Hundreds up to sūrat Qāf.

items.q: What are the Mufaṣṣal?
items.a: The suwar starting from sūrat Qāf to the end of the Qurʾān.

items.q: What is the longest sūrah in the Qurʾān?
items.a: It is sūrat al-Baqarah.

items.q: Mention 5 names of the Qurʾān?
items.a: Al-Kitāb (The Book). An-Noor (The Light). Al-Furqān (That which divides and expresses). Al-Hudā (The Guidance). Ar-Rooḥ (The Spirit).

items.q: What is the longest āyah in the Qurʾān?
items.a: It is the āyah of ad-dayn (debt), which is in sūrat al-Baqarah.

items.q: What is the shortest sūrah in the Qurʾān?
items.a: It is sūrat al-Kawthar.

items.q: What is the greatest sūrah in the Qurʾān?
items.a: Sūrat al-Fātiḥah.

items.q: What is the greatest āyah in the Qurʾān?
items.a: Āyat al-Kursī.

items.q: What are as-Sabʿ al-Mathānī (the seven of the repeated āyāt) and the Grand Qurʾān?
items.a: Sūrat al-Fātiḥah.

items.q: What sūrah is the Mother of the Qurʾān?
items.a: Sūrat al-Fātiḥah.

items.q: What are az-Zahrāwān (the Two Radiant Ones)?
items.a: Sūrat al-Baqarah and sūrat Āl ʿImrān.

items.q: Which āyah of the Qurʾān prevents the Shayṭān from coming close to the one who recites it before sleeping?
items.a: Āyat al-Kursī.

items.q: Write down āyat al-Kursī.
items.a: “Allāhu lā ilāha illā Huwa, al-Ḥayyu al-Qayyūm. Lā taʾkhuthuhu sinatun wa-lā nawm. Lahu mā fī as-samāwāti wa-mā fī al-arḍ. Man thā allathī yashfaʿu ʿindahu illā bi-ithnih. Yaʿlamu mā bayna aydīhim wa-mā khalfahum, wa-lā yuḥīṭūna bi-shayʾin min ʿilmihī illā bimā shāʾ. Wasiʿa kursiyyuhu as-samāwāti wa-l-arḍ, wa-lā yaʾūduhu ḥifẓuhumā. Wa-Huwa al-ʿAliyyu al-ʿAẓīm” (Allāhu lā ilāha illa Huwa (none has the right to be worshipped but He), the Ever Living, the One Who sustains and protects all that exists. Neither slumber, nor sleep overtake Him. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on earth. Who is he that can intercede with Him except with His Permission? He knows what happens to them (His creatures) in this world, and what will happen to them in the Hereafter. And they will never compass anything of His Knowledge except that which He wills. His Kursī extends over the heavens and the earth, and He feels no fatigue in guarding and preserving them. And He is the Most High, the Most Great) [Al-Baqaah: 255].

items.q: What two āyāt are sufficient for the one who reads them at night?
items.a: The two last āyāt of sūrat al-Baqarah.

items.q: What is the sūrah consisting of 30 āyāt, which will intercede for the reciter
items.a: Sūrat al-Mulk.

items.q: Which sūrah does not begin with "Bismillāhi ar-Raḥmāni ar-Raḥīm"?
items.a: Sūrat Barāʾah (at-Tawbah).

items.q: Which sūrah is al-Fadhihah (The Exposer)?
items.a: It is sūrat Barā'ah (at-Tawbah), because it exposed the hypocrites.

items.q: Which is the sūrah of fighting?
items.a: Sūrat Muḥammad.

items.q: Which āyāt, if memorized, a person is guarded from ad-Dajjāl?
items.a: The first ten āyat of sūrat Al-Kahf.

items.q: Mention five suwar which begin with Hā-Meem?
items.a: Ghāfir. Fuṣṣilat. Ash-Shoorā. Az-Zukhruf. Ad-Dukh-khān.

items.q: Mention five suwar which begin with Alif Lām Rā?
items.a: Yoonus. Hūd. Yūsuf. Ibrāheem. Al-Ḥijr.

items.q: Mention five suwar which begin with Alif Lām Meem?
items.a: Al-Baqarah. Āl ʿImrān. Al–ʿAnkaboot. Luqmān. As-Sajdah.

items.q: How many suwar are there in Juzʾ Tabārak?
items.a: 11 suwar.

items.q: How many suwar are there in Juzʾ ʿAmma?
items.a: 37 suwar.

items.q: Which sūrah begins with the name of two fruits?
items.a: Sūrat at-Tīn.

items.q: The term ‘Anf’ (nose) is mentioned in the Qurʾān twice. In which sūrah is that?
items.a: Sūrat al-Māʾidah: “And We ordained therein for them: Life for life, eye for eye, nose for nose, ear for ear, tooth for tooth, and wounds equal for equal. But if anyone remits the retaliation by way of charity, it shall be for him an expiation. And whosoever does not judge by that which Allāh has revealed, such are the Zālimūn (polytheists and wrong-doers – of a lesser degree)” [5:45].

items.q: In which sūrah has the term of Jalālah (Allāh) been mentioned in every āyah?
items.a: Sūrat al-Mujādilah [58].

items.q: Which is the longest word in the Qurʾān?
items.a: Allāh, Exalted is He, said: "فَأَسْقَيْنَاكُمُوهُ" (Fa asqaynākumoohu = And we give it to you to drink).

items.q: Mention the āyah in which the word "Dīnār" can be found?
items.a: Allāh, Exalted is He, said: "Among the people of the Scripture (Jews and Christians) is he who, if entrusted with a Cantar (a great amount of wealth, etc.), will readily pay it back; and among them there is he who, if entrusted with a single silver coin, will not repay it unless you constantly stand demanding, because they say: ‘There is no blame on us to betray and take the properties of the illiterates (Arabs)." But they tell a lie against Allāh while they know it". [Aal-'Imrān: 75].

items.q: In which āyah is the word "ad-Dirham" mentioned?
items.a: Allāh, Exalted is He, said: "And they sold him for a low price, - for a few Dirhams (i.e. for a few silver coins). And they were of those who regarded him insignificant". [Yūsuf: 20].

items.q: Within which āyah can the entire letters of the alphabet be found?
items.a: “Muḥammadun Rasūlu-llāhi wa-llathīna maʿahu ashiddāʾu ʿala-l-kuffāri ruḥamāʾu baynahum tarāhum rukkaʿan sujjadan yabtaghūna faḍlan mina-llāhi wa-riḍwān. Sīmāhum fī wujūhihim min athari-s-sujūd. Thālika mathaluhum fi-t-Tawrāti wa-mathaluhum fi-l-Injīli kazarʿin akhraja shaṭʾahu fa-āzarahu fa-staghlaẓa fa-stawā ʿalā sūqihi, yuʿjibu z-zurrāʿa liyaghīẓa bihimu-l-kuffār. Waʿada-llāhu llathīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū-ṣ-ṣāliḥāti minhum maghfiratan wa-ajran ʿaẓīmā” (Muḥammad is the Messenger of Allāh, and those who are with him are severe against disbelievers, and merciful among themselves. You see them bowing and falling down prostrate (in prayer), seeking Bounty from Allāh and (His) Good Pleasure. The mark of them (i.e. of their Faith) is on their faces (foreheads) from the traces of (their) prostration (during prayers). This is their description in the Taurat. But their description in the Injeel is like a (sown) seed which sends forth its shoot, then makes it strong, it then becomes thick, and it stands straight on its stem, delighting the sowers that He may enrage the disbelievers with them. Allāh has promised those among them who believe (i.e. all those who follow Islāmic Monotheism, the religion of Prophet Muḥammad , salla-llāhu 'alayhi wa sallam till the Day of Resurrection) and do righteous good deeds, forgiveness and a mighty reward (i.e. al-Jannah)). [Al-Fatḥ: 29].

items.q: Which sūrah was revealed about Banū an-Naḍeer?
items.a: Sūrat al-Ḥashr.

items.q: Which sūrah equals the third of the Qurʾān?
items.a: Sūrat al-Ikhlāṣ.

items.q: What are the two suwar one seeks refuge by reciting them?
items.a: Sūrat al-Falaq and sūrat an-Nās.

items.q: Which word is at the middle of the Qurʾān?
items.a: “Walyatalaṭṭaf” (let him be subtle) in sūrat al-Kahf.

items.q: What was the last sūrah revealed all at once?
items.a: Sūrat an-Naṣr.

items.q: Which āyah was last revealed?
items.a: Allāh, Exalted is He, said: "And be afraid of the Day when you shall be brought back to Allāh. Then every person shall be paid what he earned, and they shall not be dealt with unjustly". [Al-Baqarah: 281].

items.q: Is there a reward for reading from the muṣḥaf?
items.a: Yes, because of the statement of the Messenger of Allāh, salla-llāhu 'alayhi wa sallam: "Whoever wishes that Allāh and His Messenger love him, then he should read from the muṣḥaf".

items.q: In which sūrah is the Statement of Allāh, Exalted is He: "Today, I have perfected your religion for you and completed My Favour unto you, and have chosen for you Islām as your religion".
items.a: Sūrat al-Māʾidah.

items.q: In which sūrah is the Statement of Allāh, Exalted is He: "The Jews said that the Christians follow nothing (i.e. are not on the right religion); and the Christians said that the Jews follow nothing (i.e. are not on the right religion); though they both recite the Scripture. Like unto their word, said (the pagans) who know not. Allāh will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection about that wherein they have been differing".
items.a: In sūrat al-Baqarah.

items.q: In which sūrah is the Statement of Allāh, Exalted is He: "Verily! It is Allāh Who causes the seed-grain and the fruit-stone (like date-stone, etc.) to split and sprout. He brings forth the living from the dead, and it is He Who brings forth the dead from the living. Such is Allāh, then how are you deluded away from the truth?".
items.a: Sūrat al-Anʿām.

items.q: In which sūrah is the Statement of Allāh, Exalted is He: "Allāh is the Light of the heavens and the earth. The parable of His Light is as (if there were) a niche and within it a lamp, the lamp is in glass, the glass as it were a brilliant star, lit from a blessed tree, an olive, neither of the east (i.e. neither it gets sun-rays only in the morning) nor of the west (i.e. nor it gets sun-rays only in the afternoon, but it is exposed to the sun all day long), whose oil would almost glow forth (of itself), though no fire touched it. Light upon Light! Allāh guides to His Light whom He wills. And Allāh sets forth parables for mankind, and Allāh is All-Knower of everything".
items.a: Sūrat an-Noor.

items.q: In which sūrah is the Statement of Allāh, Exalted is He: "Corruption (sins and disobedience of Allāh, etc.) has appeared on land and sea because of what the hands of men have earned (by oppression and evil deeds, etc.), that Allāh may make them taste a part of that which they have done, in order that they may return (by repenting to Allāh, and begging His Pardon)".
items.a: Sūrat ar-Rūm.

items.q: By what means is the Qurʾān interpreted?
items.a: The Qurʾān is interpreted by the Qurʾān, Sunnah, the statements of the companions or the Tābi'een, and by the Arabic language.

items.q: Mention three beneficial tafeers (explanations) of the Qurʾān.
items.a: Tafseer bnu Jareer aṭ-Ṭabarī, Tafseer bnu Katheer, and Tafseer al-Baghawī.

items.q: What is the best tafseer of the Qurʾān?
items.a: Tafseer Ibn Katheer.

items.q: Mention the meaning of these words: al-Mann, as-salwā, al-qith-thāʾ, al-foom and al-Asbāṭ.
items.a: al-Mann: a resinous substance similar to honey. As-salwā: a bird. Al-qith-thā': cucumber. Al-foom: garlic. Al-Asbāṭ: the tribes of Banū Isrāʾeel.

items.q: What if the meaning of ‘Wirdan’ in the Statement of Allāh, Exalted is He: “And We shall drive the criminals to Hell wirdan”.
items.a: It means thirsty.

items.q: The term "ummah" is mentioned in the Qurʾān with four different meanings, what are they?
items.a: Ummah in the Qurʾān comes with the meanings: 1- A group of people: "You are the best ummah brought forth for mankind" [Āl ʿImrān: 110]. 2- A leader who is followed: "Ibrāheem was an ummah" [An-Naḥl: 120]. 3- A period of time: "The one who was saved remembered after an ummah". [Yūsuf: 45]. 4- A religion: "They said, 'We found our fathers upon an ummah'" [Az-Zukhruf: 22].

items.q: Which sūrah did Abū Saʿeed al-Khudrī, may Allāh be pleased with him, read upon the person that was stung, and it was a ruqyah for him?
items.a: Sūrat al-Fātiḥah.

items.q: Which sūrah is named aṣ-Ṣalāt (The Prayer).
items.a: Sūrat al-Fatiḥah.

items.q: In which sūrah is the Statement of Allāh, Exalted is He: "And We send down from the Qurʾān that which is a healing and a mercy to those who believe (in Islamic Monotheism and act on it), and it increases the Ẓalimūn (polytheists and wrong-doers) nothing but loss".
items.a: Sūrat al-Isrāʾ.

items.q: Which sūrah is called the minor sūrat an-Nisāʾ?
items.a: Sūrat aṭ-Ṭalāq.

items.q: Mention the meanings of these words: ummatan wasaṭan, al-fulk, bnu as-sabeel, al-ummiyyeen, ʿāqir, and an-nusuk?
items.a: Ummatan wasaṭan: just and upright nation. Al-fulk: ships. Bnu as-sabeel: stranded wayfarer. Al-ummiyyeen: the Arabs. ʿĀqir: barren, does not bear children. An-nusuk: slaughtering.

items.q: Mention the meanings of these words: al-munkhaniqah, al-mawqoothah, al-mutaraddiyah, and an-naṭeeḥah.
items.a: Al-munkhaniqah is the animal that dies from being choked. Al-mawqoothah is the animal that dies from a hard blow. Al-mutaraddiyah is the animal that falls to its death from a high place. An-nateehah is the animal that dies by being gorged by horns.

items.q: Who are al-aḥbār (Rabbis) and ar-ruhbān (monks)?
items.a: Al-aḥbār are the scholars of the Jews. Ar-ruhbān are the devout worshippers of the Christians.

items.q: What is al-akmah?
items.a: It is someone born blind.

items.q: What is the meaning of “ṣarṣarin”?
items.a: Cold and extreme.

items.q: What is the meaning of sāʾihāt?
items.a: Fasting women.

items.q: What is the meaning of al-khunnas
items.a: The stars that disappear during the day under the sunlight.

items.q: What is the meaning of "ḍabḥan" in the Statement of Allāh, Exalted is He: (وَالْعَادِيَاتِ ضَبْحًا)?
items.a: "Al-ʿĀdiyāt" are horses, "ḍabḥan" is the sound of the horses panting as they gallop.

items.q: What is meant by al-gharoor in His saying, Exalted is He: (وَغَرَّكُم بِاللَّهِ الْغَرُور)?
items.a: It is ash-Shayṭān.

items.q: What is al-mawʾoodah?
items.a: A young girl who is buried alive. The people of Jāhiliyyah used to do this out of fear of disgrace.

items.q: What is the rule of an-noon as-sākinah and tanween?
items.a: It has 4 rules: ith-hār, idghām, iqlāb, and ikhfāʾ.

items.q: What are the letters of ith-hār?
items.a: Hamzah, Hāʾ, ʿAyn, Ḥāʾ, Ghayn, Khāʾ.

items.q: What are the letters of idghām?
items.a: They are grouped in the word "يرملون" (yarmaloon).

items.q: What are the different types of idhgām?
items.a: Two types: 1- Idhgām with ghunnah (incomplete), whose letters are grouped in "ينمو" (yanmoo). 2- Idghām without ghunnah (complete), whose letters are Lām and Rāʾ (ل ر).

items.q: What are the letters of iqlāb?
items.a: One letter only, which is: Bāʾ (ب).

items.q: What are the letters of ikhfāʾ?
items.a: They are the remaining letters, grouped in the opening letters of the verse: Ṣif thā thanā kam jāda shakhṣun qad samā * dum ṭayyiban zid fee tuqan ḍaʾ thālimā.

items.q: What are the rules of al-meem as-sākinah?
items.a: Three: idghām, ikhfā', ith-hār.

items.q: Which letters cause idghām of al-meem as-sākinah?
items.a: One letter, which is Meem (م).

items.q: Which letters cause ikhfāʾ of al-Meem as-sākinah?
items.a: One letter, which is Bāʾ (ب).

items.q: How many letters are there for ith-hār al-Meem as-sākinah?
items.a: All letters except Meem and Bāʾ (م ب).

items.q: What are the letters of al-Qalqalah?
items.a: Qāf, Ṭāʾ, Bāʾ, Jeem, Dāl (قطب جد).

items.q: What is al-ghunnah?
items.a: It is a sound which is released from the nasal cavity, sustained for the duration of two counts (ḥarakāts).

items.q: How many types of elongations are there?
items.a: There are two types: original and derived.

items.q: How many types of derived elongation are there?
items.a: Two: The first is because of hamzah. The second is because of sukoon.

items.q: How many types of elongations caused by hamzah are there?
items.a: Two: Obligatory connected elongation. Permissible disconnected elongation.

items.q: How many types of elongations caused by sukoon are there?
items.a: Two: Incidental elongation due to sukoon, and necessary elongation.

items.q: How many types of necessary elongations are there?
items.a: Two: necessary word-based elongation and necessary letter-based elongation. Each of them is further divided into light and heavy.

items.q: How many general points of articulation are there?
items.a: Five: The oral cavity (al-jawf), the throat, the tongue, the lips, and the nasal passage.

items.q: What are the letters which come in the beginning of the suwar of Qurʾān called?
items.a: They are called al-Ḥuroof al-Muqattaʿah (the disjointed letters).

items.q: How many disjointed letters are there?
items.a: Fourteen, gathered in the phrase: "صِلْهُ سُحَيْرًا مَنْ قَطَعَكَ" (Ṣilhu suḥayran man qataʿaka).

items.q: How many categories of stopping (waqf) are there?
items.a: Four: compulsory, anticipatory, testing, and optional.

items.q: How many categories of optional stopping (al-waqf al-ikhtiyārī) are there?
items.a: Four: complete stop, sufficient stop, good stop, and bad stop.

items.q: Into how many categories is Arabic speech divided?
items.a: Into nouns, verbs, and particles.

items.q: Into how many types are sentences divided?
items.a: Into nominal sentences and a verbal sentences.

items.q: What is a nominal sentence?
items.a: It is a sentence that begins with a noun.

items.q: What is a verbal sentence?
items.a: It is a sentence that begins with a verb.

items.q: What is a noun?
items.a: A word that denotes a named entity and is not associated with time.

items.q: What is a verb?
items.a: A word that denotes an action or occurrence and is associated with time.

items.q: What is a particle?
items.a: A word whose meaning is understood in relation to something else.

items.q: How many types of verbs are there?
items.a: Past, present, and imperative.

items.q: What is the past tense verb?
items.a: That which denotes an action that occurred before the time of speaking.

items.q: What is the present tense verb?
items.a: That which denotes an action occurring at the time of speaking or after it.

items.q: What is the imperative verb?
items.a: That which denotes an action whose occurrence is being requested.

items.q: Distinguish the past, present, and imperative verbs from the following words: sāfara (سافر), yusāfiru (يسافر), sāfir (سافر), thahaba (يذهب), yath-habu (يذهب), ith-hab (اذهب).
items.a: Sāfara: past tense. Yusāfiru: present tense. Sāfir: Imperative. Thahaba: past tense. Yath-habu: present tense. Ith-hab: imperative.

items.q: Read sūrat al-Fīl (The Elephant) and extract three nouns and three particles.
items.a: Nouns: al-fīl, ṭayran (bird), ḥijārah (stone). Particles: Bāʾ in "bi-aṣ-ḥāb", fee, min.

items.q: Read sūrat al-Kawthar and extract from it three verbs.
items.a: Aʿtā (gave), is a past tense verb. Ṣalli (pray), is a command verb. Inḥar (slaughter), is also a command verb.

items.q: Into how many types is the noun divided?
items.a: Singular, dual, and plural. Example: man (rajul-رَجُلٌ), two men (rajulān-رَجُلَانِ), men (rijāl-رِجَالٌ).

items.q: What is al-mufrad (the singular form)?
items.a: That which indicates one, whether masculine (rajul-رَجُلٌ) or feminine (imraʾah-امْرَأَةٌ).

items.q: What is al-muthannā (the dual form)?
items.a: That which indicates two, whether masculine (rajulān-رَجُلَانِ) or feminine (imraʾatān-امْرَأَتَانِ).

items.q: What is al-jamʿ (plural)?
items.a: That which refers to more than two, whether masculine or feminine.

items.q: How many types of al-jamʿ are there?
items.a: Jamʿ muthakkar sālim (Sound masculine plural), such as "muslimoon" (Muslims). Jamʿ muʾannath sālim (Sound feminine plural), such as "muslimāt" (Muslim women). Jamʿ takseer (Broken plural), such as "rijāl" (men).

items.q: What are al-Asmāʾ al-Khamsa (The five nouns)?
items.a: Abooka (your father). Akhooka (your brother). Hamooka (your male in-laws). Fooka (your mouth). Thoo mālin (money owner).

items.q: What is the difference between al-mabnī (indeclinable) and al-muʿrab (inflected)?
items.a: Al-mabnī: its ending remains the same in all contexts. Al-muʿrab: its ending changes according to its position in the sentence.

items.q: What are the types of al-iʿrāb (case endings)?
items.a: Ar-rafʿ. An-naṣb. Al-khafḍ. Al-jazm.

items.q: What is the original sign for ar-rafʿ?
items.a: Aḍ-ḍammah ( ــُـ  ).

items.q: What is the original sign for an-naṣb?
items.a: Al-fathah ( ــَـ  ).

items.q: What is the original sign for al-khafḍ?
items.a: Al-kasra ( ــِـ  ).

items.q: What is the original sign for al-jazm?
items.a: Sukoon ( ــْـ  ).

items.q: Mention three of the words that are in the nominative (rafʿ).
items.a: Al-mubtadaʾ (subject). Al-khabar (predicate). Al-Fāʿil (doer/subject of verb).

items.q: Mention three types of words that have the sign of an-Nasb?
items.a: Al-mafʿool bihi (the thing the action is done to). Al-hāl (state). Ath-tharf (adverb of time/place / circumstance).

items.q: What is the ruling on sea water?
items.a: Its water is pure and its dead are permissible to eat.

items.q: When does water become impure?
items.a: If its color, smell or taste change due to impurities being mixed with it.

items.q: What is the ruling of the urine of the animals which are permissible to eat?
items.a: It is pure.

items.q: How many stones should be used when performing istijmār (dry purification)?
items.a: When performing istijmār, one should use an odd number of stones, no fewer than three — e.g., three, five, seven, and so on.

items.q: If a dog licks a bowl, what is the ruling?
items.a: The bowl is to be cleaned 7 times. First with soil, and the remaining with water.

items.q: Are cats impure?
items.a: No, rather cats live amongst you.

items.q: What should we do if a fly falls into a drink?
items.a: We dip the fly in and then remove it, as in one of its wings is an illness and in the other is the cure.

items.q: How is the skin of a dead animal purified?
items.a: By tanning with water and with qaraẓ (the pods from the Sant tree) or its likes.

items.q: What is the ruling on praying without wuḍooʾ?
items.a: The prayer is invalid.

items.q: Mention a ḥadīth on the virtue of wuḍooʾ?
items.a: Narrated from Abū Hurayrah, may Allāh be pleased with him, that the Messenger of Allāh, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, said: "If a Muslim (or believing) slave of Allāh, performs wuḍooʾ and washes his face, every sin he looked at with his eyes goes out of it with the water—or with the last drop of water. When he washes his hands, every sin that his hands committed goes out with the water—or with the last drop. When he washes his feet, every sin his feet walked toward goes out with the water—or with the last drop. Until he emerges pure from sins". Narrated by Muslim.

items.q: Summarise how to make wuḍooʾ?
items.a: One says Bismillāh, then wash his hands up to the wrist three times; Wash his mouth and inhale and exhale three times; Wash his face three times; Wash his hands up to the elbows three times; Wipe his head once, then wash his feet to the ankles three times.

items.q: Up to where will the ornaments/jewelry of the believer reach?
items.a: It extends to where wuḍooʾ reaches.

items.q: How does the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, know his ummah on the day of judgment?
items.a: They will come to him with radiant faces and bright limbs from the traces of wuḍooʾ.

items.q: What are the nullifiers of wuḍooʾ?
items.a: Anything exiting from the private parts or back passage, deep sleep, eating camel's meat, touching the private area, apostasy, losing one’s mind, and janābah.

items.q: If a disbeliever accepts Islām, does he make ghusl?
items.a: Yes.

items.q: What should one do if he does not find water or is unable to use it as it is harmful to him?
items.a: He makes tayammum with soil.

items.q: Describe how to make tayammum?
items.a: Strike the ground once with both palms, then blow to remove excess dust. Wipe the left palm over the right, and wipe the backs of the hand, then wipe the face.

items.q: What is the ruling on wiping over footwear?
items.a: It is a Sunnah if one wears them both while in the state of purity.

items.q: What is the duration of wiping over the footwear for the traveller and resident?
items.a: The traveller wipes over his footwear for three days and nights, while the resident wipes over them for the duration of one day and one night.

items.q: Who are those who deny wiping over footwear?
items.a: The Sheeʿāh.

items.q: What is the ruling of praying in clean footwear?
items.a: It is encouraged, (Mustahab).

items.q: How is the prayer called to?
items.a: By the athān.

items.q: State the words of the athān.
items.a: Allāhu Akbar (Allāh is the Greatest) [1]. Allāhu Akbar (Allāh is the Greatest) [2]. Allāhu Akbar (Allāh is the Greatest) [3]. Allāhu Akbar (Allāh is the Greatest) [4]. Ashhadu an lā ilāha illa Allāh (I bear witness that there is no god but Allāh) [5]. Ashhadu an lā ilāha illa Allāh (I bear witness that there is no god but Allāh) [6]. Ashhadu anna Muḥammadan Rasoolu Allāh (I bear witness that Muḥammad is the Messenger of Allāh) [7]. Ashhadu anna Muḥammadan Rasoolu Allāh (I bear witness that Muḥammad is the Messenger of Allāh) [8]. Ḥayya ʿalā aṣ-ṣalāh (Come to prayer) [9]. Ḥayya ʿalā aṣ-ṣalāh (Come to prayer) [10]. Ḥayya ʿalā al-falāḥ (Come to success) [11]. Ḥayya ʿalā al-falāḥ (Come to success) [12]. Allāhu Akbar (Allāh is the Greatest) [13]. Allāhu Akbar (Allāh is the Greatest) [14]. Lā ilāha illa Allāh (There is no god but Allāh) [15].

items.q: What do we add in the first athān for Ṣubḥ prayer?
items.a: Aṣ-ṣalātu khayrun mina an-nawm (the prayer is better than sleep), twice, after (Ḥayya ʿalā al-falāḥ).

items.q: What does the muʾath-thin say if there’s rain and it’s hard for people to come out to prayer?
items.a: Pray in your dwellings (ṣalloo fee riḥālikum), or, pray in your homes (ṣalloo fee buyootikum).

items.q: What is the ruling on saying “ḥayya ʿalā khayri al ʿamal” (come to the best of deeds), in athān?
items.a: It is an innovation from the innovations of the Sheeʿah.

items.q: What is the backbone of Islām?
items.a: The prayer.

items.q: What’s the ruling on abandoning prayer?
items.a: Abandoning prayer is Kufr.

items.q: When did Allāh make the prayer obligatory upon His Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: On the night of Isrāʾ.

items.q: How many prayers are obligatory?
items.a: Five prayers in every day and night.

items.q: Mention the number of raka˓āt in each prayer.
items.a: Fajr: 2 raka˓āt. Ẓuhr: 4 raka˓āt. ʿAṣr: 4 raka˓āt. Maghrib: 3 raka˓āt. ʿIshāʾ: 4 raka˓āt.

items.q: What’s the ruling on someone who prays outside the prescribed time?
items.a: Their prayer is invalid.

items.q: What is the proof that every prayer must be performed in its set time?
items.a: Allāh, Exalted is He, said: “Indeed, the prayer has been enjoined upon the believers at fixed times” [An-Nisāʾ: 103].

items.q: In which sūrah is the Statement of Allāh, Exalted is He: "Truly, We offered Al-Amānah (the trust or moral responsibility or honesty and all the duties which Allāh has ordained) to the heavens and the earth, and the mountains, but they declined to bear it and were afraid of it (i.e. afraid of Allāh's Torment). But man bore it. Verily, he was unjust (to himself) and ignorant (of its results)"?
items.a: Sūrat al-Aḥzab.

items.q: When does the time for Fajr prayer begin and end?
items.a: It begins at the break of true dawn (al-fajr aṣ-Ṣādiq) and ends at sunrise.

items.q: When does the time for Ẓuhr prayer begin and end?
items.a: It begins after the sun passes its zenith and ends when the shadow of everything equals its length.

items.q: When does the time for ʿAṣr prayer begin and end?
items.a: It begins when the shadow of everything equals its length and ends at sunset.

items.q: When does the time for Maghrib prayer begin and end?
items.a: It begins at sunset and ends when the red twilight disappears.

items.q: When does the time for ʿIshāʾ prayer begin and end?
items.a: It begins when the red twilight disappears and ends at the middle of the night.

items.q: In which sūrah is the Statement of Allāh, Exalted is He: "Guard strictly (five obligatory) Aṣ-Ṣalawāt (the prayers) especially the middle Ṣalāt (i.e. the best prayer - ʿAṣr). And stand before Allāh with obedience [and do not speak to others during the Salat (prayers)]"?
items.a: Sūrat al-Baqarah.

items.q: Which is the middle prayer?
items.a: ʿAṣr prayer.

items.q: What are the two colds referred to in the statement of the Messenger, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam: “Whoever prays the two colds enters al-Jannah”?
items.a: Fajr and ʿAṣr prayers.

items.q: What prayer if left and neglected causes one's deeds to be null and void?
items.a: ʿAṣr prayer.

items.q: What is the most difficult prayer upon the hypocrites?
items.a: ʿIshāʾ and Fajr prayers.

items.q: Which of the 5 prayers has the longest recitation?
items.a: Ṣubḥ prayer.

items.q: Which prayer is the Prayer of Ghadāt (the Early Morning Prayer)?
items.a: Ṣubḥ prayer.

items.q: Who are the people from whom the pen is lifted?
items.a: The insane, until they regain reason; the sleeping, until they awaken; and the child, until they reach puberty.

items.q: What must one if he forgets a prayer or sleeps through it?
items.a: He should perform it as soon as he remembers or wakes up.

items.q: What is the ʿawrah (nakedness) of a man?
items.a: From the navel to the knees.

items.q: What is the ʿawrah of a woman?
items.a: A woman’s entire body is ʿawrah.

items.q: Is it permissible to pray in the places where camels and sheep are kept?
items.a: It is permissible to pray where sheep are kept, but not where camels are kept.

items.q: Is it permissible to pray in graveyards and bathrooms?
items.a: No, it is impermissible.

items.q: What should the one praying place in front of him when he wants to pray?
items.a: He places a sutrah (cover) directly in front of him and prays toward it.

items.q: What should the one praying do if someone tries to pass between him and his sutrah?
items.a: He prevents him.

items.q: What is the height of the sutrah?
items.a: It is similar in height to what is placed on the riding animal, which is approximately 2/3rds of an arm’s length.

items.q: What are the things that break prayer?
items.a: A grown woman, a donkey, and a black dog.

items.q: In which direction does the one praying face?
items.a: In the direction of the Kaʿbah. Allāh, Exalted is He, said: "So turn your face in the direction of Al-Masjid al-Ḥarām (at Makkah). And wheresoever you people are, turn your faces (in prayer) in that direction". [Al-Baqarah: 134].

items.q: Who is the narrator of the ḥadīth: “Pray as you saw me pray”?
items.a: Al-Bukhārī narrated it from Mālik bnu al-Ḥuwayrith, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: What are the conditions of prayer?
items.a: There are 9 conditions of prayer, and they are: 1- Islām. 2- Sanity. 3- Discernment. 4- Removal of ḥadath (ritual impurity). 5- Removal of physical impurities. 5- Covering that ʿawrah. 6- The entrance of the prescribed time. 7- Facing the Qiblah. 9- The niyyah (intention).

items.q: What are the pillars of the prayer?
items.a: 1- Standing if able to. 2- Takbeerat (saying Allāhu Akbar) al-iḥrām (the beginning of prayer). 3- Reciting sūrat al-Fātiḥah. 4- Bowing (Rukooʿ). 5- Rising form the position of bowing. 6- Standing upright after bowing. 7- Prostrating (sujood) on seven body parts. 8- Raising from prostration. 9- Sitting between the two prostrations. 10- Tranquility in all actions. 11- The last tashahhud. 12- Sitting for it. 13- Ṣalāt upon the Prophet. 14- The two tasleems: (saying assalāmu ʿalaykum).

items.q: What are the obligations of Prayer?
items.a: 1- All takbeers other than the opening takbeer. 2- Saying “Samiʿa Allāhu liman ḥamidah” for the imām and the one praying alone. 3- Saying “Rabbanā wa laka al-ḥamd” for everyone. 4- Saying “Ṣubḥāna Rabbiya al-ʿAẓeem” in rukooʿ. 5- Saying “Ṣubḥāna Rabbiya al-Aʿlā” in sujood. 6- Saying “Rabbi ghfir lee” between the two prostrations. 7- The first tashahhud. 8- Sitting for it.

items.q: What invalidates the prayer?
items.a: Deliberate speech while remembering and knowing, laughing, eating, drinking, exposure of the ʿawrah, excessive turning away from the direction of the qiblah, excessive continuous movement during the prayer, and the nullification of purification.

items.q: Is having a niyyah (intention) obligatory for prayer?
items.a: Yes, every action must have a niyyah.

items.q: What was the response of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, to that who asked him about how to pray on a ship?
items.a: He said: "Pray standing except if you fear drowning".

items.q: How does a sick person pray?
items.a: He prays standing, and if he is unable to, then sitting, and if he is unable to, then lying on his side.

items.q: What is the Key to prayer?
items.a: Aṭ-Ṭahoor (Purification).

items.q: What is its taḥreem (cause of starting it)?
items.a: At-takbeer (saying Allāhu Akbar).

items.q: What is its taḥleel (cause of ending it)?
items.a: At-tasleem (saying assalāmu ʿalaykum).

items.q: What should the one praying do when he says “Allāhu Akbar”?
items.a: He raises both his hands up to the level of his shoulders.

items.q: Where should the one praying place his hands?
items.a: He places his right hand over his left hand on the chest, or below it (there's flexibility in the matter).

items.q: What does one say after the opening takbeer?
items.a: They recite Duʿāʾ al-Istiftāḥ.

items.q: Mention Duʿāʾ al-Istiftāḥ.
items.a: Allāhumma bāʿid baynee wa bayna khaṭāyāya (O Allāh, distance me from my sins) kamā bāʿadta bayna al-mashriqi wa al-maghrib (as You have distanced the East from the West). Allāhumma naqqinee mina al-khaṭāya (O Allāh, cleanse me of sins) kamā yunaqqā ath-thawbu al-abyaḍu mina ad-danas (as a white garment is cleansed of dirt). Allāhumma ghsil khaṭāyāya (O Allāh, wash away my sins) bil-māʿi wa th-thalji wa al-barad (with water, snow, and hail).

items.q: What do you recite after the opening supplication?
items.a: Al-Istiʿātha: Aʿoothu billāhi mina ash-shayṭāni ar-rajeem (I seek refuge in Allāh from the accursed Devil).

items.q: What do you recite after al-Istiʿāthah?
items.a: The Basmala: Bismi Allāhi ar-Raḥmāni ar-Raḥeem (In the Name of Allāh, the All-Merciful, the Especially Merciful).

items.q: Are the seeking refuge and Basmala said silently or aloud?
items.a: Silently.

items.q: What do you recite after the Basmala?
items.a: Al-Fātiḥah, and whatever you are able to recite from the Qurʾān.

items.q: What do you say after al-Fātiḥah?
items.a: Āmeen.

items.q: What is the reward for saying (Āmeen) after al-Fātiḥah in prayer?
items.a: If one’s saying of Āmeen coincides with that of the angels, all past sins are forgiven.

items.q: What do the Jews envy the most from us [Muslims]?
items.a: The statement Āmeen and the salām.

items.q: What is the ruling on one who does not recite al-Fātiḥah?
items.a: His prayer is null and void.

items.q: In which suwar did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, perform Prostration of Recitation?
items.a: Sāḍ, an-Najm, al-Inshiqāq, al-ʿAlaq.

items.q: What is the ruling of looking upwards whilst praying?
items.a: It is not permissible.

items.q: What is feared for the one who does this [look up] while praying?
items.a: It is feared that his sight will be snatched.

items.q: Mention what is said during rukooʿ and during raising from it.
items.a: In rukooʿ: 1- Ṣubḥāna Rabbi-ya al-ʿAẓeem (Exalted is my Lord, the Great), three times. 2- Subbooḥun Quddoos, Rabbu al-Malāʾikati wa ar-Rooḥ (Supreme in Exaltation and Supreme in Holiness, Lord of the angels and the Spirit). 3- Ṣubḥānaka Allāhumma wa bi Ḥamdik, Allāhumma ghfir lee (Exalted You are, O Allāh, and praise is Yours. O Allāh, forgive me). During raising from rukooʿ: 1- Samiʿa Allāhu li man ḥamidah, Rabbanā wa laka al-ḥamd (Allāh hears those who praise Him; O our Lord, to you is all praise).

items.q: What are the body-parts involved in sujood?
items.a: Seven: (1-2) the hands, (3-4) the knees, (5) the forehead and nose, (6-7) and the tips of the feet.

items.q: What is feared for the one who bows and prostrates before the imām?
items.a: It is feared that he will be transformed into a donkey, or his head will transform into that of a donkey.

items.q: When is the believer closest to His Lord?
items.a: During sujood.

items.q: State some of what is said during sujood.
items.a: 1- Ṣubḥāna Rabbiya al-Aʿlā (Exalted He is, my Lord, the Most High), three times. 2- Allāhumma ghfir lee thanbee kullah, diqqahu wa jillah, wa awwalahu wa ākhirahu, wa ʿalāniyatahu wa sirrahu (O Allāh, forgive me all my sin, its minor and major, its first and last, its open and hidden). 3- Allāhumma yā muqalliba al-quloob, thabbit qalbee ʿalā deenik (O Allāh, Turner of hearts, keep my heart firm upon Your religion).

items.q: What do we say between the two prostrations?
items.a: We say: Rabbi ghfir lee (O my Lord, forgive me) (twice).

items.q: What are the animal-like postures we were forbidden from imitating in prayer?
items.a: Turning like the turning of a fox, sitting like the sitting of a dog, pecking like the pecking of a rooster, and spreading the forearms like the spreading of a predatory beast.

items.q: Where does one praying place his hands whilst reciting tashahhud and with which finger does he point?
items.a: He places them on his knees, and he points with the index finger of his right hand, and he joins his thumb with the middle finger.

items.q: Mention the tashahhud.
items.a: At-taḥiyyātu lillāh, wa aṣ-ṣalawātu, wa aṭ-ṭayyibātu (All compliments, prayers, and good things are due to Allāh). As-salāmu ʿalayka ayyuha an-nabiyyu wa raḥmatu-llāhi wa barakātuh (peace be upon you, O Prophet, and the mercy of Allāh and His blessings). As-salāmu ʿalaynā wa ʿalā ʿibādillāhi aṣ-ṣāliheen (peace be upon us and upon the righteous slaves of Allāh). Ash-hadu an lā ilāha illa-llāh (I bear witness that there is no god but Allāh), wa ash-hadu anna Muḥammadan ʿAbduhu wa rasooluh (and I bear witness that Muḥammad is His slave and Messenger).

items.q: Mention aṣ-ṣalāt al-Ibrāheemiyyah.
items.a: Allāhumma ṣalli ʿalā Muḥammadin wa ʿalā āli Muḥammadin (O Allāh, bestow your blessings upon Muḥammad and the family of Muḥammad), kamā ṣallayta ʿalā Ibrāheema wa ʿalā āli Ibrāheema (as You bestowed blessings upon Ibrāheem and the family of Ibrāheem), innaka Ḥameedun Majeed (you are indeed All-Praiseworthy, All-Glorious). Allāhumma bārik ʿalā Muḥammadin wa ʿalā āli Muḥammadin (O Allāh, bless Muḥammad and the family of Muḥammad), kamā bārakta ʿalā Ibrāheema wa ʿalā āli Ibrāheema (as You blessed Ibrāheem and the family of Ibrāheem), innaka Ḥameedun Majeed (you are indeed All-Praiseworthy, All-Glorious).

items.q: What should one state after reciting the Abarahamic prayer?
items.a: They seek refuge in Allāh from four things: from the torment of the grave, and the torment of Hell, and the tribulations of life and death, and the tribulations of al-Maseeḥ ad-Dajjāl.

items.q: What is the ruling of tranquility in prayer?
items.a: It is a pillar of prayer.

items.q: What's the ruling on that who laughs in prayer?
items.a: Their prayer is invalid.

items.q: What is the name of the devil who does waswasah (evil whispering) when one is praying?
items.a: Khinzab.

items.q: What should a Muslim do if he has a lot of waswasah while praying?
items.a: He lets out a light tongue-puff to his left, three times, and says: Aʿoothu billāhi mina ash-shayṭāni ar-rajeem (I seeks refuge with Allāh from the accursed Shayṭān).

items.q: What should one do if he lapses during prayer?
items.a: Perform Sujood as-Sahw (Prostration of Lapse).

items.q: What should the follower (maʾmūm) do if the imām lapses?
items.a: Say: Ṣubḥānallāh.

items.q: What should women do if the imām lapses?
items.a: Clap, because tasbeeḥ is for men and clapping is for women.

items.q: What are the two black animals the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, commanded to be killed [even] while praying?
items.a: Snakes and scorpions.

items.q: What should one do if he needs to spit whilst praying?
items.a: He should not spit in front of him, nor on his right, rather, he spits on his left in a napkin or on his garment.

items.q: What should one say after completing his prayer?
items.a: He says: “Astaghfirullāh” (I seek Allāh’s forgiveness) (three times). “Allāhumma anta as-Salām, wa minka as-Salām, tabārakta yā thā al-Jalāli wa al-Ikrām” (O Allāh, You are Peace and from You is peace. Blessed are You, O Possessor of majesty and honour). “Ṣubḥānallāh” (Glory be to Allāh) (33 times). “Al-ḥamdu lillāh” (All praise belongs to Allāh) (33 times). “Allāhu akbar” (Allāh is the Greatest) (33 times). Then, completing one hundred, he says: “Lā ilāha illa Allāh waḥdahu lā sharīka lah, lahu al-Mulku wa lahu al-ḥamd, wa huwa ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīr” (There is no god except Allāh alone, without partner. To Him belongs all Sovereignty and praise, and He is over all things capable). “Allāhumma aʿinnī ʿalā thikrika, wa shukrika, wa ḥusni ʿibādatik” (O Allāh, help me to remember You, to thank You, and to worship You in the best manner). He [then] recites Āyat al-Kursī.

items.q: How many regular Sunnah prayers (rawātib) are there?
items.a: Two raka˓āt before Ṣubḥ prayer, four before Ẓuhr and two raka˓āt after, two raka˓āt after Maghrib, two raka˓āt after ˓Ishāʾ.

items.q: What is the reward for regularly performing rawātib?
items.a: A house is built in al-Jannah for those who do.

items.q: Which is better in voluntary prayers: to be prayed in the masjid or at home?
items.a: At home.

items.q: Where are the obligatory prayers prayed?
items.a: They are to be prayed in congregation in the masjid.

items.q: What is the best prayer after the obligatory prayers?
items.a: The voluntary night prayer.

items.q: Which prayer did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, encourage praying and informed that Allāh loves it?
items.a: al-Witr prayer.

items.q: What is the best time to pray al-Witr?
items.a: The last third of the night.

items.q: What is the Witr of the day?
items.a: Maghrib prayer.

items.q: In which prayers do the successive angels meet each other?
items.a: Fajr and ʿAṣr prayers.

items.q: Is it permissible to pray al-Witr in the beginning of the night?
items.a: Yes, it is permissible to pray al-Witr after the ʿIshāʾ prayer, in the beginning of the night, the middle, and the last portion of the night.

items.q: What 2 rakaʿāt are better than this world and all that it contains?
items.a: The Ṣubḥ Sunnah.

items.q: How many rakaʿāt is the Ḍuḥā prayer?
items.a: At least two, and one may add whatever Allāh wills.

items.q: By how many degrees is praying in congregation superior to praying by oneself?
items.a: By 27 degrees.

items.q: Which row of men is the best?
items.a: The front row.

items.q: Which women row is the best?
items.a: The last row.

items.q: What is the reward for the one who fills a gap in a row?
items.a: Whoever fills a gap in a row, Allāh will be generous to them.

items.q: Who leads the prayer?
items.a: The one who is most proficient in the Book of Allāh.

items.q: Is it permissible for a woman to lead men in prayer?
items.a: It is not permissible.

items.q: What should one say if he hears a man announcing a lost item in the masjid?
items.a: We say: “May Allāh not return it to you”.

items.q: Where should the one who has eaten garlic, onion, or leek, pray?
items.a: He should pray in his home and does not come close to the masjid.

items.q: Is it permissible to take a book or Muṣ-ḥaf from the masjid?
items.a: It is not permissible; because it is endowed for the masjid.

items.q: Is buying and selling permitted in the masjid?
items.a: No, verily the masjids were built for the remembrance of Allāh.

items.q: Is it permitted to raise one's voice in the masjid for other than the remembrance of Allāh?
items.a: No, it is not permissible.

items.q: How should we pray during travel?
items.a: The prayers that are 4 rakaʿāt (Ẓuhr, ʿAṣr and ʿIshāʾ) are shortened to 2 rakaʿāt, Maghrib (3 rakaʿāt) and Fajr (2 rakaʿāt) remain the same.

items.q: Is it permissible to pray in masjids which contain graves?
items.a: It is not permissible.

items.q: Mention the discouraged times of prayer.
items.a: Severely discouraged: at sunrise until it rises fully, when the sun is at its zenith until it declines, and at sunset until it sets. Lightly discouraged: after Fajr until sunrise, and after ʿAṣr until sunset.

items.q: What is the ruling on one who neglectfully abandons three Jumuʿahs?
items.a: A seal is placed upon his heart.

items.q: Upon whom is Jumuʿah not obligatory?
items.a: The slave, the sick, the traveler, the child, and the woman.

items.q: Is buying and selling permissible on Friday after the athān of Jumuʿah?
items.a: It is not permissible.

items.q: How many khuṭbahs does Jumuʿah have?
items.a: Two khuṭbahs, separated by a sitting.

items.q: What is Khuṭbat al-Ḥājah?
items.a: It is the khuṭbah which the righteous predecessors used to precede their lessons, their books, and their various affairs. Its words are: “Inna al-ḥamda lillāh, naḥmaduhu wa nastaʿīnuhu wa nastaghfiruh, wa naʿūthu billāhi min shurūri anfusinā wa min sayyiʾāti aʿmālinā, man yahdihi Allāhu fa-lā muḍilla lah, wa man yuḍlil fa-lā hādiya lah, wa ashhadu an lā ilāha illā Allāh, wa ashhadu anna Muḥammadan ʿabduhu wa rasūluh” (All praise is for Allāh. We praise Him, seek His help, and seek His forgiveness. We seek refuge with Allāh from the evils of ourselves and from the evils of our deeds. Whomever Allāh guides, none can misguide; and whomever He misguides, none can guide. And I bear witness that no god except Allāh, and I bear witness that Muḥammad is His slave and Messenger). “Yā ayyuhā allathīna āmanū, ittaqū Allāha ḥaqqa tuqātih, wa lā tamūtunna illā wa antum muslimūn” (O you who believe, fear Allāh as He truly deserves to be feared, and do not die except as Muslims). “Yā ayyuhā an-nāsu, ittaqū rabbakumu allathī khalaqakum min nafsin wāḥidah, wa khalaqa minhā zawjahā, wa bath-tha minhumā rijālan kathīran wa nisāʾā, wa ittaqū Allāha allathī tasāʾalūna bihī wa al-arḥām, inna Allāha kāna ʿalaykum Raqībā" (O mankind, fear your Lord, who created you from a single soul and created from it its mate, and spread from them many men and women. And fear Allāh through whom you ask one another, and the wombs. Indeed, Allāh is ever Watchful over you). “Yā ayyuhā allathīna āmanū, ittaqū Allāha wa qūlū qawlan sadīdā, yuṣliḥ lakum aʿmālakum wa yaghfir lakum thunūbakum, wa man yuṭiʿ Allāha wa rasūlahu fa-qad fāza fawzan ʿaẓīmā” (O you who believe, fear Allāh and speak upright words. He will [then] ammend your deeds and forgive your sins. Whoever obeys Allāh and His Messenger has certainly attained a great success).

items.q: What did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, liken a khuṭbah without tashahhud to?
items.a: To a severed hand.

items.q: Is returning salām permissible during the Jumuʿah khuṭbah?
items.a: It is not permissible. Whoever speaks has engaged in idle speech, and whoever engages in idle speech has no Jumuʿah.

items.q: Is Taḥiyyat al-Masjid prayed while the Imām is delivering the Jumuʿah khuṭbah?
items.a: Yes. He prays two rakʿahs and makes them brief.

items.q: What is the ruling on one who speaks while the Imām is delivering the Jumuʿah khuṭbah?
items.a: He has no Jumuʿah.

items.q: What suwar should be recited for Jumuʿah prayer after sūrat al-Fātiḥah?
items.a: In the 1st rakʿah, sūrat al-Aʿlā, and in the 2nd, sūrat al-Ghāshiyah.

items.q: Is there an athān and an iqāmah for the ʿEid Prayer?
items.a: No.

items.q: Should the minbar (pulpit) be present for the ʿEid prayer?
items.a: No.

items.q: Should women attend the ʿEid prayer?
items.a: Yes, all women should attend, even the menstruating women and those young unmarried, virgins should attend.

items.q: What is the name of the prayer which is prayed when an eclipse occurs between the sun and moon?
items.a: Kusoof (eclipse) prayer.

items.q: What should we do if the land is afflicted with drought?
items.a: We pray the Istisqāʿ prayer (the prayer for seeking rain).

items.q: In which sūrah is the Statement of Allāh: "If they (non-Muslims) had believed in Allāh, and went on the Right Way (i.e. Islām) We should surely have bestowed on them water (rain) in abundance"?
items.a: Sūrat al-Jinn.

items.q: Should the minbar (pulpit) be brought out for the prayer for seeking rain?
items.a: Yes.

items.q: What is the best clothing?
items.a: White clothing.

items.q: What is the Sunnah regarding walking with the funeral?
items.a: Hastening with it.

items.q: Describe the method of Janāzah (funeral) prayer.
items.a: Four takbīrs. After the first, he recites Sūrat al-Fātiḥah. After the second, aṣ-Ṣalāt al-Ibrāhīmiyyah. After the third, he supplicates for the deceased. After the fourth, the taslīm.

items.q: What is the reward for praying Janāzah prayer?
items.a: Whoever performs it will be rewarded a Qeerāṭ.

items.q: What is the reward for following the funeral?
items.a: Whoever follows it will be rewarded a Qeerāṭ.

items.q: How much is a Qeerāṭ?
items.a: It is the same as the mountain of Uḥud.

items.q: What is said when the deceased is placed in the grave?
items.a: Bismillāh, wa ʿalā millati rasooli Allāh (In the Name of Allāh, upon the way of the Messenger).

items.q: What is the Sunnah after the deceased has been buried?
items.a: To say: Allāhumma ghfir lahu wa thabbit-hu (O Allāh forgive him, and make him steadfast).

items.q: What is the ruling regarding reciting sūrat Yāseen upon the deceased?
items.a: It is a bidʿah (innovation). There is no proof about that, and the narration which says: “Recite Yāseen upon your deceased”, is weak.

items.q: What is the ruling on reciting sūrat al-Fātiḥah upon the soul of the deceased?
items.a: An innovation.

items.q: Is it permissible to weep for the deceased?
items.a: Yes; but wailing is prohibited.

items.q: Is it permissible to plaster graves or write on them?
items.a: No , it is prohibited.

items.q: Is it permissible to dig up the graves of the disbelievers?
items.a: Yes, for necessities.

items.q: How should you console the family of the deceased?
items.a: By saying: "Inna lillāhi mā akhatha, wa lahu mā aʿṭā, wa kullun ʿindahu bi-ajalin musammā" (Whatever Allāh takes or gives belongs to Him, and everything with Him has a fixed limited time).

items.q: Is there any virtue in offering condolences?
items.a: The Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, said: "There is no believer who offers condolences to his brother for a calamity except that Allāh, Ṣubḥānahu, will clothe him in garments of honor on the Day of Resurrection".

items.q: Mention the recipients of zakāt?
items.a: Allāh mentioned them in His Book: "The alms are only for the poor, the needy, those employed to collect [zakāh], those whose hearts are to be reconciled, for freeing captives, for those in debt, in the cause of Allāh, and for the wayfarer – an obligation from Allāh. And Allāh is All-Knowing, All-Wise" [At-Tawbah: 60]

items.q: Who is the true miskīn (poor person)?
items.a: He is the one who does not find what suffices him, and he is not noticed so that charity is given to him, nor does he ask people.

items.q: Is zakāt permitted for the family of the Messenger, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: No, it is not permissible.

items.q: Who among the family of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, are not allowed to receive zakāt?
items.a: They are Banū Hāshim and Banū al-Muṭṭalib. Banū Hāshim include: the family of ʿAlī, the family of ʿAqīl, the family of Jaʿfar, the family of al-ʿAbbās, and the family of al-Ḥārith.

items.q: What did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, say to al-Ḥasan, may Allāh be pleased with him, when he ate a date that was from ṣadaqah?
