items.a: He said to him, "Kikh! Kikh! Throw it away. Do you not know that we do not eat from ṣadaqah?".

items.q: What are the animals on which zakāt is due?
items.a: Camels, cattle, and sheep/goats, including rams and female goats.

items.q: Is zakāt obligatory on gold and silver?
items.a: Yes.

items.q: What is the niṣāb (minimum threshold) for gold and silver?
items.a: Gold: 20 Deenārs, which is equivalent to 85 grams. Silver: 200 Dirhams, which is equivalent to 595 grams.

items.q: What is the amount of zakāt for gold and silver?
items.a: Fourth of a tenth (4/10).

items.q: Which items have a condition that it should remain for a year before paying zakāt on it?
items.a: livestock, gold, and silver.

items.q: Name the crops upon which zakāt is obligatory?
items.a: There are 4 types: Dates, raisins, wheat, barley.

items.q: What is the minimum weight for crops and fruits for it to be eligible for zakāt?
items.a: Five awsuq (wasqs).

items.q: How much is a wasq?
items.a: Each wasq is 60 Sāʿ, every Sāʿ is 4 amdād (mudds).

items.q: How much zakāt is due on crops and fruits?
items.a: What is watered from rain or from springs, or free of cost, is a tenth (1/10). What is watered for a cost, or by difficult means is half a tenth.

items.q: Who will have a bald-headed poisonous male snake with two black spots over its eyes wrap around him and remain attached?
items.a: It is the one who refuses to pay zakāt.

items.q: What is the name of the zakāt which is distributed at the end of Ramaḍān?
items.a: It is zakāt al-Fiṭr.

items.q: What is the amount of zakāt al-Fiṭr, and when is its time?
items.a: A ṣāʿ of wheat, barley, raisins, dates, dried curd, or other food, on behalf of every adult and child, male and female, free and slave. It is to be given before the ʿEid prayer.

items.q: What is the ruling of the fast of Ramaḍān?
items.a: It is a pillar from the pillars of Islām.

items.q: In which sūrah is the following Statement of Allāh, Exalted is He, found: "O you who believe! Observing the fasting is prescribed for you as it was prescribed for those before you, that you may become muttaqūn (pious)"?
items.a: Sūrat al-Baqarah.

items.q: How many Ramaḍāns did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, fast?
items.a: Nine.

items.q: About which deed did Allāh say: "It is specifically for me, and I [am the one to] reward for it"?
items.a: Fasting.

items.q: What are the two joys of a fasting person?
items.a: The joy at the time of breaking the fast, and the joy when he meets his Lord.

items.q: What is the name of the door from the doors of al-Jannah that no one will enter except those who fast?
items.a: The door called ar-Rayyān.

items.q: What is the cold gain?
items.a: Fasting in winter.

items.q: When do we fast Ramaḍān?
items.a: When the crescent of Ramaḍān appears.

items.q: What should we say upon sighting the crescent?
items.a: "Allāhumma ahillahu ʿalaynā bi al-yumni wa al-īmān, wa as-salāmah wa al-islām, rabbī wa rabbuka Allāh" (O Allāh, let it appear upon us with blessings, faith, safety, and Islām. My Lord and your Lord is Allāh).

items.q: What should be done if the crescent is not sighted due to cloudy weather?
items.a: Complete 30 days of the month of Shaʿbān.

items.q: What is the ruling for someone who forgets and eats or drinks while fasting?
items.a: He completes his fast with no blame on him.

items.q: When does the fasting person break his fast?
items.a: When night falls from the east, the day departs toward the west, and the sun sets.

items.q: What is the Sunnah concerning fuṭoor (breaking the fast)?
items.a: The Sunnah is to hasten breaking the fast at sunset.

items.q: What is the Sunnah concerning suḥoor (pre-dawn meal)?
items.a: To delay it.

items.q: What days are prohibited to fast?
items.a: The two ʿEids, the Day of Doubt, the days of Tashreeq except for those performing Ḥajj and did not bring their sacrificial animal, and singling out Fridays for fasting.

items.q: What is required for someone who dies leaving missed fasts?
items.a: His guardian must fast on his behalf.

items.q: What are the three white days?
items.a: The 13th, 14th, and 15th of each month.

items.q: When did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, perform Iʿtikāf?
items.a: The last ten days of Ramaḍān.

items.q: What is the greatest night of the year?
items.a: The Night of al-Qadr in Ramaḍān.

items.q: When is the Night of al-Qadr most likely?
items.a: It is most likely to occur on the odd nights of the last ten nights of Ramaḍān.

items.q: What is the virtue of the night of al-Qadr and spending it in prayer?
items.a: It is better than a thousand months. Whoever spends it in prayer out of faith and seeking reward will be forgiven their past sins.

items.q: What should one do who breaks the fast for a valid excuse in Ramaḍān?
items.a: Make it up on other days.

items.q: Who are those with valid excuses that allow them to break the fast?
items.a: The traveler, the sick, the pregnant and the nursing woman if they fear harm to themselves or their babies.

items.q: How many times is it obligatory to make ḥajj in one’s lifetime?
items.a: Once.

items.q: When did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, make ḥajj?
items.a: In the 10th year of hijrah.

items.q: How many times did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, make ḥajj and ʿumrah after his hijrah?
items.a: He made ḥajj once and ʿumrah four times.

items.q: Mention three things that erase the sins that came before them.
items.a: Islām, hijrah, and ḥajj.

items.q: Mention the designated places (miqāts) for ḥajj.
items.a: For the people of Madeenah: Thu al-Ḥulayfah. For the people of ash-Shām: Al-Juḥfah. For the people of Najd: Qarn al-Manāzil. For the people of Yemen: Yalamlam. For the people of ʿIrāq: Thāt ʿIrq.

items.q: Does the Black Stone bring harm or benefit? And is there a reward in touching it?
items.a: It does not cause harm nor benefit, but it bears witness on the Day of Resurrection for those who touched it upon truth.

items.q: Mention a thing that is on earth and it is from al-Jannah?
items.a: The Black Stone.

items.q: What is al-Mashʿar al-Ḥarām and al-Mashʿar al-Ḥalāl?
items.a: Al-Mashʿar al-Ḥarām is Muzdalifah, and al-Mashʿar al-Ḥalāl is ʿArafah.

items.q: For how many people does a cow or camel suffice in sacrifice?
items.a: It suffices for seven.

items.q: How is a camel slaughtered?
items.a: While tied and standing.

items.q: What is at-Talbiyah?
items.a: Saying: "Labbayka Allāhumma labbayk, labbayka lā sharīka laka labbayk, inna al-ḥamda wa an-niʿmata laka wa al-Mulk, lā sharīka laka" (Here I am O Allāh, here I am, You have no partner, here I am. All praise is for You, and every bounty is from You, and all Dominion is Yours – You have no partner).

items.q: What is the ruling on hunting for the muḥrim (i.e., the one in iḥrām, whether for ḥajj or ʿumrah)?
items.a: Hunting land game is prohibited for him, while hunting sea game is permitted by consensus.

items.q: From where does ṭawāf (circumambulation) of the House begin?
items.a: Starting from the Black Stone or alongside it.

items.q: If one is unable to kiss the Black stone during his ṭawāf?
items.a: He touches it with his hand or a stick and then kisses it, or he points at it from a distance and does not kiss his hand in that case.

items.q: How many corners does the Kaʿbah have?
items.a: Four corners: the Yemeni Corner, the corner containing the Black Stone, and the two Shāmī corners.

items.q: What does one say while performing ṭawāf?
items.a: He should stay in the remembrance of Allāh, recite Qurʾān and make duʿāʾ.

items.q: What is al-iḍibāʿ?
items.a: To place the middle of the upper garment under the right shoulder and throw its ends over the left shoulder, leaving the right shoulder exposed. This is done during the ṭawāf of arrival.

items.q: What does he do after ṭawāf?
items.a: He prays two rakaʿāt: in the first he recites al-Fātiḥah and Qul yā ayyuha al-kāfiroon; in the second al-Fātiḥah and Qul huwa Allāhu Aḥad.

items.q: How many circuits is ṭawāf?
items.a: Seven circuits.

items.q: How many times should one go back and forth between aṣ-Ṣafā and Marwah?
items.a: Seven times.

items.q: From where does one begin [when going back and forth between aṣ-Ṣafā and Marwah]?
items.a: From aṣ-Ṣafā.

items.q: Is it better to shave or trim the hair?
items.a: It is better to shave.

items.q: How many types of Ḥajj are there, and which is best?
items.a: Three: Tamattuʿ, Qirān, and Ifrād. Tamattuʿ is best.

items.q: What is the 8th day of Thu al-Ḥijjah called?
items.a: It is called the day of at-Tarwiyah.

items.q: Where do the pilgrims go on that day?
items.a: To Minā, where they pray Ẓuhr, ʿAṣr, Maghrib, ʿIshāʾ, and Fajr, shortening the prayers without combining them.

items.q: What is the 9th day of Thul Ḥijjah called?
items.a: It is called the day of ʿArafah.

items.q: Where do the pilgrims go on that day?
items.a: To ʿArafah, where they combine Ẓuhr and ʿAṣr with jamʿ taqdeem (early combining).

items.q: Does the Day of ʿArafah have any virtue?
items.a: Yes. It is the day on which Allāh frees more [of his] slaves from the Fire than on any other day.

items.q: Is there any virtue in duʿāʾ on the Day of ʿArafah?
items.a: It is the best duʿāʾ.

items.q: What is the ruling on standing at ʿArafah?
items.a: It is a pillar of ḥajj.

items.q: Where do the pilgrims go after sunset on the Day of ʿArafah?
items.a: To Muzdalifah, where they pray Maghrib, ʿIshāʾ, and Fajr.

items.q: Does Muzdalifah have another name?
items.a: Yes. It is called Jamʿ, and al-Mashʿar al-Ḥarām.

items.q: How many Jamarāt are there?
items.a: Three: the small, the middle, and Jamrat al-ʿAqabah (the largest).

items.q: With how many pebbles are the Jamarāt stoned?
items.a: With seven pebbles.

items.q: Which Jamrah is stoned on the Day of an-Naḥr (Slaughtering)?
items.a: Jamrat al-ʿAqabah, and it is stoned in the forenoon.

items.q: Which Jamarāt are stoned during the Days of Tashreeq?
items.a: All three are stoned; each day after the sun passes its zenith.

items.q: Where do the pilgrims spend the night on the Days of Tashreeq?
items.a: At Minā.

items.q: Mention some types of ṭawāf in ḥajj.
items.a: Ṭawāf al-Qudoom (Arrival). Ṭawāf al-Ifāḍah (after returning from ʿArafāt). Ṭawāf al-Wadāʿ (Farewell).

items.q: What is a ʿumrah in Ramaḍān equivalent to?
items.a: A ḥajj with the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam.

items.q: Where does the pilgrim slaughter his sacrificial animal?
items.a: At Minā.

items.q: What actions do the pilgrims perform on the day of Naḥr (The Day of Slaughtering)?
items.a: Stoning Jamrat al-ʿAqabah, then slaughtering, then shaving or trimming, then performing Ṭawāf al-Ifāḍah.

items.q: What is the Prophet's ḥajj called?
items.a: It is called Ḥajjat al-Wadāʿ (Farewell Ḥajj).

items.q: What are the pillars of ʿumrah?
items.a: Iḥrām, ṭawāf of the House, and going back and forth between aṣ-Ṣafā and Marwah.

items.q: How many ʿumrahs did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam perform?
items.a: Four. All of them in Thu al-Qiʿdah, except the one he, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, performed along with his ḥajj.

items.q: What is the ruling for the one who changes his Islām to another religion?
items.a: He is a disbelieving apostate, and his prescribed punishment is killing, due to the statement of the Messenger of Allāh, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam: “Whoever changes his religion, kill him”. Narrated by al-Bukhārī.

items.q: Is a woman’s consent a condition for her marriage?
items.a: Yes.

items.q: What is the prescribed punishment for the muḥṣan adulterer?
items.a: He is to be stoned to death.

items.q: What is the prescribed punishment for the non-muḥṣan adulterer?
items.a: He is whipped one hundred times and exiled for a year.

items.q: What is the punishment for the thief?
items.a: His hand is cut off.

items.q: What is the prescribed punishment for one who drinks alcohol?
items.a: He is to be whipped 40 times.

items.q: Is it permissible to eat locusts?
items.a: Yes. The Companions ate them during some expeditions.

items.q: Is it permissible to eat hyena meat?
items.a: Yes, due to the ḥadīth: “The hyena is game”.

items.q: Is it permissible to eat the slaughter of one who does not mention Allāh’s name?
items.a: No, because of the Statement of Allāh: "And do not eat of that upon which the name of Allāh has not been mentioned, for indeed it is disobedience" [al-Anʿām: 121].

items.q: What is the ruling on eating wild donkey?
items.a: It is permissible.

items.q: What is the ruling on eating a domestic donkey?
items.a: It is prohibited.

items.q: What is al-Jallālah?
items.a: An animal that eats filth and impurities.

items.q: What is the ruling in eating such animals?
items.a: It is ḥarām. However, it could be confined for a period until its flesh becomes pure, then it may be eaten.

items.q: When is the ʿaqeeqah slaughtered?
items.a: On the seventh day after birth.

items.q: What is al-Kalālah?
items.a: One who dies leaving no child and no parent to inherit him.

items.q: How long is the obligatory hospitality?
items.a: One day and one night, and the encouraged amount is three days.

items.q: How will the wound of the person who is wounded for the sake of Allāh be on the day of Resurrection?
items.a: His wound will bleed the color of blood and its scent will be that of musk.

items.q: What does breastfeeding prohibit?
items.a: It prohibits that which the lineage prohibits.

items.q: How many breastfeedings establish prohibition?
items.a: Five breastfeedings.

items.q: What is the complete duration of breastfeeding?
items.a: Two complete years.

items.q: What is the ʿiddah (waiting period) of a divorced woman who is not pregnant?
items.a: Three menstrual cycles; and if she is post-menopausal or young, then three months.

items.q: What is the waiting period of a woman whose husband has died and she is not pregnant?
items.a: Four months and ten days.

items.q: What is the waiting period of a pregnant woman?
items.a: Until she delivers her pregnancy.

items.q: What was the color of the turban of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, when entering Makkah?
items.a: Black.

items.q: What is the ruling of wearing silk and gold?
items.a: It is permissible for females and prohibited for males.

items.q: What is al-barakah?
items.a: It is the continuity of divine goodness in a thing.

items.q: How many years is a century?
items.a: One hundred years.

items.q: Are the evil things equal to the good things?
items.a: They are not equal.

items.q: What is the Umm al-Khabāʾith (Mother of Evils)?
items.a: Al-khamr (Intoxicants).

items.q: What is the āyah which prohibited al-khamr in totality?
items.a: Allāh, Exalted is He, said: "O you who believe! Intoxicants, gambling, and arrows for seeking luck or decision are an abomination of Shayṭān's handiwork. So avoid that in order that you may be successful" [Al-Māʾidah: 90].

items.q: Who is the true raqoob?
items.a: A person whose children have not died before him.

items.q: What is Umm Mildam?
items.a: Fever; a heat that occurs between the skin and the flesh.

items.q: Who is considered illiterate?
items.a: The one who does not read or write that which is written.

items.q: What is the weapon of a believer?
items.a: Duʿāʾ.

items.q: What is the meaning of "Āmeen"?
items.a: O Allāh, answer.

items.q: What are the titles of the kings of Persia, Abyssinia, Rome, Egypt, and Yemen?
items.a: Persia: Kisrā; Abyssinia: Najāshī; Rome: Qayṣar; Egypt: Muqawqis; Yemen: Tubbaʿ.

items.q: In which sūrah is the story of the People of the al-Ukhdood (Ditch) mentioned?
items.a: In sūrat al-Burooj.

items.q: By what will the latter part of this ummah be set right?
items.a: By that which set right its first part.

items.q: What are the pillars of partisanship (ḥizbiyyah)?
items.a: Lying, deception, and trickery.

items.q: What is the worst trait in a man?
items.a: Panic-driven miserliness and crippling cowardice.

items.q: What is the Day of Increase (Yawm al-Mazeed)?
items.a: Friday.

items.q: Which tree is likened to the Muslim, whose leaves do not fall?
items.a: A date palm.

items.q: In which part of the human body does Shayṭān spend the night?
items.a: The nasal passage.

items.q: What was the first house established for people?
items.a: Al-Bayt al-Ḥarām (Sacred House) in Makkah.

items.q: Mention some of the clear signs in al-Bayt al-Ḥarām.
items.a: Maqām (Station of) Ibrāheem, and whoever enters it is safe.

items.q: What are the three masājid?
items.a: Al-masjid al-Ḥarām, al-masjid of the Messenger of Allāh, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, al-masjid al-Aqṣā.

items.q: What is the greatest day of the week?
items.a: Friday.

items.q: What is the finest perfume?
items.a: Musk.

items.q: Mention the days of celebration for the Muslims?
items.a: ʿEid al-Aḍḥā, the days of Tashreeq, Friday, ʿEid al-Fiṭr

items.q: What are baheematu al-an˓ām (livestock)
items.a: Camels, cattle, and sheep.

items.q: When will the Hour be established?
items.a: None knows that except Allāh.

items.q: In which sūrah is the Statement of Allāh: "Verily, Allāh alone knows the knowledge of the Hour, He sends down rain, and knows that which in the wombs. No person knows what he will earn tomorrow, and no person knows in what land he will die, Verily, Allāh is All Knower, All Aware".
items.a: Sūrat Luqmān.

items.q: What is the blazing lamp (as-sirāj al-wahhāj)?
items.a: The sun.

items.q: Who are those who earned Allāh’s Anger and those who went astray in sūrat al-Fātiḥah?
items.a: Those who earned Allāh’s anger are the Jews, and those who went astray are the Christians.

items.q: By what did Allāh stabilize the earth?
items.a: By the mountains.

items.q: How long is a day with Allāh?
items.a: One thousand years of what we count.

items.q: What is the frailest of houses?
items.a: The spider’s house.

items.q: Which town did Allāh make sacred?
items.a: Makkah.

items.q: What is Umm al-Qurā?
items.a: Makkah.

items.q: What are Ṭayyibah and Ṭābah?
items.a: Al-Madeenah.

items.q: Who said: “If a man were to intend therein—meaning al-Masjid al-Ḥarām—an act of deviation with injustice while he is in ʿAdan Abyan, Allāh would make him taste a painful punishment”?
items.a: ʿAbdullāh bnu Masʿood, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: What are meant by: ath-Thaqalān (the Two Weighty Ones), al-ʿUmarān (the Two ʿUmars), al-Qiblatān (the Two Qiblahs), al-Ḥaramān (the Two Sanctuaries), aṣ-Ṣaḥeeḥān (the Two Authentics), al-Qamarān (the Two Luminaries), al-Aṣgharān (the Two Smaller Ones), and al-Aswadān (the Two Black Ones)?
items.a: Ath-Thaqalān: humans and jinn. Al-ʿUmarān: Abū Bakr and ʿUmar. Al-Qiblatān: the Kaʿbah and al-Masjid al-Aqṣā. Al-Ḥaramān: Makkah and al-Madeenah. aṣ-Ṣaheehān: al-Bukhārī and Muslim. Al-Qamarān: the sun and the moon. Al-Aṣgharān: the heart and the tongue. Al-Aswadān: dates and water (from what is eaten), and the snake and the scorpion among venomous creatures.

items.q: How many categories of people are mentioned at the beginning of sūrat al-Baqarah?
items.a: Believers, disbelievers, and hypocrites.

items.q: Which is the most beloved city to Allāh and His Messenger, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: Makkah.

items.q: What are the most beloved places to Allāh?
items.a: The masājid.

items.q: What are the most hated places to Allāh?
items.a: Market places.

items.q: How many joints does a human have?
items.a: Three hundred and sixty joints.

items.q: How many months are there?
items.a: Twelve months.

items.q: What are the names of the months?
items.a: Muḥarram, Ṣafar, Rabeeʿ al-Awwal, Rabeeʿ al-Ākhir, Jumādā al-Ūlā, Jumādā al-Ākhirah, Rajab, Shaʿbān, Ramaḍān, Shawwāl, Thū al-Qaʿdah, Thū al-Ḥijjah.

items.q: Which are the Ḥarām (sacred) Months?
items.a: Thū al-Qaʿdah, Thū al-Ḥijjah, Muḥarram, and Rajab.

items.q: Who is the orphan?
items.a: One whose father died, and is still under the age of puberty.

items.q: Who are the two weak ones in the statement of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam: “O Allāh, I declare inviolable the rights of the two weak ones”?
items.a: The orphan and the woman.

items.q: What is the difference between our fasting and the fasting of the People of the Book?
items.a: As-Saḥoor (the pre-dawn meal).

items.q: When is the Day of ʿArafah?
items.a: The ninth day of Thū al-Ḥijjah.

items.q: On which day does Allāh free people the most from the Fire?
items.a: The Day of ʿArafah.

items.q: When is the Day of ʿĀshoorāʾ?
items.a: The tenth day of Muḥarram.

items.q: How many sacrificial animals did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, slaughter with his own hand during his ḥajj?
items.a: Sixty-three camels.

items.q: Who slaughtered the remainder?
items.a: ʿAlī bnu Abī Ṭālib, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: What is the first matter judged between people on the Day of Resurrection?
items.a: From the rights of Allāh: the first they are taken to account for is prayer. From the rights of creation: bloodshed.

items.q: Mention five harmful creatures.
items.a: The rabid dog, the crow, the scorpion, the mouse, and the snake.

items.q: What is the ruling regarding these five?
items.a: They are to be killed in both the sacred precinct and outside it.

items.q: Mention four creatures the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, forbade killing.
items.a: The ant, the bee, the hoopoe, and the shrike.

items.q: Which animal did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, inform is from the animals of al-Jannah?
items.a: Sheep.

items.q: Mention the stages of the fetus in its mother’s womb.
items.a: Forty days as a drop, then forty days as a clinging clot, then forty days as a chewed-like lump, then bones, then the bones are covered with flesh.

items.q: Who will have the longest necks on the Day of Resurrection?
items.a: The callers to prayer (muʾaththins).

items.q: What is the best day upon which the sun has risen?
items.a: Friday.

items.q: On which days are deeds presented to Allāh?
items.a: Monday and Thursday.

items.q: What is the Day of the Greater Ḥajj?
items.a: The Day of an-Naḥr (Slaughtering).

items.q: What is the Sunnah when ascending during travel?
items.a: Takbeer.

items.q: What is the Sunnah when descending during travel?
items.a: Tasbeeḥ.

items.q: On which day did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, prefer to travel?
items.a: Thursday.

items.q: How should you bid farewell to a traveler, and how should he reply?
items.a: You tell him: "Astawdiʿu Allāh dīnaka wa amānataka wa khawātīm ʿamalika" (I place your religion, your trust, and the final deeds of your actions in Allāh’s care), and he replies: "Astawdiʿuka Allāh alladhī lā taḍīʿu wadāʾiʿahu" (I place you in the care of Allāh, whose trusts are never lost).

items.q: What is the duʿāʾ for riding?
items.a: “Ṣubḥānallathī sakhkhara lanā hāthā, wa mā kunnā lahu muqrīnīn, wa innā ilā rabbina lamunqalibūn.” (Glory be to the One who has subjected this to us, and we were not capable of it; and indeed to our Lord we shall return).

items.q: What is the tree whose water is a cure for the eye?
items.a: The truffle (al-kamʾah).

items.q: Which wind was the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, aided by?
items.a: Aṣ-Ṣabā.

items.q: Which wind destroyed the people of ʿĀd?
items.a: Ad-Daboor.

items.q: What is aṣ-Ṣabā?
items.a: A wind that comes from the east.

items.q: What is ad-Daboor?
items.a: A wind that comes from the west.

items.q: Who will destroy the Kaʿbah at the end of time?
items.a: Thū as-Suwayqatayn from Abyssinia.

items.q: Where was the winter journey of Quraysh to?
items.a: Yemen.

items.q: Where was the summer journey of Quraysh to?
items.a: Ash-Shām.

items.q: Name the fingers starting with the thumb.
items.a: Al-Ibhām (Thumb), as-Sabābah (index), al-Wusṭā (middle), al-Binṣar (ring), al-Khinṣar (little finger).

items.q: What is al-Ḥaneef?
items.a: One who turns toward Allāh and turns away from all besides Him.

items.q: Who are the most fearful of Allāh among people?
items.a: The scholars; and at their peak, the prophets.

items.q: Do inanimate objects fear Allāh, ʿazza wa jall?
items.a: Yes.

items.q: What animal used to blow the fire upon Ibrāheem, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: The gecko.

items.q: What is the reward for killing a gecko?
items.a: Whoever kills it with the first strike gets one hundred good deeds.

items.q: Mention some foods the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, loved.
items.a: Sweets, honey, cucumber with ripe dates, pumpkin, butter and dates.

items.q: What is ath-thareed?
items.a: Bread with meat.

items.q: What plant did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, love and look for in his food?
items.a: Pumpkin.

items.q: What are the benefits of pumpkin?
items.a: It is a fast-growing plant, has large leaves that provide shade, is not approached by flies, produces nutritious fruit, and can be eaten raw or cooked with its flesh and peel.

items.q: Why did Allāh create the stars?
items.a: For three purposes: to adorn the sky, to repel the devils, and as signs to guide.

items.q: Why did Allāh create livestock?
items.a: For riding, eating their meat and drinking their milk, carrying loads to distant lands, benefiting from their skins and wool, and other benefits.

items.q: What is the female of a horse (ḥiṣān) called?
items.a: A mare (faras).

items.q: Allāh says: “He creates you in the wombs of your mothers: creation after creation in three veils of darkness” [Az-Zumar: 6]. What are the three veils of darkness?
items.a: The darkness of the womb, the darkness of the placenta, and the darkness of the belly.

items.q: What tree will the Jews hide behind during their war with the Muslims at the end of time?
items.a: The gharqad tree.

items.q: What is the height of the Kaʿbah?
items.a: Thirteen meters.

items.q: Allāh mentioned three things regarding the date-stone in the Qurʾān. What are they?
items.a: An-naqeer: the small pit on the back of the date-stone. Al-fateel: the thread in the middle of the date-stone. Al-qiṭmeer: the thin wrapping around the date-stone.

items.q: How did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, describe a house with no dates in it?
items.a: Its residents are hungry, or like a house with no food.

items.q: Who are those on whom Allāh has bestowed His Grace?
items.a: The prophets, the ṣiddīqqoon, the shaheeds, and the righteous.

items.q: Who is al-baʿl?
items.a: The husband.

items.q: Where is al-ʿaql (mind)?
items.a: In the heart.

items.q: With whom was the Prophet’s, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, seal after his death?
items.a: It was with Abū Bakr, may Allāh be pleased with him, then ʿUmar, may Allāh be pleased with him, then ʿUthmān, may Allāh be pleased with him, then it fell from him into the well of Arees.

items.q: Can a person have two hearts?
items.a: No. Allāh says: “Allāh has not placed two hearts in a man’s body” [Al-Aḥzāb: 4].

items.q: Mention some of the things that have been reported as being seven.
items.a: The heavens are seven; the earths are seven; seven whom Allāh will shade in His shade; al-Fātiḥah, (the Seven Oft-Repeated); ṭawāf is seven circuits; saʿy is seven circuits; in the stoning of the jamarāt are seven pebbles; and the limbs of prostration are seven.

items.q: Mention seven Salafi tafseers.
items.a: Ibn Katheer, Ibn Abī Ḥātim, Ibn Jareer, al-Baghawī, Ibn Taymiyyah, ash-Shanqeeṭī, as-Saʿdī.

items.q: What is the best tafseer?
items.a: Tafseer Ibn Katheer.

items.q: Mention three tafseers of the innovators.
items.a: Al-Kashshāf by az-Zamakhsharī, Mukhtaṣar Ibn Katheer by aṣ-Ṣāboonī, Fi Ẓilāl al-Qurʾān by Sayyid Quṭb.

items.q: What is the best book on major sins?
items.a: Az-Zawājir ʿan Iqtirāf al-Kabāʾir by Ibn Ḥajar al-Haytamī, may Allāh have mercy upon him.

items.q: What is the best book on history?
items.a: Al-Bidāyah wa an-Nihāyah by Ibn Katheer.

items.q: What is the best book in the history of Yemen?
items.a: Hijaru al-ʿIlm wa Maʿāqiluhu by al-Akwaʿ, though it has a few criticisms.

items.q: What are the most authentic compiled books?
items.a: Saḥeeḥ al-Bukhārī and Saḥeeḥ Muslim.

items.q: What are the six Ummahāt (Mothers)?
items.a: Saḥeeḥ all-Bukhārī, Saḥeeḥ Muslim, Sunan Abū Dāwood, Sunan at-Tirmithī, Sunan an-Nasāʾī, and Sunan Ibn Mājah, may Allāh have mercy on them all.

items.q: What are the four Sunan?
items.a: Sunan Abū Dāwood, Sunan at-Tirmithī, Sunan an-Nasāʾī, Sunan Ibn Mājah.

items.q: Name the authors of the six Ummahāt.
items.a: Al-Bukhārī: Muḥammad bnu Ismāʿeel. Muslim: Muslim bnu al-Ḥajjāj. Abū Dawood: Sulaymān bnu al-Ashʿath. At-Tirmithī: Muḥammad bnu ʿĪsā. An-Nasāʾī: Aḥmad bnu Shuʿayb. Ibn Mājah: Muḥammad bnu Yazeed al-Qazweenī, may Allāh have mercy on them all.

items.q: Mention some Umarāʾ al-Mumʾmineen in al-Ḥadeeth (Commanders of the Believers in Ḥadeeth)
items.a: Abū az-Zinād, Shuʿbah, al-Bukhārī, Aḥmad, and ad-Dāraqutnī, may Allāh have mercy on them.

items.q: Who are the shrewdest Arabs?
items.a: ʿAmr bnu al-ʿĀṣ, al-Mugheerah bnu Shuʿbah, Muʿāwiyah bnu Abī Sufyān, may Allāh be pleased with them all, and Ziyād bnu Abeeh.

items.q: Mention some heretics of Islām.
items.a: Abū Ḥayyān at-Tawḥīdī, Abū al-ʿAlāʾ al-Maʿarrī, and Ibn ar-Rāwandī.

items.q: Mention three false claimants to prophet-hood.
items.a: Musaylimah al-Kaththāb (Liar), al-Aswad al-ʿAnsī, Ṭulayḥah al-Asadī.

items.q: Who was the woman from Banū Tameem who claimed prophet-hood?
items.a: Sajāḥ. However, she embraced Islām at the end of her life.

items.q: Mention four prominent early Yemeni scholars.
items.a: ʿAbd ar-Razzāq bnu Hammām as-Sanʿānī, Muḥammad bnu Ibrāheem al-Wazeer, Muḥammad bnu Ismāʿeel al-Ameer, Muḥammad bnu ʿAlī ash-Shawkāni, may Allāh have mercy on them.

items.q: Mention four mujaddideen (revivers) of this era.
items.a: Ibn Bāz, al-Albānī, al-ʿUthaymeen, al-Wādiʿī.

items.q: Who was the one about whom shaykh Muqbil said: “He is a man loved in both the Ḥill and the Ḥaram (i.e., everywhere)”?
items.a: Shaykh Ibn Bāz.

items.q: Who was the one about whom shaykh al-ʿUthaymīn said: “He possesses vast learning, far-reaching scholarship, and is highly persuasive”?
items.a: Shaykh al-Albānī.

items.q: Who said: “A library that does not contain the books of al-AlbānĪ is a poor library”?
items.a: Shaykh Muqbil al-Wādiʿī, may Allāh have mercy on him.

items.q: Mention five works of Shaykh al-‘Uthaymeen (may Allāh have mercy on him).
items.a: 1- Ash-Sharḥ al-Mumtiʿ ʿalā Zād al-Mustaqniʿ; 2- Al-Qawl al-Mufīd fī Sharḥ Kitāb at-Tawḥīd; 3- Sharḥ al-ʿAqīdah al-Wāsiṭiyyah; 4- Sharḥ al-ʿAqīdah as-Saffārīniyyah; 5- Al-Uṣūl min ʿIlm al-Uṣūl.

items.q: Mention five works of Shaykh Muqbil ibn Hādi al-Wādiʿī (may Allāh have mercy on him).
items.a: 1- Aṣ-Ṣaḥīḥ al-Musnad mimmā laysa fī aṣ-Ṣaḥīḥayn; 2- Ash-Shafāʿah; 3- Riyāḍ al-Jannah fī ar-Radd ʿalā Aʿdāʾ as-Sunnah; 4- Ṣaʿqat az-Zilzāl li-Nasf Abāṭīl ar-Rafḍ wa al-Iʿtizāl; 5- al-Makhraj min al-Fitnah.

items.q: Mention the author of each of the following books: Zād al-Maʿād; Minhāj as-Sunnah; Ḥādī al-Arwāḥ; al-ʿAqīdah al-Wāsiṭiyyah; al-Uṣūl ath-Thalāthah; as-Silsilah aṣ-Ṣaḥīḥah; as-Silsilah aḍ-Ḍaʿīfah; al-Jāmiʿ aṣ-Ṣaḥīḥ mimmā laysa fī aṣ-Ṣaḥīḥayn; Bulūgh al-Marām; Fatḥ al-Bārī Sharḥ Ṣaḥīḥ al-Bukhārī; Lumʿat al-Iʿtiqād; ʿUmdat al-Aḥkām; Nayl al-Awṭār; ad-Darārī al-Muḍiyyah; Subul as-Salām; Riyāḍ aṣ-Ṣāliḥīn.
items.a: 1- Zād al-Maʿād and Ḥādī al-Arwāḥ: Ibn al-Qayyim (Muḥammad bnu Abī Bakr bnu Ayyūb az-Zarʿī). 2- Minhāj as-Sunnah and al-ʿAqīdah al-Wāsiṭiyyah: Shaykh al-Islām Ibn Taymiyyah (Aḥmad bnu ʿAbd al-Ḥalīm al-Ḥarrānī). 3- Al-Uṣūl ath-Thalāthah: Muḥammad bnu ʿAbd al-Wahhāb at-Tamīmī an-Najdī. 4- Lumʿat al-Iʿtiqād: Muwaffaq ad-Dīn al-Maqdisī (ʿAbdullāh bni Aḥmad bnu Qudāmah). 5- ʿUmdat al-Aḥkām: ʿAbd al-Ghanī bnu ʿAbd al-Wāḥid al-Maqdisī. 6- As-Silsilah aṣ-Ṣaḥīḥah and as-Silsilah aḍ-Ḍaʿīfah: al-Albānī (Muḥammad Nāṣir ad-Dīn bnu al-Ḥājj Nūḥ bnu Najātī, may Allāh have mercy on him). 7- Al-Jāmiʿ aṣ-Ṣaḥīḥ mimmā laysa fī aṣ-Ṣaḥīḥayn: Muqbil bnu Hādī al-Wādiʿī. 8- Bulūgh al-Marām and Fatḥ al-Bārī Sharḥ Ṣaḥīḥ al-Bukhārī: Ibn Ḥajar al-ʿAsqalānī (Aḥmad bnu ʿAlī bnu Ḥajar). 9- Subul as-Salām: Ibn al-Amīr aṣ-Ṣanʿānī (Muḥammad bnu Ismāʿīl al-Amīr). 10- Nayl al-Awṭār and ad-Darārī al-Muḍiyyah: ash-Shawkānī (Muḥammad bnu ʿAlī). 11- Riyāḍ aṣ-Ṣāliḥīn: an-Nawawī (Yaḥyā bnu Sharaf bnu Murriy).

items.q: Who authored aṭ-Ṭibb an-Nabawī?
items.a: Ibn al-Qayyim, may Allāh have mercy on him.

items.q: Who authored ar-Risālah and al-Umm?
items.a: As-Shāfiʿī, may Allāh have mercy on him.

items.q: Who is meant by “Shaykh al-Islām” when no further specification is given?
items.a: Shaykh al-Islām Ibn Taymiyyah.

items.q: Who are the Imāms of the four mathāhib?
items.a: Aḥmad bnu Ḥanbal, as-Shāfiʿī, Mālik, and Abū Ḥaneefah, may Allāh have mercy on them.

items.q: What is the first ḥadīth in Ṣaḥīḥ al-Bukhārī?
items.a: The ḥadīth of ʿUmar bnu al-Khaṭṭab, may Allāh be pleased with him, who said: “I heard the Messenger of Allāh, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, say: ‘Actions are only by intentions, and each person shall have only what he intended. Thus, whoever’s migration was for a worldly gain, or a woman he wishes to marry, then his migration is to that for which he migrated’”.

items.q: What is the last ḥadīth in Saḥeeḥ al-Bukhārī?
items.a: The ḥadīth of Abū Hurayrah, may Allāh be pleased with him, who said: The Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, said: “Two phrases beloved to Ar-Rahmān (The All-Merciful), light on the tongue, heavy on the scale: Ṣubḥānallāhi wa biḥamdihī, Ṣubḥānallāhi al-ʿAẓīm (Glory be to Allāh, and all praise is His. Glory be to Allāh, the Magnificent)”.

items.q: Who said: “This knowledge is religion, so be careful about whom you learn your religion from”?
items.a: Al-Imām Muḥammad bnu Seereen, may Allāh have mercy on him.

items.q: Who said: “Knowledge is not attained with bodily comfort”?
items.a: Al-Imām Yaḥyā bnu Abī Katheer, may Allāh have mercy on him.

items.q: Who said: “A shy person cannot learn, nor the arrogant”?
items.a: Al-Imām Mujāhid, may Allāh have mercy on him.

items.q: Who said: “We are a people whom Allāh has honored with Islām; if we seek honor through anything else, Allāh will make us humiliated”?
items.a: ʿUmar bnu al-Khaṭṭāb.

items.q: Who said: “Live roughly, for blessings do not last”?
items.a: ʿUmar bnu al-Khaṭṭāb, may Allāh be pleased with him.

items.q: Who said: “Know that the Sunnah is Islām, and Islām is the Sunnah”?
items.a: Al-Imām al-Barbahārī, may Allāh have mercy on him.

items.q: Who said: “Look after Ahl as-Sunnah, for they are strangers”?
items.a: Al-Imām Sufyān al-Thawrī, may Allāh have mercy on him.

items.q: Who said: “Follow and do not innovate, for you have been spared the need for anything else”?
items.a: ʿAbdullāh bnu Masʿood.

items.q: Who said: “Whoever introduces an innovation in Islām and sees it as good, then he has claimed that Muḥammad has betrayed the message”?
items.a: Al-Imām Mālik bnu Anas, may Allāh have mercy on him.

items.q: Who said: “Do not sit with those of deviant desires, as that makes the heart sick”?
items.a: ʿAbdullāh bnu ʿAbbās, may Allāh have mercy on him.

items.q: Who said: “Ahl as-Sunnah are the purity of the Muslims, and they are the best of people to people”?
items.a: Shaykh al-Islām Ibn Taymiyyah, may Allāh have mercy on him.

items.q: Who said: “The foundation of knowledge is fear of Allāh”?
items.a: Al-Imām Aḥmad bnu Ḥanbal, may Allāh have mercy on him.

items.q: Who said: “The Zaydī mathhab is built upon delusions”?
items.a: Shaykh Muqbil bnu Hādī al-Wādiʿī, may Allāh have mercy on him.

items.q: Who said: “The Muslim Brotherhood cannot be trusted with the religion”?
items.a: Shaykh Muqbil ibn Hādi al-Wādi‘y, may Allāh have mercy on him.

items.q: Who are the heirs of the Prophets?
items.a: The scholars.

items.q: Who testified to the Oneness of Allāh?
items.a: The angels and the people of knowledge.

items.q: For whom do the fish in the sea seek forgiveness?
items.a: For the one who teaches people khayr (goodness).

items.q: In which sūrah is the Statement of Allāh, Exalted is He: “Say: This is my way; I call to Allāh with clear insight, I and those who follow me. Glory be to Allāh, and I am not of the mushrikeen”?
items.a: In sūrat Yusuf.

items.q: What is the ruling on seeking knowledge?
items.a: An obligation upon every Muslim.

items.q: In which sūrah is the Statement of Allāh, Exalted is He: “I did not create the jinn and mankind except that they worship Me * I do not want from them any provision, nor do I want that they feed Me * Indeed, Allāh is Ar-Razzāq (the Provider), the Possessor of strength, Al-Mateen (the Firm)”?
items.a: In sūrat ath-Thāriyāt.

items.q: In which sūrah is the Statement of Allāh, Exalted is He: “And Allāh brought you out of your mothers’ wombs knowing nothing, and He gave you hearing, sight, and hearts, so that you may be thankful”?
items.a: In sūrat an-Naḥl.

items.q: What did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, liken one who teaches people good but neglects himself?
items.a: To a lamp that gives light to people while burning himself.

items.q: Who are the two insatiable ones?
items.a: The seeker of knowledge and the seeker of dunyā (worldly life).

items.q: What preserves knowledge?
items.a: Writing.

items.q: What is the ruling on lying about the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: The Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, said: “Whoever lies about me deliberately, has earned his seat in the Fire”.

items.q: What is the recompense of one who conceals knowledge while able to convey it?
items.a: He will be bridled with a bridle of fire on the Day of Resurrection.

items.q: How can one repent for concealing knowledge and not conveying it to people?
items.a: He must repent, rectify, and clarify the knowledge as best as he can.

items.q: How will knowledge be removed at the end of time?
items.a: By the death of the scholars.

items.q: In which sūrah is the Statement of Allāh, Exalted is He: “Indeed, only those of His slaves who are knowledgeable truly fear Allāh”?
items.a: In sūrat Fāṭir.

items.q: What should one say upon waking from sleep?
items.a: “Al-ḥamdu lillāhi allathī aḥyānā baʿda mā amatanā, wa ilayhi an-nushūr” (All praise is due to Allāh Who gave us life after He caused us to die, and to Him is the resurrection).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What should one do after waking up?
items.a: Use the siwāk (tooth-stick), not dip the hand in a vessel until it is washed three times, perform wuḍooʾ (ablution), inhale water through his nostrils, and pray.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Is there virtue in the siwāk?
items.a: It purifies the mouth and pleases the Rabb (Lord).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What should one say at the beginning of wuḍooʾ?
items.a: “Bismillāh” (In the name of Allāh).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Should one recite any thikr during wuḍooʾ?
items.a: No, because there is no evidence for that.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What should one say upon finishing wuḍooʾ?
items.a: “Ashhadu an lā ilāha illallāhu waḥdahu lā sharīka lahu, wa ashhadu anna Muḥammadan ʿabduhu wa rasūluhu” (I bear witness that there is no god but Allāh alone, with no partner, and I bear witness that Muḥammad is His slave and Messenger).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the best time for Witr prayer?
items.a: The last part of the night, for it is witnessed.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Why is the prayer at the last part of the night superior?
items.a: Because Allāh descends to the lowest heaven at that time and calls: “Is there anyone asking so that I may grant his request? Is there anyone seeking forgiveness so that I may forgive him?”. Also, the angels witness it.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What should one say in the qunoot of Witr?
items.a: “Allāhumma hdinī fīman hadayta, wa ʿāfinī fīman ʿāfayta, wa tawallanī fīman tawallayta, wa bārik lī fīmā aʿṭayta, wa qinī sharra mā qaḍayta, Innaka taqḍī wa lā yuqḍā ʿAlayka, wa innahu lā yathillu man wālayta, wa lā yaʿizzu man ʿādayta, tabārakta Rabbanā wa taʿālayta” (O Allāh, guide me among those You have guided, grant me well-being among those You have granted well-being, grant me Your support among those You have granted Your support, bless me in what You have given, and protect me from the evil of what You have decreed. Indeed, You decree and none can decree against You. No one You support is humiliated, and no one You take as enemy is honored. Blessed are You, our Lord, and Exalted).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What should one say after the salām of Witr?
items.a: “Ṣubḥāna al-Maliki al-Quddoos” (Glory be to the King, the Most-Holy) (three times).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What should one say when entering the bathroom?
items.a: “Allāhumma innī aʿūthu bika mina al-khubthi wa al-khabāʾith” (O Allāh, I seek refuge with You from the male and female devils).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Which foot should be put forward when entering the bathroom?
items.a: The left foot.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention some etiquettes of relieving oneself.
items.a: Do not face the qiblah or turn your back to it, do not hold the private part with the right hand while urinating, do not wipe oneself with the right hand, do not use fewer than three stones for istinjāʾ, do not use dung or bones, do not relieve oneself in the paths of people or their shade spots, maintain privacy, and avoid urine touching clothing or body.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: If using stones for cleaning after relieving oneself, how many should be used?
items.a: Three or more, and the number should be odd.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Is it permissible to mention Allāh while relieving oneself?
items.a: No, it is not permissible.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Which foot is put forward when exiting the bathroom?
items.a: The right foot.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What should one say when exiting the bathroom?
items.a: “Ghufrānaka” (I ask Your forgiveness).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What should one say upon hearing the athān?
items.a: Repeat what is said, except for “Ḥayya ʿalā aṣ-ṣalāh, ḥayya ʿalā al-falāḥ” (come to the prayer, come to success), for which one says: “La ḥawla wa lā quwwata illā billāh” (There is no power and no strength except with Allāh).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What should one say after the athān?
items.a: Ṣalāt upon the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, then say: “Allāhumma rabba hāthihi ad-daʿwati at-tāmmah, wa aṣ-ṣalāti al-qāʾimah, āti Muḥammadan al-wasīlata wa al-faḍīlah, wa bʿath-hu maqāman maḥmūdan allathī waʿadtah” (O Allāh, Lord of this complete call and established prayer, grant Muḥammad al-Waseelah and al-Faḍeelah, and raise him to the praised station You promised him).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What should one say upon hearing the crowing of roosters?
items.a: “Allāhumma innī asʾāluka min faḍlika” (O Allāh, I ask You from Your Grace).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention the virtue of two rakʿahs before Ṣubḥ prayer.
items.a: Better than worldly life and all it contains.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is recited in these two rakʿahs?
items.a: In the first: Al-Fātiḥah and “Qul ya ayyuha al-kāfiroon”. In the second: Al-Fātiḥah and “Qul Huwa Allāhu Aḥad”.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What does one do between the athān and iqāmah?
items.a: Make duʿāʾ, for supplication is answered at that time.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention an āyah where Allāh commands duʿāʾ and promises answering.
items.a: Allāh, Exalted is He, said: “And your Lord says, ‘Call upon Me; I will answer you. Indeed, those who scorn My worship will enter Jahannam humiliated’” [Ghāfir:60].
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What does one say when leaving the house?
items.a: "Bismi llāhi tawakkaltu ʿalā llāhi, lā ḥawla wa-lā quwwata illā bi-llāhi. Allāhumma innī aʿūthu bika an aḍilla aw uḍalla, aw azilla aw uzalla, aw aẓlima aw uẓlama, aw ajhala aw yujhala ʿalayya" (In the name of Allāh, I have entrusted myself to Allāh. O Allāh, I seek refuge in You lest I go astray or be led astray, lapse or be made to lapse, wrong or be wronged, blunder or be blundered upon).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What does one say when going to the masjid for Ṣubḥ prayer?
items.a: “Allāhumma jʿal fī qalbī nūran, wa fī lisānī nūran, wa-jʿal fī samʿī nūran, wa-jʿal fī baṣarī nūran, wa-jʿal min khalfī nūran, wa min amāmī nūran, wa-jʿal min fawqī nūran, wa min taḥtī nūran. Allāhumma ʿṭinī nūran” (O Allāh, place light in my heart, light on my tongue, light in my hearing, and light in my sight. Place light behind me and light before me; place light above me and light beneath me. O Allāh, grant me light).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the virtue of walking in darkness to prayer?
items.a: He will have The Complete Light on the Day of Resurrection.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Who has greater reward when walking to prayer: the far or the near?
items.a: The one farther from the masjid.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Does one hurry when walking to prayer?
items.a: No; he should walk with calmness and dignity.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is said upon entering and exiting the masjid?
items.a: When entering: “Allāhumma ftaḥ lī abwāba raḥmatik. Aʿūthu bi-llāhi al-ʿAẓīm, wa bi-Wajhihi al-Karīm, wa Sulṭānihi al-Qadīm, mina ash-shayṭāni ar-rajīm” (O Allāh, open for me the gates of Your mercy. I seek refuge in Allāh the Mighty, in His Noble Face, and in His eternal Sovereignty, from ash-Shayṭān the accursed). When exiting: “Allāhumma innī as’aluka min faḍlik” (O Allāh, I ask You from Your bounty).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Which foot is put forward when entering the masjid?
items.a: The right foot.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What does one do if he enters the masjid and wants to sit?
items.a: He prays two rakaʿāt: Taḥiyyat (The Greeting of) al-Masjid.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What if he prays in sandals?
items.a: He turns them over, checks them, removes impurities if present, then prays in them.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention some rewards of maintaining Fajr prayer.
items.a: He is under Allāh’s protection. It’s also among the causes of entering al-Jannah.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the reward of sitting after Fajr saying thikr until sunrise, then praying two rakaʿāt?
items.a: They get the reward of a complete, complete, complete ḥajj and ʿumrah.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention some morning and evening athkār.
items.a: Among evening athkār: The last two āyāt of al-Baqarah. Among morning and evening athkār: * "Bismi llāhi allathī lā yaḍurru maʿa ismihi shay’un fī al-arḍi wa-lā fī as-samā’i wa-huwa as-Samīʿu al-ʿAlīm" (In the name of Allāh, with Whose Name nothing can cause harm on earth or in the heaven, and He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing) [three times]. * Sayyid al-Istighfār (The Cheif of Asking Forgiveness). * "Allāhumma bika aṣbaḥnā, wa-bika amsaynā, wa-bika naḥyā, wa-bika namūtu, wa-ilayka an-nushūr" (O Allāh, by You we enter upon morning, by You we enter upon evening, by You we live, and by You we die, and to You is the resurrection). * "Allāhumma bika amsaynā, wa-bika aṣbaḥnā, wa-bika naḥyā, wa-bika namūtu, wa-ilayka al-maṣīr" (O Allāh, by You we enter upon evening, by You we enter upon morning, by You we live, and by You we die, and to You is the final return). * "Ṣubḥāna llāhi wa-biḥamdihī ʿadada khalqihi, wa-riḍā nafsihi, wa-zinata ʿarshihi, wa-midāda kalimātihi" (Glory be to Allāh and praise is His, as many times as the number of His creation, according to His pleasure, as heavy as His Throne, and as much as the ink for His words).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is Sayyid al-Istighfār?
items.a: "Allāhumma anta Rabbī, lā ilāha illā Anta, khalaqtanī wa-anā ʿabduka, wa-anā ʿalā ʿahdika wa-waʿdika mā istaṭaʿtu. Aʿūthu bika min sharri mā ṣanaʿtu. Abūʾu laka biniʿmatika ʿalayya, wa-abūʾu laka bithanbī fa-ghfir lī; fa-innahu lā yaghfiru ath-thunūba illā Anta" (O Allāh, You are my Lord; there is no god except You. You created me and I am Your slave, and I remain upon Your covenant and promise as much as I am able. I seek refuge in You from the evil of what I have done. I acknowledge before You Your favor upon me, and I acknowledge my sin to You, so forgive me; for none forgives sins except You).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the reward for saying Sayyid al-Istighfār?
items.a: Whoever says it by day or night and dies, enters al-Jannah.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention some of the virtues of Ḍuḥā prayer.
items.a: It suffices in place of ṣadaqah (charity) for every joint and protects from that day’s evil.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the reward of one ṣalāt upon the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: Allāh sends ten upon that who does it.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is said at the beginning and end of eating?
items.a: At the beginning: Bismillāh (In the Name of Allāh). At the end: Alḥamdu li-llāh (All praise is for Allāh).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the virtue of praising Allāh after eating and drinking?
items.a: A reason for Allāh’s pleasure, preserving the blessing, and increasing it.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention some etiquettes of eating.
items.a: Eating with the right hand, from what is nearest, from the sides, licking fingers, removing impurities from fallen food then eating it, not criticizing food, eating together.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention an evidence that food is blessed when many eat from it.
items.a: Narrated from Abū Hurayrah, may Allāh be pleased with him, that he said: The Messenger of Allāh, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, said: "The food of two is sufficient for three, and the food of three is sufficient for four". Agreed upon.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What does a Muslim do when yawning?
items.a: Suppresses it as much as possible and covers his mouth.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What glad tidings remain after prophet-hood?
items.a: Righteous dreams.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention the types of dreams?
items.a: They are three: Glad tidings from Allāh, self-talk, and frightening dreams from ash-Shayṭān.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What does a Muslim do if he sees a bad dream?
items.a: Does three light tongue-puffs, turns sides, seeks refuge in Allāh from the Shayṭān and the bad of the dream, tells no one, and may pray if they wish to.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What should one say when asked about what he does not know?
items.a: Allāh knows best (Allāhu aʿlam).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: In which sūrah is the Statement of Allāh: “And follow not that of which you have no knowledge. Verily! The hearing, and the sight, and the heart, of each of those one will be questioned”?
items.a: In sūrat al-Isrāʾ.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: If his father orders him to sin?
items.a: Obedience to him is not obligatory, due to the saying of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam: “There is no obedience to a created being in disobedience to Allāh, the Mighty and Majestic”. Narrated by Aḥmad (1095) from ʿAlī, may Allāh be pleased with him.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Does sin cause humiliation?
items.a: Yes.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is obligatory toward parents?
items.a: Obedience, dutifulness, and supplication for them.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Is serving parents jihād?
items.a: Yes.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Whose right is greater: mother or father?
items.a: The mother’s.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Who is regarded as being in the position of the mother?
items.a: The maternal aunt.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, advise young men who are unable to marry?
items.a: Fasting.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: How is one congratulated on getting married?
items.a: “Bāraka Llāhu laka, wa-bāraka ʿalayka, wa-jamaʿa baynakumā fī khayr” (May Allāh bless you, grant blessings upon you, and unite you both in goodness).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is said when stopping at a place?
items.a: "Aʿūthu bi-kalimāti Llāhi at-tāmmāti min sharri mā khalaq" (I seek refuge in the perfect Words of Allāh from the evil of what He has created).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What do you say when someone does you a favor?
items.a: "Jazāka Allāhu khayran" (May Allāh reward you with good).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What does the one who sneezes say and how is he answered?
items.a: Al-ḥamdu li-llāh (All praise is for Allāh). Whoever hears him, tells him: yarḥamuka Allāh  (May Allāh have mercy on you). The one who sneezed replies: yahdīkumu Allāhu wa-yuṣliḥu bālakum (May Allāh guide you and rectify your affairs).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What does one do upon receiving good news?
items.a: He prostrates in thanks to Allāh.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is said when rain falls?
items.a: "Allāhumma ṣayyiban nāfiʿan" (O Allāh, [make it] beneficial rain).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention an āyah that proves rain is also called ‘raḥmah’ (mercy).
items.a: Allāh, Exalted is He, said: “And it is He Who sends the winds as heralds of glad tidings, going before His Mercy (rain). Till when they have carried a heavy-laden cloud, We drive it to a land that is dead, then We cause water (rain) to descend thereon. Then We produce every kind of fruit therewith. Similarly, We shall raise up the dead, so that you may remember or take heed” [Al-Aʿrāf: 57].
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention an āyah that proves rain is called ‘provision’ (rizq).
items.a: Allāh, Exalted is He, said: “And in the alternation of night and day, and the provision (rain) that Allāh sends down from the sky, and revives therewith the earth after its death, and in the turning about of the winds (i.e. sometimes towards the east or north, and sometimes towards the south or west sometimes bringing glad tidings of rain etc., and sometimes bringing the torment), are signs for a people who understand” [Al-Jāthiyah: 5].
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What does one say when angry?
items.a: "Aʿūthu bi-llāhi mina ash-shayṭāni ar-rajīm" (I seek refuge in Allāh from the accursed Shayṭān).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: In which sūrah does Allāh say: “The good deed and the evil deed cannot be equal. Repel (the evil) with one which is better then verily he, between whom and you there was enmity, (will become) as though he was a close friend”?
items.a: In sūrat Fuṣṣilat.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What does one do when ash-Shayṭān provokes him?
items.a: He seeks refuge in Allāh from the accursed Shayṭān.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What does one do upon seeing an evil?
items.a: Change it by hand, and if unable to, by tongue, and if unable to, then by heart, which is the weakest of faith.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: How does one put on footwear?
items.a: Start with the right, and start with the left when removing.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What should one do if one of his footwear breaks?
items.a: He should remove the other one, as there is a ḥadīth which says: “Wear both footwear or remove both” (Agreed upon).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is said when entering the house?
items.a: "Bismillāh" (In the Name of Allāh).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the best form of salām?
items.a: "As-salāmu ʿalaykum wa-raḥmatu Allāhi wa-barakātuhu" (Peace, mercy, and blessings of Allāh be upon you).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What the reward for the salām?
items.a: If one says: “As-salāmu ʿalaykum”, he is granted ten ḥasanāt. If he says: “As-salāmu ʿalaykum wa-raḥmatu Allāhi”, he is granted twenty ḥasanāt. If he says: “As-salāmu ʿalaykum wa-raḥmatu Allāhi wa-barakātuhu”, he is granted thirty ḥasanāt.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Do you greet only those you know?
items.a: No, rather those you know and those you do not.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Do you greet children?
items.a: Yes, the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, did so.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What do you say when visiting the sick?
items.a: "Lā baʾsa, ṭahūrun in shāʾa Allāh" (No harm, it is a purification, if Allāh wills).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Is it appropriate to force a sick person to eat and drink?
items.a: No, it is not appropriate.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: In which sūrah is does Allāh, Exalted is He, say: “For indeed, with hardship comes ease. Indeed, with hardship comes ease”?
items.a: Sūrat ash-Sharḥ.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention some etiquettes of greeting (salām)?
items.a: A person who is walking greets those who are seated; a rider greets those on foot; a smaller group greets a larger one; the younger greets the older. One should offer greetings upon entering a gathering and again upon leaving it.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What should one say when the wind blows?
items.a: "O Allāh, I ask You for its good, and the good within it, and the good it was sent with, and I seek refuge in You from its harm, and the harm within it, and the harm it was sent with".
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention some etiquettes of seeking permission (istiʾthān).
items.a: Knock at the door gently; do not stand directly in front of it, but step to the right or left.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the phrasing for seeking permission?
items.a: Say: "As-salāmu ʿalaykum, may I enter?".
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: How many times should one seek permission?
items.a: Three times; if permission is granted, enter, otherwise leave.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the virtue of saying "La ḥawla wa la quwwata illā billāh"?
items.a: It is a treasure from the treasures of al-Jannah.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the reward for fasting Ramaḍān or spending its nights in prayer out of faith and seeking reward?
items.a: All his past sins are forgiven.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the reward for giving charity secretly?
items.a: Allāh will shade him under His shade on the day when there is no shade except His.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention some categories of people whom Allāh will shade on the day when there is no shade except His.
items.a: A just ruler; a young person raised in the worship of Allāh; a person whose heart is attached to the masjids; two people who love each other for the sake of Allāh, meeting and parting on that; a person whom a woman of beauty and status invites but he says, "I fear Allāh"; a person who gives charity secretly, even his right hand does not know what his left spent; a person who remembers Allāh alone until his eyes shed tears.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Does a Muslim’s wealth decrease by giving charity?
items.a: No.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the virtue of fasting on the Day of, ʿArafah?
items.a: It expiates the sins of the past and the coming year.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the virtue of fasting on ʿĀshūrāʾ?
items.a: It expiates the sins of the past year.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the virtue of fasting Ramaḍān and then following it with six days of Shawwāl?
items.a: It is like fasting a lifetime.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the al-Bayt al-ʿAteeq (The Ancient House)?
items.a: Al-Masjid al-Ḥarām (The Sacred Mosque).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: How many prayers is one prayer in Al-Masjid al-Ḥarām worth?
items.a: One hundred thousand prayers.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: How many prayers is one prayer in al-Masjid an-Nabawī worth?
items.a: One thousand prayers.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Which masjid’s prayer is like performing a ʿumrah?
items.a: Masjid Qubāʾ.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the reward of guiding someone to good or guidance?
items.a: One receives the reward like that of the doer.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What thikr causes one’s sins to be shed, even if they are like the foam of the sea?
items.a: Saying "Ṣubḥāna Allāhi wa biḥamdihi" (Glory be to Allāh, and all praise is His) 100 times.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What thikr gives one a thousand ḥasanāt or sheds a thousand sins?
items.a: Saying "Ṣubḥāna Allāhi" 100 times.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the most beloved speech to Allāh?
items.a: "Ṣubḥāna Allāhi, wa al-ḥamdu lillāhi, wa lā ilāha illa Allāhu, wa Allāhu Akbar".
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: There are two statements, light on the tongue, heavy on the scale, beloved to Ar-Raḥmān; what are they?
items.a: "Ṣubḥāna Allāhi wa-biḥamdihi, subḥāna Allāhi al-ʿAẓīm".
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: How far should a Muslim’s izār (lower garment) and thawb (long robe) reach?
items.a: Preferably to the middle of the shin, but it is permissible up to above the ankles.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the ruling on one whose garment extends below the ankle?
items.a: It is forbidden; what is below the ankles will be in the Fire.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Is it permissible to wear trousers/pants?
items.a: No; because it imitation of the disbelievers.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Can a man wear women’s clothes or vice versa?
items.a: No.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the ruling on shaving or trimming the beard?
items.a: Forbidden.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention five of the natural disposition (Fiṭrah) characteristics.
items.a: 1) Circumcision; 2) Removing pubic hair; 3) Trimming the mustache; 4) Plucking underarm hair; 5) Trimming nails.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the ruling on cursing fever?
items.a: It is not permissible to curse it, as it removes sins.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: How do we reduce fever?
items.a: By using water, as it is from the heat of Jahannam.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Is it permissible to sit between shade and sunlight?
items.a: No.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Define: tuḥfah, walīmah, ghadeerah, ʿaqeeqah, qirā.
items.a: Tuḥfah: food presented to a visitor. Walīmah: wedding feast. Ghadeera: feast for circumcision. ʿAqeeqah: sacrifice for the newborn, preferably on the seventh day. Qirā: food for guests.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Who is the ar-ruwaybiḍah?
items.a: A foolish person who speaks about public matters.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the hour on Friday during which supplication is answered?
items.a: The strongest view is that it is the last hour after ʿAṣr.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is ḥasad (malicious envy)?
items.a: Wishing for the removal of a blessing from others.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the ruling on ḥasad?
items.a: Forbidden.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention an āyah where “crow” is mentioned.
items.a: Allāh, Exalted is He, says: “Then Allāh sent a crow who scratched the ground to show him to hide the dead body of his brother. He (the murderer) said: ‘Woe to me! Am I not even able to be as this crow and to hide the dead body of my brother?’ Then he became one of those who regretted” [Al-Māʾidah: 31].
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is gheebah (backbiting)?
items.a: Mentioning your brother in a way he dislikes.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the ruling on gheebah?
items.a: Forbidden.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is nameemah (tale-bearing)?
items.a: It’s spreading words among people to cause discord.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the punishment of the tale-bearer?
items.a: The Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam,  said: “No tale-bearer will enter al-Jannah” (Agreed upon).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the punishment of one who has two faces in this world?
items.a: He will have two tongues of fire on the Day of Resurrection.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the reward for protecting a Muslim’s honor?
items.a: Allāh will protect his face from the Fire on the Day of Resurrection.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the most beloved deed to Allāh?
items.a: That which a person persists in, even if little.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the ruling on cursing ad-dahr (course of time)?
items.a: Forbidden.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Why did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, forbid al-khathf  (pelting small stones)?
items.a: Because it does not hunt game; it only blinds the eye or breaks teeth.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Is it permissible to kill ants or other insects by burning them?
items.a: No.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Can alcohol be a medicine?
items.a: No; if Allāh prohibits something, He does not make it a cure.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Who are the two actions that bring curses upon their doer?
items.a: Relieving oneself on public roads, or in shaded spots where people gather.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the ruling on urinating in stagnant water?
items.a: Not permissible.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the ruling on wasting water?
items.a: Not permissible; the wasteful are brothers of the devils.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Is it permissible to waste wealth?
items.a: No.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Is it permissible to sit on graves, step on them, or pass over them with shoes?
items.a: No.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Is it permissible to visit graves?
items.a: Permissible if in accordance with the Sunnah to remember the Hereafter and pray for the deceased; not permissible if it involves innovations or shirk.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What do we say when visiting graves?
items.a: “As-salāmu ʿalaykum ahla ad-diyāri mina al-muʾminīna wa al-muslimīna, wa-innā in shāʾa Allāhu bikum lāḥiqūn, nasʾalu Allāha lanā wa-lakumu al-ʿāfiyah” (Peace be upon you, O inhabitants of these homes, from among the believers and the Muslims. If Allāh wills, we shall join you. We ask Allāh for well-being for us and for you).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What do you do when passing by a disbeliever’s grave?
items.a: Give him the tidings of the Fire.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the jihād of women that does not involve fighting?
items.a: Ḥajj and ʿumrah.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Is it permissible to shake hands with a non-maḥram woman?
items.a: No.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the ruling on seclusion (khalwah) with a non-maḥram woman?
items.a: Forbidden.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: If a woman wants to travel, what is required for her?
items.a: She must have a maḥram with her.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Who are the prohibited women (muḥarramāt/maḥrams)s?
items.a: They’re mentioned in Allāh’s saying: “Forbidden to you (for marriage) are: your mothers, your daughters, your sisters, your father’s sisters, your mother’s sisters, your brother’s daughters, your sister’s daughters, your foster mother who gave you suck, your foster milk suckling sisters, your wives’ mothers, your step daughters under your guardianship, born of your wives to whom you have gone in - but there is no sin on you if you have not gone in them (to marry their daughters), - the wives of your sons who (spring) from your own loins, and two sisters in wedlock at the same time, except for what has already passed; verily, Allāh is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful * Also (forbidden are) women already married, except those (slaves) whom your right hands possess. Thus has Allāh ordained for you. All others are lawful, provided you seek (them in marriage) with Mahr (bridal-money given by the husband to his wife at the time of marriage) from your property, desiring chastity, not committing illegal sexual intercourse, so with those of whom you have enjoyed sexual relations, give them their Mahr as prescribed; but if after a Mahr is prescribed, you agree mutually (to give more), there is no sin on you. Surely, Allāh is Ever All-Knowing, All-Wise” [An-Nisāʾ: 23-24]
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Is it permissible to sell dogs?
items.a: No; its price is forbidden.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the ruling on owning dogs?
items.a: Not permissible unless it is a hunting, livestock, or farm dog.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the punishment for owning a dog other than hunting or livestock dogs?
items.a: A daily reduction of one qeeraṭ (in some narrations: two qeeraṭs) from his reward.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is bribery (rishwah)?
items.a: Money paid to achieve falsehood or invalidate a right.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the ruling on bribery?
items.a: Forbidden; as there is a ḥadīth which mentions that the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, cursed both the briber and the bribed.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is a person called who hides defective goods beneath good merchandise so that people do not see them?
items.a: He is called a cheater.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the ruling on deceit?
items.a: Forbidden; the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, said: "Whoever cheats us is not from among us".
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the ruling on one who unjustly seizes land?
items.a: Seven lands will be tied around his neck on the Day of Resurrection.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention a ḥadīth indicating not harming Muslims.
items.a: The Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, said: "There is to be no harm, nor reciprocating harm".
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: When a person dies, his deeds are cut off except from three. Mention them.
items.a: Continuous charity, beneficial knowledge, and a righteous child who prays for him.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: If your Muslim brother gifts you something, what should you do?
items.a: Accept it and reward him for it.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, accept gifts?
items.a: Yes, he accepted gifts but did not accept charity (ṣadaqah).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Why is eating and drinking with the left hand forbidden?
items.a: Because ash-Shayṭān eats and drinks with his left hand.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the ruling on drinking while standing?
items.a: Discouraged (makrūh).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the ruling on drinking from vessels of gold and silver?
items.a: Ḥarām.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: The Messenger of Allāh, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam said: “The upper hand is better than the lower hand”. What are the upper and lower hands?
items.a: The upper hand is the giving or spending hand; the lower hand is the begging or taking hand.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: If you encounter a disbeliever, do you greet him with salām?
items.a: No.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: If a disbeliever initiates salām, how do you reply?
items.a: You say: “wa ʿalaykum” (And upon you).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Is it permissible for a woman to be a president, minister, or judge?
items.a: It is not permissible, due to the saying of the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam: “Any people who entrust their affairs to a woman will never succeed” (Narrated by al-Bukhārī).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: In which sūrah is the āyah “Men are the protectors and maintainers of women, because Allāh has made one of them to excel the other, and because they spend (to support them) from their means. Therefore the righteous women are devoutly obedient (to Allāh and to their husbands), and guard in the husband’s absence what Allāh orders them to guard (e.g. their chastity, their husband’s property). As to those women on whose part you see ill-conduct, admonish them (first), (next), refuse to share their beds, (and last) beat them (lightly, if it is useful); but if they return to obedience, seek not against them means (of annoyance). Surely, Allāh is Ever Most High, Most Great”?
items.a: In sūrat an-Nisāʾ.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: The Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam,  said: “No one who severs ties will enter al-Jannah”. Who is the “one who severs ties”?
items.a: One who severs family ties.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the part, if sound, the whole body is sound?
items.a: The heart.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the ruling on imitating Jews, Christians, and polytheists, and what is the evidence?
items.a: It is forbidden. The Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, said: “Whoever imitates a people, then he is one of them”. (Narrated by Abū Dāwūd)
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention some forms of imitation.
items.a: Eating with the left hand, wearing trousers, celebrating non-Islāmic holidays, image-making, elections, democracy, demonstrations.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Who are those that take the graves of their prophets and righteous as places of worship?
items.a: Jews and Christians.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Who is a strong person?
items.a: One who controls himself when angry.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the most false of speech?
items.a: Conjecture.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: When is repentance not accepted?
items.a: When the sun rises from the west, and when the soul reaches the throat.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Write down the ḥadīth of intention.
items.a: The ḥadīth of ʿUmar bnu al-Khaṭṭāb, may Allāh be pleased with him, who said: “Actions are only by intentions, and every person will have only what they intended.
