Whoever migrates for Allāh and His Messenger, then their migration is for Allāh and His Messenger, and whoever migrates for worldly gain or to marry a woman, then their migration is for whatever they migrated for” (agreed upon).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What did Ibrāhīm say when he was thrown into the fire?
items.a: “Ḥasbunallāhu wa niʿma al-Wakīl” (Allāh is Sufficient for us, and the Best Disposer of affairs).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, say when he was told: “The people have gathered against you”?
items.a: He said: “Ḥasbunallāhu wa niʿma al-Wakīl”.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What are the two blessings many people lose out on?
items.a: Health and free time.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Can we count Allāh’s blessings?
items.a: No.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: With what are al-Jannah and Jahannam surrounded?
items.a: Al-Jannah is surrounded by hardships; Jahannam is surrounded by desires.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Who are the best of people?
items.a: Those whose lives are long, and their deeds are good.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Who are the worst of people?
items.a: Those whose lives are long, and their deeds are bad.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Upon what will slaves be resurrected?
items.a: Upon what they died.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: The Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, said: “If Allāh wants good for a slave, He ‘sweetens’ him”. What does that mean?
items.a: Allāh opens for him a righteous deed before death, then takes him while he is doing it.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the destroyer of pleasures the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, ordered us to remember often?
items.a: Death.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the first thing to rot in a human after death?
items.a: His guts.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the fast of Dāwūd?
items.a: To fast one day and break the fast the next.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: If you see someone not praying or committing sins, what is your duty?
items.a: To advise him, using wisdom and good admonition.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: In which sūrah Allāh said: “Invite to the Way of your Lord with wisdom and fair preaching, and argue with them in a way that is better. Truly, your Lord knows best who has gone astray from His Path, and He is the Best Aware of those who are guided”?
items.a: Sūrat an-Naḥl.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Write the ḥadīth: “The religion is counsel”.
items.a: From Abū Ruqayyah Tamīm ad-Dārī, may Allāh be pleased with him, that the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, said: "The religion is sincere counsel (an-naṣīḥah)". We said, "To whom?". He said: "To Allāh, to His Book, to His Messenger, to the leaders of the Muslims, and to their general populace". Narrated by Muslim.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: The Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam,  said: “The signs of a hypocrite are three”. Mention them.
items.a: Whenever he speaks, he lies; whenever he makes a promise, he breaks it; and whenever he is entrusted, he betrays.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Do the disbelievers and the hypocrites reform on the earth, or do they spread corruption?
items.a: They spread corruption.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the most harmful trial the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, left for men?
items.a: Women.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: When is a child commanded to pray, and when is he struck for it?
items.a: He is to be commanded at seven years old, and struck for it at ten.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Every nation has a trial. What is the trial of this nation?
items.a: Wealth.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: In which sūrah is the Statement of Allāh, Exalted is He: “O you who believe! Let not your properties or your children divert you from the remembrance of Allāh. And whosoever does that, then they are the losers”?
items.a: Sūrat al-Munāfiqūn.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What was the trial of Banī Isrāʾīl?
items.a: Women.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, fear poverty for his ummah?
items.a: No. Rather, he feared that the worldly life would be expanded for them.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention some of the desires of this world, and what did Allāh call them?
items.a: Women, children, gold, silver, fine horses, livestock, and crops. Allāh called them temporary enjoyment.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is true richness?
items.a: Richness of the soul.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the world like for the believer and the disbeliever?
items.a: The world is a prison for the believer and a paradise for the disbeliever.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Which noble companion narrated the ḥadīth: “Leave what gives you suspicion for what gives you none”?
items.a: Al-Ḥasan bnu ʿAlī, may Allāh be pleased with them both.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: For what did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, use his right hand?
items.a: For eating, drinking, taking, and giving.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Who were the first to introduce handshaking?
items.a: The people of Yemen.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Was handshaking practiced among the Companions of the Messenger of Allāh, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam?
items.a: Yes.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the virtue of handshaking?
items.a: When two Muslims shake hands, they are forgiven before they part.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: How long is a month?
items.a: Thirty days or twenty-nine days.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Who eats with one gut?
items.a: The believer.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Who eats with seven guts?
items.a: The disbeliever.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What are the most beloved names to Allāh?
items.a: ʿAbdullāh (Slave of Allāh) and ʿAbdurraḥmān (Slave of the All-Merciful).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the seed that is a cure for every disease except death?
items.a: The black seed.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Healing is in three things. What are they?
items.a: A drink of honey, cupping, and cauterization with fire.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the lifespan of this ummah?
items.a: Between sixty and seventy, only few exceed that.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is al-fuwaysiqah?
items.a: The mouse.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What do we prompt the dying person with?
items.a: We prompt him: “Lā ilāha illā Allāh” (There is no god but Allāh).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Allāh divided people in sūrat al-Wāqiʿah into three categories. Mention them.
items.a: The nearest, those on the right, and those on the left.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What words did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, teach Ibn ʿAbbās?
items.a: “Preserve Allāh and He will preserve you; preserve Allāh and you will find Him before you. If you ask, ask Allāh; and if you seek help, seek help from Allāh. And know that if the entire ummah were to gather to benefit you with something, they could not benefit you except with what Allāh has written for you; and if they were to gather to harm you with something, they could not harm you except with what Allāh has written against you. The pens have been lifted and the pages have dried”. Narrated by at-Tirmithī (2516), authenticated by al-Albānī as in al-Mishkāt (5302), and by al-Wādiʿī in aṣ-Ṣaḥīḥ al-Musnad (685).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Complete the ḥadīth: The Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, said: “I was only sent…”.
items.a: “…to complete noble character”, from Abū Hurayrah, may Allāh be pleased with him. Narrated by Aḥmad and others.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the best description of al-Jannah?
items.a: In it is what no eye has seen, no ear has heard, and no human heart has imagined.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What admits people into al-Jannah the most?
items.a: Taqwā (fear) of Allāh and good character.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the best provision a person can take along?
items.a: Taqwā.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What things did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, say bless homes homes and increase lifespans?
items.a: Maintaining kinship ties, good neighborliness, and good character.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: The Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam,  told al-Ashajj ʿAbd al-Qays that he had two traits loved by Allāh. What are they?
items.a: Forbearance and deliberateness.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention some traits that Allāh loves.
items.a: Iḥsān (Excellence), repentance, purification, taqwā (fear of Allāh), patience, jihād, reliance upon Allāh, justice, fairness, and following the Messenger.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention some traits that Allāh does not love.
items.a: Kufr (Disbelief), corruption, transgression, sin, oppression, betrayal, arrogance, pride, and haughtiness.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention two major sins whose doers Allāh threatened with war.
items.a: Usury and enmity toward the allies of Allāh.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Why are women the majority of the people of Fire?
items.a: They curse frequently and are ungrateful to their husbands.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Is a woman’s softening of her voice a temptation?
items.a: Yes.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the virtue of saying: “Bismillāhi al-lathī lā yaḍurru maʿa ismihi shayʾun fī al-arḍi wa-lā fī as-samāʾi, wa-huwa as-Samīʿu al-ʿAlīmu” (In the name of Allāh, with whose Name nothing harms on earth or in the heavens, and He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing) three times?
items.a: Nothing will harm him, and no sudden calamity will strike him.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the reward of one who restrains his anger while able to act upon it?
items.a: Allāh will call him before all creation on the Day of Resurrection and allow him to choose from al-ḥoor al-ʾeen whatever he wishes.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the reward for one who builds a masjid for Allāh?
items.a: Allāh builds for him a house in al-Jannah.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the reward of reciting āyat al-Kursi after an obligatory prayer?
items.a: Nothing prevents him from entering al-Jannah except death.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the virtue of reciting āyat al-Kursi before sleep?
items.a: No devil approaches him, and a guardian from Allāh remains with him until morning.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What supplication, if said before sleep and one dies, causes him to die upon the fiṭrah?
items.a: “Allāhumma aslamtu nafsī ilayka, wa fawwaḍtu amrī ilayka, wa aljaʾtu ẓahrī ilayka, rahbatan wa raghbatan ilayka, lā maljaʾa wa lā manjā minka illā ilayka, āmantu bi-kitābika allathī anzalta, wa bi-nabiyyika allathī arsalta” (O Allāh, I have submitted myself to You, entrusted my affair to You, and turned my back to You, out of fear and hope in You. There is no refuge and no escape from You except to You. I believe in Your Book which You revealed, and in Your Prophet whom You sent).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the reward of saying: “Subḥāna Allāhi al-ʿAẓīmi wa bi-ḥamdiḥi”?
items.a: A palm tree is planted for him in al-Jannah.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the reward of one who loses his eyesight and remains patient?
items.a: Allāh compensates him with al-Jannah.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the reward for kindness to daughters?
items.a: They will be a shield for him from the Fire.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the reward of praying ʿIshāʾ and Fajr in congregation?
items.a: As if he prayed the entire night, and he is under the protection of Allāh.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the reward of one who increases prostration to Allāh?
items.a: He does not prostrate to Allāh once except that Allāh raises him by it one rank and removes from him by it one misdeed.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What should a Muslim do when he intends a matter?
items.a: He prays the prayer of istikhārah (seeking guidance).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention how the prayer of istikhārah is performed.
items.a: He prays two rakʿahs other than the obligatory prayer, then he supplicates with this supplication: “Allāhumma innī astakhīruka bi-ʿIlmika, wa-astaqdiruka bi-Qudratika, wa-asʾaluka min Faḍlika al-ʿaẓīm, fa-innaka taqdiru wa-lā aqdir, wa-taʿlamu wa-lā aʿlam, wa-anta ʿallāmu al-ghuyūb. Allāhumma in kunta taʿlamu anna hāthā al-amra — and he names the matter — khayrun lī fī dīnī wa-maʿāshī wa-ʿāqibati amrī, fa-qdurhu lī wa-yassirhu lī, thumma bārik lī fīh. Wa in kunta taʿlamu anna hāthā al-amra sharrun lī fī dīnī wa-maʿāshī wa-ʿāqibati amrī, fa-ṣrifhu ʿannī wa-ṣrifnī ʿanhu, wa-qdur lī al-khayra ḥaythu kān, thumma arḍinī bih” (O Allāh, I seek Your guidance by Your Knowledge, and I seek ability by Your Power, and I ask You from Your Immense Bounty, for You have power and I have none; You know and I do not know; and You are the Knower of the unwitnessed. O Allāh, if You know that this matter — and he names the matter — is good for me in my religion, my livelihood, and the outcome of my affair, then decree it for me, make it easy for me, and bless it for me. And if You know that this matter is bad for me in my religion, my livelihood, and the outcome of my affair, then turn it away from me and turn me away from it, and decree for me the good wherever it may be, then make me content with it).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What does a Muslim do when he reads an āyah containing a prostration?
items.a: He performs the prostration of recitation.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What happens to ash-Shayṭān when a Muslim performs the prostration of recitation?
items.a: He withdraws, crying.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Why does ash-Shayṭān cry?
items.a: Because Allāh commanded him to prostrate to Ādam and he refused, while the Muslim was commanded to prostate and he did.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is one called who dies fighting to elevate the word of Allāh?
items.a: A shaheed.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention some types of shahādah.
items.a: One who dies of plague, stomach illness, drowning, collapsing buildings, and a woman who dies in childbirth.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Is everything forgiven for the shaheed for the sake of Allāh?
items.a: Yes, except debt.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the proof that a Muslim should not swear excessively?
items.a: Allāh, Exalted is He, says: “And preserve your oaths” [Al-Māʾidah: 89].
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: If one swears an oath then finds something else better than it, what should he do?
items.a: He expiates his oath and does what is better.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the expiation for an oath?
items.a: Feeding ten poor people, clothing them, or freeing a slave; if unable, fasting three days.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Who was the first to swear a false oath?
items.a: Iblees, may Allāh curse him. Allāh, Exalted is He, says: “And he [Shaiṭān (Satan)] swore by Allāh to them both (saying): ‘Verily, I am one of the sincere well-wishers for you both’” [Al-Aʿrāf: 21].
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the ghamoos oath?
items.a: A false oath by which one wrongfully takes a Muslim’s wealth.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the reward for that who fasts one day for the sake of Allāh?
items.a: Allāh distances him from the Fire by seventy years.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Which two eyes will not be touched by the Fire?
items.a: An eye that wept from fear of Allāh, and an eye that stayed awake guarding for the sake of Allāh.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: If a person gives a gift, may he take it back?
items.a: No.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the ruling on one who takes back his gift?
items.a: Like a dog that vomits then returns to its vomit.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention some times when supplication is answered.
items.a: The last third of the night, between the athān and iqāmah, the last hour [before sunset] of Friday, during rainfall, in prostration, when fasting, and when traveling.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: How is the reward of fasting an entire year written for a Muslim?
items.a: By fasting Ramaḍān and three days of each month.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What righteous deed increases provision and lengthens life?
items.a: Maintaining kinship ties.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Do you want the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, to guarantee al-Jannah for you?
items.a: Then guarantee what is between your jaws and what is between your thighs — meaning the tongue and the private parts.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is al-khullah (intimate, exclusive love)?
items.a: It is the highest level of love.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Whom did Allāh single out as His khalīl?
items.a: Ibrāhīm and Muḥammad, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhimā wa-sallam.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the ruling on listening to songs?
items.a: Forbidden.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Is it permissible to look at indecent images?
items.a: It is not permissible.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Can a child fully repay his parent?
items.a: No, except if he finds him enslaved, buys him, and frees him.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the ruling on one who curses his parents?
items.a: Forbidden; Allāh curses the one who curses his parents.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Three are doomed to humiliation; who are they?
items.a: One who reaches his parents when they are elderly , yet does not enter al-Jannah. One in whose presence the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, is mentioned and he does not send prayers upon him. One who reaches Ramaḍān and is not forgiven.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Does a child’s seeking forgiveness benefit his parent?
items.a: Yes; his rank is raised in al-Jannah.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Is it permissible to seek forgiveness for a parent or relative who is a mushrik?
items.a: Not permissible; but one treats them kindly if they are is alive.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: How does a man insult his parents?
items.a: He insults another man’s father and that man insults his father; and he insults his mother and that man insults his mother.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention some manners to observe with one’s father.
items.a: Not calling him by his name; not walking ahead of him; not sitting before him; not raising one’s voice over him; not reminding him of favors; not frowning at him.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: If relatives cut ties with you, what do you do?
items.a: I maintain ties with them even if they cut me off.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What did Allāh command regarding the neighbor?
items.a: Kindness to him, being good to him, and not harming him.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Which neighbor should you begin with when giving gifts?
items.a: The neighbor whose door is closest.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Which neighbors Allāh regards as the best?
items.a: The one who is best to his neighbor.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention four things of happiness and four things of misery.
items.a: From happiness: a spacious home, a righteous wife, a comfortable mount, and a righteous neighbor. From misery: a cramped home, an evil wife, a bad mount, and a wicked neighbor.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the reward for one who supports a widow or a poor person?
items.a: He’s like a mujāhid for the sake of Allāh, and like one who fasts by day and prays by night.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the virtue of one whose children die, and he regards them as a cause for reward?
items.a: Whoever has three children who die, and he seeks reward from that, will enter al-Jannah; the same applies if he has two.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What are the rights of the road?
items.a: Lowering the gaze, avoid causing harm, returning salām, enjoining good and forbidding evil.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What do you do if you see a thorn or a stone on the road?
items.a: Remove it from the road.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What reward results from removing harm from the road?
items.a: It’s a cause for entering al-Jannah, and it is of faith.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention evidence that forms of charity are many.
items.a: From Jābir, may Allāh be pleased with him, he said: The Messenger of Allāh, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, said: “Every act of goodness is a sadaqah (charity)”. Narrated by al-Bukhārī. Also, narrated by Muslim from Ḥuthayfah, may Allāh be pleased with him.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What do you do if you see a fault in your Muslim brother?
items.a: I correct him; for the believer is the mirror of his brother.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: To what did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, liken a good companion and a bad companion?
items.a: To the perfume bearer and the blacksmith’s bellows.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: How was the Prophet’s, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, laughter?
items.a: His laughter was a smile.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Should a Muslim laugh excessively?
items.a: No; for excessive laughter deadens the heart.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, joke?
items.a: Yes, and his joking was truthful.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Is the believer generous or stingy?
items.a: Generous; stinginess is a grave disease.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the punishment of one who curses people?
items.a: Those who curse will not be intercessors or witnesses on the Day of Resurrection.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the reward of visiting one’s Muslim brother?
items.a: It is said to him: Wholesome you are, and wholesome is your walk, and you are granted a lofty place in al-Jannah.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Is smiling and cheerfulness toward a Muslim among righteous deeds?
items.a: Yes.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the virtue of loving for the sake of Allāh?
items.a: Allāh loves him as he loved his companion for Allāh’s sake; and a person will be resurrected with whom he loved.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention a ḥadīth on manners with elders and youngsters.
items.a: From ʿAbdullāh bnu ʿAmr, may Allāh be pleased with them both, from the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, who said: “Whoever does not show mercy to our young and recognize the rights of our elders is not one of us”. Narrated by Aḥmad and al-Ḥākim; also by Aḥmad (6733) and at-Tirmithī (1920). Authenticated by al-Albānī in Ṣaḥīḥ al-Adab al-Mufrad (272/354), and by al-Wādīʿī in aṣ-Ṣaḥīḥ al-Musnad (783).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: If a gathering has elders and youngsters, who begins speaking?
items.a: The elder.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the reward for that who treats animals with kindness?
items.a: Allāh shows him mercy and admits him into al-Jannah.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Is killing an animal aimlessly permissible?
items.a: It is not permissible.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Is it permissible to take chicks from their nest?
items.a: Not permissible.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What do you do if you see a thirsty dog or animal?
items.a: I give it water.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the danger of lying?
items.a: Lying leads to wickedness, and wickedness leads to the Fire.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the reward of truthfulness?
items.a: Truthfulness leads to righteousness, and righteousness leads to al-Jannah.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention the virtue of honesty in buying and selling.
items.a: If the two parties are truthful, their sale is blessed; if they lie, its blessing is erased.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: If Allāh forbids something, is it permissible to sell it?
items.a: Not permissible.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What do you do if you see two people quarreling?
items.a: I reconcile them. Allāh, Exalted is He, says: “The believers are but brothers, so make peace between your brothers.” [Al-Ḥujurāt: 10]
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the ruling on insulting a Muslim?
items.a: It is fisq (disobedience), not permissible.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: In which sūrah does Allāh, Exalted is He, say: “O you who believe! Let not a group scoff at another group, it may be that the latter are better than the former. Nor let (some) women scoff at other women, it may be that the latter are better than the former. Nor defame one another, nor insult one another by nicknames. How bad is it to insult one’s brother after having Faith. And whosoever does not repent, then such are indeed the wrong-doers”?
items.a: Sūrat al-Ḥujurāt.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the gravity of oppression?
items.a: Oppression will be darkness on the Day of Resurrection, and the supplication of the oppressed has no barrier between it and Allāh.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Who is the truly bankrupt (al-muflis) person?
items.a: He is the one who comes on the Day of Resurrection with many good deeds, but owes rights to the creatures — from harm, insult, beating, and the like. They take from his good deeds until they are exhausted, then their sins are added to him, and he is thrown into the Fire.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What should one do if he passes through the mosque or the market while carrying a weapon?
items.a: He should hold tightly to its edge (or tip) and not harm anyone with it.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the reward of one who relieves a difficulty for a Muslim, or conceals a fault of theirs?
items.a: Whoever conceals a Muslim’s fault, Allāh will conceal his faults; and whoever relieves him of a worldly difficulty, Allāh will relieve him of a difficulty on the Day of Resurrection.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What are the rights of a Muslim over another Muslim?
items.a: Greeting him; to respond with a blessing when he sneezes; advising him; accepting his invitation; visiting him when ill; following his funeral.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What should one do if invited to a feast while fasting?
items.a: Supplicate for the host; if breaking the fast is more beneficial, then break it.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the virtue of gathering in Allāh’s houses to read and study the Qurʾān?
items.a: Tranquility descends upon them; mercy envelops them; angels surround them; Allāh mentions them among those with Him.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: If a Muslim was consistent in righteous deeds then fell ill, what is written for him?
items.a: What he used to do while healthy is written for him.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: How much may a person give in charity from his wealth before death?
items.a: Maximum of one third.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Is a bequest to an heir permissible?
items.a: Not permissible.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What are four qualities that, if they are combined in a person in a single day, he enters al-Jannah?
items.a: Fasting, visiting the sick, following a funeral, and giving charity.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the reward for one afflicted with fever and is patient?
items.a: Fever removes sins like a furnace removes impurities from iron.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the virtue of visiting the sick?
items.a: He remains in the gardens of al-Jannah until he returns.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What do you do when visiting the sick?
items.a: Sit by his head and say seven times: “Asʾalu Allāha al-‘Aẓīm, Rabba al-ʿArshi al-ʿAẓīm, an yashfiyaka” (I ask Allāh the Mighty, Lord of the Mighty Throne, to cure you), and say: “Don’t worry, it is purifying, if Allāh wills”.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is haughtiness?
items.a: Rejecting the truth and belittling people.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: How will the haughty be resurrected on the Day of Resurrection?
items.a: Like tiny ants in human form, driven to Hell.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the benefit of giving gifts?
items.a: It brings love. The Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, said: “Exchange gifts, and you will love one another”.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention two causes that bring love among Muslims.
items.a: Spreading salām; giving gifts.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: If you love someone for the sake of Allāh, do you tell him?
items.a: Yes.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the benefit of raising the hands in duʿāʾ?
items.a: It is one of the means by which duʿāʾ is answered, for Allāh is shy to return them empty.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention a comprehensive supplication the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, used often.
items.a: “Allāhumma ātinā fī ad-dunyā ḥasanah, wa fī al-ākhirati ḥasanah, wa qinā ʿathāba an-nār” (Our Lord, give us good in this world and good in the Hereafter and protect us from the punishment of the Fire).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention a supplication for steadfastness.
items.a: “Yā muqalliba al-quloobi thabbit qalbī ʿalā deenik” (O Turner of hearts, keep my heart firm upon Your religion).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention some supplications for distress.
items.a: “Lā ilāha illā Allāhu al-ʿAlīmu al-Ḥalīm. Lā ilāha illā Allāhu Rabbu al-ʿArshi al-ʿAẓīm. Lā ilāha illā Allāhu Rabbu as-samāwāti wa Rabbu al-arḍ, Rabbu al-ʿArshi al-Karīm” (There is no god except Allāh, the All-Knowing, the Forbearing. There is no god except Allāh, Lord of the Mighty Throne. There is no god except Allāh, Lord of the heavens and Lord of the earth, Lord of the Noble Throne). * “Allāhu Rabbī lā ushriku bihi shayʾan” (Allāh is my Lord; I associate nothing with Him).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What should one do if his name is ugly? Give examples.
items.a: Change it to a good name; the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, changed ‘ʿAaṣiyah’ (Hard/Disobedient) to ‘Jameelah’ (Beautiful), and ‘Aṣram’ (Cut Off) to ‘Zurʿah’ (Seed/Sowing Ground).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the ruling on poetry?
items.a: Its good is good; its bad is bad.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the reward for guiding a lost person to the right path?
items.a: Like freeing a slave.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What do you say when amazed?
items.a: “Ṣubḥāna Allāh”.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: When do children and slaves seek permission?
items.a: At noon rest, after ʿIshāʾ, and before Fajr.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the punishment for eavesdropping when people dislike it?
items.a: Molten lead will be poured into his ears on the Day of Resurrection.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention five etiquettes a Muslim observes at night.
items.a: Closing doors; tying water skins; covering containers; extinguishing fire; keeping children indoors at sunset.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Why did Allāh command extinguishing fire before sleep?
items.a: Because fire is our enemy, and Shayṭān guides the mouse to burn the house.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Why did Allāh command us to keep children inside at sunset?
items.a: Because that is when the devils spread.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Complete the ḥadīth: “If Allāh intends to take a slave from a land…”.
items.a: “…He makes him in need of it”. From the ḥadīth of Abī ʿAzzah Yasār, narrated by at-Tirmithī.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Complete the ḥadīth: “Whomsoever Allāh intends good for…”.
items.a: “…He grants him fiqh (understanding) of religion”. From the ḥadīth of Muʿawiyah, Agreed upon. [Another ḥadīth is: ]“…He afflicts him with trials”, from Abū Hurayrah in al-Bukhārī.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Should you leave the house without your parents’ permission?
items.a: No, that is not appropriate.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What does Allāh look at in his slaves?
items.a: The heart and the deeds.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: To what extent does Allāh multiply a good deed?
items.a: Up to tenfold, up to seven hundredfold, and to many times over.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What did Allāh promise the people of towns if they believed and feared Allāh?
items.a: The opening of blessings from the heavens and the earth.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: If a person commits sins and wants to repent, does Allāh accept it?
items.a: Yes. Allāh, Exalted is He, says: “Indeed, Allāh forgives all sins” [Az-Zumar: 53].
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: In which sūrah is the āyah: “And perform prayer, at the two ends of the day and in some hours of the night. Verily, the good deeds remove the evil deeds. That is a reminder for the mindful”?
items.a: In sūrat Hūd.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: In which sūrah is the āyah: “Say: ‘O My slaves who have transgressed against themselves! Despair not of the Mercy of Allāh: verily, Allāh forgives all sins. Truly He is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful’”?
items.a: In sūrat Az-Zumar.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Does Allāh rejoice in the repentance of [his] slaves?
items.a: Yes.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: How should a believer act when afflicted by ease or hardship?
items.a: He should be thankful in ease and patient in hardship.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What did the Prophet, ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa-sallam, say when his people struck him until he bled?
items.a: He said: “O Allāh, forgive my people, for they do not know”.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: Mention an evidence that a Muslim adheres to all commands of Islām in every aspect.
items.a: Allāh, Exalted is He, says: “O you who believe! Enter perfectly in Islām” [Al-Baqarah: 208].
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is the condition of a gathering in which Allāh is not remembered?
items.a: It will be a source of regret for its participants on the Day of Resurrection, and when they leave it, they will leave it as one would leave a donkey’s carcass.
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

items.q: What is Kaffārat al-Majlis (expiation for a gathering)?
items.a: Saying: “Ṣubḥānaka Allāhumma wa bi-ḥamdika, ash-hadu an lā ilāha illā Anta, astaghfiruka wa atūbu ilayk” (Glory is to You, O Allāh, and praise is Yours. I bear witness that there is no god except You. I seek Your forgiveness and turn to You in repentance).
items.section: Chapter: The Deeds of a Muslim by Day and Night

about.title: Kanz al-Atfal
body: This app presents Kanz al-Atfal, a comprehensive educational book for children and beginners in the creed of Ahl al-Sunnah wal-Jamāʿah.
It contains 1536 questions and answers across 22 chapters, covering Tawḥīd, Faith, Fiqh, Biography, and Islamic etiquettes.
Browse chapters, search any question or answer, save favourites, and test yourself with four quiz modes. The app also tracks your weak spots automatically.
The app works offline after the first visit — install it to your home screen like a regular app, with no internet required.
